text.NRSV: When in the future your child asks you, ‘What does this mean?’ you shall answer, ‘By strength of hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt, from the house of slavery.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be when thy son shall ask thee in time to come, saying, What is this? Then shalt thou say unto him: With strength of hand, did Yahweh bring us forth out of Egypt out of the house of slaves.
text.DRB: And when thy son shall ask thee tomorrow, saying: What is this? thou shalt answer him: With a strong hand did the Lord bring us forth out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the Lord killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from human firstborn to the firstborn of animals. Therefore I sacrifice to the Lord every male that first opens the womb, but every firstborn of my sons I redeem.’
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when Pharaoh had shown himself too hardened to let us go, then did Yahweh slay every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of men even unto the firstborn of beasts. For this cause, am, I, sacrificing to Yahweh whatsoever is born first, of the males, and every firstborn of my sons, must I redeem.
text.DRB: For when Pharao was hardened, and would not let us go, the Lord slew every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of man to the firstborn of beasts: therefore I sacrifice to the Lord all that openeth the womb of the male sex, and all the firstborn of my sons I redeem.

text.NRSV: It shall serve as a sign on your hand and as an emblem on your forehead that by strength of hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be for a sign upon thy hand, and for frontlets between thine eyes,—that, with strength of hand, did Yahweh bring us forth out of Egypt.
text.DRB: And it shall be as a sign in thy hand, and as a thing hung between thy eyes, for a remembrance: because the Lord hath brought us forth out of Egypt by a strong hand.

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God thought, “If the people face war, they may change their minds and return to Egypt.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when Pharaoh had let the people go, that God did not lead by the land-way of the Philistines, although it was near,—for God said—Lest the people be dismayed when they see war and turn back to Egypt.
text.DRB: And when Pharao had sent out the people, the Lord led them not by the way of the land of the Philistines which is near: thinking lest perhaps they would repent, if they should see wars arise against them, and would return into Egypt.

text.NRSV: But God led the people around by the way of the wilderness toward the Red Sea. The Israelites went up out of the land of Egypt prepared for battle.
text.Rotherham: So God took the people round the desert-way of the Red Sea,—and the sons of Israel went up armed, out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: But he led them about by the way of the desert, which is by the Red Sea: and the children of Israel went up armed out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: And Moses took with him the bones of Joseph who had required a solemn oath of the Israelites, saying, “God will surely take notice of you, and then you must carry my bones with you from here.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses took the bones of Joseph, with him,—for he had, taken an oath, of the sons of Israel saying, God will, surely visit, you, so then ye shall carry up my bones from hence with you.
text.DRB: And Moses took Joseph's bones with him: because he had adjured the children of Israel, saying: God shall visit you, carry out my bones from hence with you.

text.NRSV: They set out from Succoth, and camped at Etham, on the edge of the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Succoth,—and encamped in Etham, at the edge of the desert.
text.DRB: And marching from Socoth they encamped in Etham in the utmost coasts of the wilderness.

text.NRSV: The Lord went in front of them in a pillar of cloud by day, to lead them along the way, and in a pillar of fire by night, to give them light, so that they might travel by day and by night.
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, was going before them—by day, in a pillar of cloud to lead them the way, and by night, in a pillar of fire to give them light,—that they might journey day and night:
text.DRB: And the Lord went before them to shew the way by day in a pillar of a cloud, and by night in a pillar of fire: that he might be the guide of their journey at both times.

text.NRSV: Neither the pillar of cloud by day nor the pillar of fire by night left its place in front of the people.
text.Rotherham: the pillar of cloud by day ceased not, nor the pillar of fire by night,—from before the people.
text.DRB: There never failed the pillar of the cloud by day, nor the pillar of fire by night, before the people.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Tell the Israelites to turn back and camp in front of Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, in front of Baal-zephon; you shall camp opposite it, by the sea.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, that they turn back and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea,—before Baal-zephon, over against it, shall ye encamp, by the sea.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel: Let them turn and encamp over against Phihahiroth which is between Magdal and the sea over against Beelsephon: you shall encamp before it upon the sea.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh will say of the Israelites, ‘They are wandering aimlessly in the land; the wilderness has closed in on them.’
text.Rotherham: Then will Pharaoh say, of the sons of Israel, Entangled, they are in the land,—the desert hath closed in upon them.
text.DRB: And Pharao will say of the children of Israel: They are straitened in the land, the desert hath shut them in.

text.NRSV: I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and he will pursue them, so that I will gain glory for myself over Pharaoh and all his army; and the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord. And they did so.
text.Rotherham: So will I let the heart of Pharaoh wax bold, and he will pursue them, that I may get me honour over Pharaoh, and over all his forces, and the Egyptians shall know, that I, am Yahweh. And they did so.
text.DRB: And I shall harden his heart, and he will pursue you: and I shall be glorified in Pharao, and in all his army: and the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord. And they did so.

text.NRSV: When the king of Egypt was told that the people had fled, the minds of Pharaoh and his officials were changed toward the people, and they said, “What have we done, letting Israel leave our service?”
text.Rotherham: And it was told the king of Egypt, that the people had fled,—and the heart of Pharaoh and his servants was turned against the people, and they said—What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?
text.DRB: And it was told the king of the Egyptians that the people was fled: and the heart of Pharao and of his servants was changed with regard to the people, and they said: What meant we to do, that we let Israel go from serving us?

text.NRSV: So he had his chariot made ready, and took his army with him;
text.Rotherham: So he yoked his chariot,—and his people, took he with him.
text.DRB: So he made ready his chariot, and took all his people with him.

text.NRSV: he took six hundred picked chariots and all the other chariots of Egypt with officers over all of them.
text.Rotherham: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt,—with warriors in every one.
text.DRB: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots that were in Egypt: and the captains of the whole army.

text.NRSV: The Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt and he pursued the Israelites, who were going out boldly.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh let the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt wax bold, and he pursued the sons of Israel,—when the sons of Israel, were going out with a high hand.
text.DRB: And the Lord hardened the heart of Pharao king of Egypt, and he pursued the children of Israel: but they were gone forth in a mighty hand.

text.NRSV: The Egyptians pursued them, all Pharaoh’s horses and chariots, his chariot drivers and his army; they overtook them camped by the sea, by Pi-hahiroth, in front of Baal-zephon.
text.Rotherham: So the Egyptians pursued them and overtook them, encamping by the sea—all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his forces,—by Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon.
text.DRB: And when the Egyptians followed the steps of them who were gone before, they found them encamped at the sea side: all Pharao's horse and chariots, and the whole army were in Phihahiroth before Beelsephon.

text.NRSV: As Pharaoh drew near, the Israelites looked back, and there were the Egyptians advancing on them. In great fear the Israelites cried out to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Now when, Pharaoh, had drawn near, the sons of Israel lifted up their eyes, and lo! the Egyptians, moving along after them and they feared greatly, and the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh.
text.DRB: And when Pharao drew near, the children of Israel, lifting up their eyes, saw the Egyptians behind them: and they feared exceedingly, and cried to the Lord.

text.NRSV: They said to Moses, “Was it because there were no graves in Egypt that you have taken us away to die in the wilderness? What have you done to us, bringing us out of Egypt?
text.Rotherham: Then said they unto Moses, Was it because there were no graves in Egypt, that thou didst fetch us to die in the desert? What is this thou hast done to us, bringing us forth out of Egypt?
text.DRB: And they said to Moses: Perhaps there were no graves in Egypt, therefore thou hast brought us to die in the wilderness: why wouldst thou do this, to lead us out of Egypt?

text.NRSV: Is this not the very thing we told you in Egypt, ‘Let us alone and let us serve the Egyptians’? For it would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.”
text.Rotherham: Is not this the word which we spake unto thee in Egypt, saying, Let us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians,—for it is better for us, to serve the Egyptians, than to die in the desert?
text.DRB: Is not this the word that we spoke to thee in Egypt, saying: Depart from us that we may serve the Egyptians? for it was much better to serve them, than to die in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the people, “Do not be afraid, stand firm, and see the deliverance that the Lord will accomplish for you today; for the Egyptians whom you see today you shall never see again.
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto the people—Do not fear, stand still and see the salvation of Yahweh, which he will work for you to-day,—for as for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to-day, ye shall not again see them any more to times age-abiding.
text.DRB: And Moses said to the people: Fear not: stand and see the great wonders of the Lord, which he will do this day: for the Egyptians, whom you see now, you shall see no more for ever.

text.NRSV: The Lord will fight for you, and you have only to keep still.”
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will fight for you,—and, ye, shall forbear.
text.DRB: The Lord will fight for you, and you shall hold your peace.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Why do you cry out to me? Tell the Israelites to go forward.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, What outcry wouldst thou make unto me? Speak unto the sons of Israel, that they go forward;
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Why criest thou to me? Speak to the children of Israel to go forward.

text.NRSV: But you lift up your staff, and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it, that the Israelites may go into the sea on dry ground.
text.Rotherham: and, thou, lift high thy staff and stretch forth thy hand over the sea and cleave it asunder,—that the sons of Israel may enter into the midst of the sea on dry ground;
text.DRB: But lift thou up thy rod, and stretch forth thy hand over the sea, and divide it: that the children of Israel may go through the midst of the sea on dry ground.

text.NRSV: Then I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians so that they will go in after them; and so I will gain glory for myself over Pharaoh and all his army, his chariots, and his chariot drivers.
text.Rotherham: and, I, behold me letting the heart of the Egyptians wax bold, that they may enter after them,—that I may get me honour over Pharaoh and over his forces, over his chariots, and over his horsemen,
text.DRB: And I will harden the heart of the Egyptians to pursue you: and I will be glorified in Pharao, and in all his host, and in his chariots, and in his horsemen.

text.NRSV: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I have gained glory for myself over Pharaoh, his chariots, and his chariot drivers.”
text.Rotherham: And the Egyptians shall know, that, I, am Yahweh,—when I have gotten me honour over Pharaoh, over his chariots and over his horsemen.
text.DRB: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall be glorified in Pharao, and in his chariots and in his horsemen.

text.NRSV: The angel of God who was going before the Israelite army moved and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud moved from in front of them and took its place behind them.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of God who was going on before the camp of Israel removed, and came on behind them,—and the pillar of cloud removed from before them, and stood behind them;
text.DRB: And the angel of God, who went before the camp of Israel, removing, went behind them: and together with him the pillar of the cloud, leaving the forepart,

text.NRSV: It came between the army of Egypt and the army of Israel. And so the cloud was there with the darkness, and it lit up the night; one did not come near the other all night.
text.Rotherham: so it came in between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel, so it became a cloud and darkness, and yet lighted up the night,—so that the one came not near unto the other, all the night.
text.DRB: Stood behind, between the Egyptians' camp and the camp of Israel: and it was a dark cloud, and enlightening the night , so that they could not come at one another all the night.

text.NRSV: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea. The Lord drove the sea back by a strong east wind all night, and turned the sea into dry land; and the waters were divided.
text.Rotherham: And Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and Yahweh carried away the sea by a mighty east wind all the night, and turned the sea into dry land,—and the waters were cloven asunder.
text.DRB: And when Moses had stretched forth his hand over the sea, the Lord took it away by a strong and burning wind blowing all the night, and turned it into dry ground: and the water was divided.

text.NRSV: The Israelites went into the sea on dry ground, the waters forming a wall for them on their right and on their left.
text.Rotherham: Then went the sons of Israel into the midst of the sea on the dry ground,—and, the waters, to them, were a wall, on their right hand and on their left.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel went in through the midst of the sea dried up: for the water was as a wall on their right hand and on their left.

text.NRSV: The Egyptians pursued, and went into the sea after them, all of Pharaoh’s horses, chariots, and chariot drivers.
text.Rotherham: And the Egyptians pursued, and entered after them—all the horses of Pharaoh his chariots and his horsemen,—into the midst of the sea.
text.DRB: And the Egyptians pursuing went in after them, and all Pharao's horses, his chariots and horsemen through the midst of the sea,

text.NRSV: At the morning watch the Lord in the pillar of fire and cloud looked down upon the Egyptian army, and threw the Egyptian army into panic.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the morning watch, that Yahweh looked down into the camp of the Egyptians, through the pillar of fire and cloud, and confused the camp of the Egyptians;
text.DRB: And now the morning watch was come, and behold the Lord looking upon the Egyptian army through the pillar of fire and of the cloud, slew their host.

text.NRSV: He clogged their chariot wheels so that they turned with difficulty. The Egyptians said, “Let us flee from the Israelites, for the Lord is fighting for them against Egypt.”
text.Rotherham: and he took off the wheels of their chariots, and made them drive heavily,—so that the Egyptians said—We must flee from the face of Israel, for Yahweh, is fighting for them against the Egyptians.
text.DRB: And overthrew the wheels of the chariots, and they were carried into the deep. And the Egyptians said: Let us flee from Israel: for the Lord fighteth for them against us.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the sea, so that the water may come back upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots and chariot drivers.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Stretch forth thy hand over the sea,—that the waters may return upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Stretch forth thy hand over the sea, that the waters may come again upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots and horsemen.

text.NRSV: So Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and at dawn the sea returned to its normal depth. As the Egyptians fled before it, the Lord tossed the Egyptians into the sea.
text.Rotherham: So Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea and the sea returned, towards morning, to its steady flow, when the Egyptians, were fleeing to meet it,—thus Yahweh shook off the Egyptians into the midst of the sea;
text.DRB: And when Moses had stretched forth his hand towards the sea, it returned at the first break of day to the former place: and as the Egyptians were fleeing away, the waters came upon them, and the Lord shut them up in the middle of the waves.

text.NRSV: The waters returned and covered the chariots and the chariot drivers, the entire army of Pharaoh that had followed them into the sea; not one of them remained.
text.Rotherham: thus the waters returned and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, even all the forces of Pharaoh that were entering after them into the sea,—there was not left remaining among them so much as one.
text.DRB: And the waters returned, and covered the chariots and the horsemen of all the army of Pharao, who had come into the sea after them, neither did there so much as one of them remain.

text.NRSV: But the Israelites walked on dry ground through the sea, the waters forming a wall for them on their right and on their left.
text.Rotherham: But the sons of Israel, had gone their way on dry ground in the saidst of the sea,—and the waters had been to them a wall, on their right hand and on their left.
text.DRB: But the children of Israel marched through the midst of the sea upon dry land, and the waters were to them as a wall on the right hand and on the left:

text.NRSV: Thus the Lord saved Israel that day from the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the seashore.
text.Rotherham: Thus did Yahweh, on that day, save Israel, out of the hand of the Egyptians,—and Israel saw the Egyptians, dead on the shore of the sea.
text.DRB: And the Lord delivered Israel on that day out of the hands of the Egyptians.

text.NRSV: Israel saw the great work that the Lord did against the Egyptians. So the people feared the Lord and believed in the Lord and in his servant Moses.
text.Rotherham: And when Israel saw the mighty hand wherewith Yahweh had wrought against the Egyptians, then did the people revere Yahweh,—and they put their trust in Yahweh, and in Moses, his servant.
text.DRB: And they saw the Egyptians dead upon the sea shore, and the mighty hand that the Lord had used against them: and the people feared the Lord, and they believed the Lord, and Moses his servant.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and the Israelites sang this song to the Lord: “I will sing to the Lord, for he has triumphed gloriously; horse and rider he has thrown into the sea.
text.Rotherham: Then, sang Moses, and the sons of Israel, this song unto Yahweh, and they spake, saying,—I will sing to Yahweh, for he is exalted, exalted,—The horse and his rider, hath he cast into the sea.
text.DRB: Then Moses and the children of Israel sung this canticle to the Lord: and said: Let us sing to the Lord: for he is gloriously magnified, the horse and the rider he hath thrown into the sea.

text.NRSV: The Lord is my strength and my might, and he has become my salvation; this is my God, and I will praise him, my father’s God, and I will exalt him.
text.Rotherham: My might and melody, is Yah, And he became mine, by salvation,—This, is my GOD, and I will glorify him, The God of my father and I will set him on high.
text.DRB: The Lord is my strength and my praise, and he is become salvation to me: he is my God and I will glorify him: the God of my father, and I will exalt him.

text.NRSV: The Lord is a warrior; the Lord is his name.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh is a warlike one,—Yahweh, is his name.
text.DRB: The Lord is as a man of war, Almighty is his name.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh’s chariots and his army he cast into the sea; his picked officers were sunk in the Red Sea.
text.Rotherham: The chariots of Pharaoh and his train, hath he cast into the sea,—Yea, the choice of his warriors, were sunk in the Sedgy Sea.
text.DRB: Pharao's chariots and his army he hath cast into the sea: his chosen captains are drowned in the Red Sea.

text.NRSV: The floods covered them; they went down into the depths like a stone.
text.Rotherham: Roaring deeps, covered them,—They went down in the raging depths like a stone.
text.DRB: The depths have covered them, they are sunk to the bottom like a stone.

text.NRSV: Your right hand, O Lord, glorious in power— your right hand, O Lord, shattered the enemy.
text.Rotherham: Thy right hand, O Yahweh is splendid in power,—Thy right hand, O Yahweh dasheth in pieces a foe;
text.DRB: Thy right hand, O Lord, is magnified in strength: thy right hand, O Lord, hath slain the enemy.

text.NRSV: In the greatness of your majesty you overthrew your adversaries; you sent out your fury, it consumed them like stubble.
text.Rotherham: And in the greatness of thine exaltation, dost thou tear down thine opposers,—Thou dost send forth thy wrath, it consumeth them as straw;
text.DRB: And in the multitude of thy glory thou hast put down thy adversaries: thou hast sent thy wrath, which hath devoured them like stubble.

text.NRSV: At the blast of your nostrils the waters piled up, the floods stood up in a heap; the deeps congealed in the heart of the sea.
text.Rotherham: And with the blast of thy nostrils, heaped up are the waters, Upreared like a mound, are the flowing waves,—Roaring deeps are congealed in the heart of the sea.
text.DRB: And with the blast of thy anger the waters were gathered together: the flowing water stood, the depths were gathered together in the midst of the sea.

text.NRSV: The enemy said, ‘I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil, my desire shall have its fill of them. I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.’
text.Rotherham: Said the foe—I will pursue—overtake divide spoil,—Take her fill of them—shall my soul, I will bare my sword, root them out—shall my hand.
text.DRB: The enemy said: I will pursue and overtake, I will divide the spoils, my soul shall have its fill: I will draw my sword, my hand shall slay them.

text.NRSV: You blew with your wind, the sea covered them; they sank like lead in the mighty waters.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst blow with thy wind they were covered by the sea,—They rolled like lead, into the waters so wide.
text.DRB: Thy wind blew and the sea covered them: they sunk as lead in the mighty waters.

text.NRSV: Who is like you, O Lord, among the gods? Who is like you, majestic in holiness, awesome in splendor, doing wonders?
text.Rotherham: Who is, like unto thee, among the mighty O Yahweh? Who is, like unto thee, Majestic in holiness,—To be revered in praises, Doing a marvellous thing?
text.DRB: Who is like to thee, among the strong, O Lord? who is like to thee, glorious in holiness, terrible and praiseworthy, doing wonders?

text.NRSV: You stretched out your right hand, the earth swallowed them.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst stretch forth thy right hand, earth swallowed them up.
text.DRB: Thou stretchedst forth thy hand, and the earth swallowed them.

text.NRSV: In your steadfast love you led the people whom you redeemed; you guided them by your strength to your holy abode.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast led forth in thy lovingkindness, the people which thou hast redeemed,—Thou hast guided them in thy might into the home of thy holiness.
text.DRB: In thy mercy thou hast been a leader to the people which thou hast redeemed: and in thy strength thou hast carried them to thy holy habitation.

text.NRSV: The peoples heard, they trembled; pangs seized the inhabitants of Philistia.
text.Rotherham: Peoples have heard they tremble,—A pang, hath seized the dwellers of Philistia:
text.DRB: Nations rose up, and were angry: sorrows took hold on the inhabitants of Philisthiim.

text.NRSV: Then the chiefs of Edom were dismayed; trembling seized the leaders of Moab; all the inhabitants of Canaan melted away.
text.Rotherham: Then, were amazed the chiefs of Edom, The mighty ones of Moab, there seizeth them, a trembling,—Melted away, have all the dwellers of Canaan:
text.DRB: Then were the princes of Edom troubled, trembling seized on the stout men of Moab: all the inhabitants of Chanaan became stiff.

text.NRSV: Terror and dread fell upon them; by the might of your arm, they became still as a stone until your people, O Lord, passed by, until the people whom you acquired passed by.
text.Rotherham: There falleth upon them a terror and dread, With the greatness of thine arm, are they struck dumb as a stone,—Till thy people pass over O Yahweh, Till the people pass over, which thou hast made thine own:—
text.DRB: Let fear and dread fall upon them, in the greatness of thy arm: let them become unmoveable as a stone, until thy people, O Lord, pass by: until this thy people pass by, which thou hast possessed.

text.NRSV: You brought them in and planted them on the mountain of your own possession, the place, O Lord, that you made your abode, the sanctuary, O Lord, that your hands have established.
text.Rotherham: Thou bringest them in and plantest them, In the mountain of thine inheritance, Thy dwelling-place to abide in which thou hast made, O Yahweh,—A hallowed place O My Lord which thy hands have established.
text.DRB: Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thy inheritance, in thy most firm habitation which thou hast made, O Lord; thy sanctuary, O Lord, which thy hands have established.

text.NRSV: The Lord will reign forever and ever.”
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, shall reign unto times age-abiding and beyond.
text.DRB: The Lord shall reign for ever and ever.

text.NRSV: When the horses of Pharaoh with his chariots and his chariot drivers went into the sea, the Lord brought back the waters of the sea upon them; but the Israelites walked through the sea on dry ground.
text.Rotherham: For the horses of Pharaoh, with his chariots and his horsemen, entered into the sea, Then did Yahweh bring back upon them the waters of the sea,—But the sons of Israel, had gone their way on the dry ground through the midst of the sea.
text.DRB: For Pharao went in on horseback with his chariots and horsemen into the sea: and the Lord brought back upon them the waters of the sea: but the children of Israel walked on dry ground in the midst thereof.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Miriam, Aaron’s sister, took a tambourine in her hand; and all the women went out after her with tambourines and with dancing.
text.Rotherham: Then took Miriam the prophetess, sister of Aaron the timbrel in her hand,—and all the women came forth after her with timbrels and dances,
text.DRB: So Mary the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand: and all the women went forth after her with timbrels and with dances:

text.NRSV: And Miriam sang to them: “Sing to the Lord, for he has triumphed gloriously; horse and rider he has thrown into the sea.”
text.Rotherham: And Miriam responded to the men, Sing to Yahweh for he is exalted—exalted, The horse and his rider, hath he cast into the sea.
text.DRB: And she began the song to them, saying: Let us sing to the Lord, for he is gloriously magnified, the horse and his rider he hath thrown into the sea.

text.NRSV: Then Moses ordered Israel to set out from the Red Sea, and they went into the wilderness of Shur. They went three days in the wilderness and found no water.
text.Rotherham: And Moses caused Israel to break up from the Red Sea, and they went out into the desert of Shur,—and journeyed three days in the desert and found no water;
text.DRB: And Moses brought Israel from the Red Sea, and they went forth into the wilderness of Sur: and they marched three days through the wilderness, and found no water.

text.NRSV: When they came to Marah, they could not drink the water of Marah because it was bitter. That is why it was called Marah.
text.Rotherham: and, when they came in towards Marah they could not drink the waters of Marah, for they were bitter,—for this cause, was the name thereof called Marah.
text.DRB: And they came into Mara, and they could not drink the waters of Mara, because they were bitter: whereupon he gave a name also agreeable to the place, calling it Mara, that is, bitterness.

text.NRSV: And the people complained against Moses, saying, “What shall we drink?”
text.Rotherham: So the people murmured against Moses—saying, What are we to drink?
text.DRB: And the people murmured against Moses, saying: What shall we drink?

text.NRSV: He cried out to the Lord; and the Lord showed him a piece of wood; he threw it into the water, and the water became sweet. There the Lord made for them a statute and an ordinance and there he put them to the test.
text.Rotherham: And he made outcry unto Yahweh, and Yahweh pointed out a tree, and he cast it into the waters, and the waters became sweet,—There, he appointed him a statute and regulation And, there, he put him to the proof.
text.DRB: But he cried to the Lord, and he shewed him a tree, which when he had cast into the waters, they were turned into sweetness. There he appointed him ordinances, and judgments, and there he proved him,

text.NRSV: He said, “If you will listen carefully to the voice of the Lord your God, and do what is right in his sight, and give heed to his commandments and keep all his statutes, I will not bring upon you any of the diseases that I brought upon the Egyptians; for I am the Lord who heals you.”
text.Rotherham: And he said: If thou, wilt indeed hearken, to the voice of Yahweh thy God, And the thing that is right in his eyes, thou wilt do, And so give ear to his commandments, And keep all his statutes, None of the sicknesses which I laid on the Egyptians, will I lay upon thee, For, I, am Yahweh, thy physician.
text.DRB: Saying: If thou wilt hear the voice of the Lord thy God, and do what is right before him, and obey his commandments, and keep all his precepts, none of the evils that I laid upon Egypt, will I bring upon thee: for I am the Lord thy healer.

text.NRSV: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees; and they camped there by the water.
text.Rotherham: Then came they in to Elim, and there, were twelve fountains of water and seventy palm-trees,—so they encamped there, by the waters.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel came into Elim, where there were twelve fountains of water, and seventy palm trees: and they encamped by the waters.

text.NRSV: The whole congregation of the Israelites set out from Elim; and Israel came to the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after they had departed from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then moved they on from Elim, and all the assembly of the sons of Israel entered into the desert of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month, by their coming forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And they set forward from Elim, and all the multitude of the children of Israel came into the desert of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai: the fifteenth day of the second month, after they came out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: The whole congregation of the Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Then did all the assembly of the sons of Israel murmur against Moses and against Aaron in the desert;
text.DRB: And all the congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: The Israelites said to them, “If only we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the fleshpots and ate our fill of bread; for you have brought us out into this wilderness to kill this whole assembly with hunger.”
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Israel said unto them—Oh that we had died by the hand of Yahweh, in the land of Egypt, when we remained by the pot of flesh, when we did eat bread to the full,—for ye have brought us forth into this desert, to cause all this multitude to die by famine.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel said to them: Would to God we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat over the flesh pots, and ate bread to the full. Why have you brought us into this desert, that you might destroy all the multitude with famine?

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “I am going to rain bread from heaven for you, and each day the people shall go out and gather enough for that day. In that way I will test them, whether they will follow my instruction or not.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Behold me! raining down for you bread out of heaven,—and the people shall go out and gather the portion for a day, on its day, that I may prove them whether they will walk in my law, or not.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Behold I will rain bread from heaven for you: let the people go forth, and gather what is sufficient for every day: that I may prove them whether they will walk in my law, or not.

text.NRSV: On the sixth day, when they prepare what they bring in, it will be twice as much as they gather on other days.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass on the sixth day, that they shall settle what they shall bring in,—and it shall be twice as much as they gather day by day.
text.DRB: But the sixth day let them provide for to bring in: and let it be double to that they were wont to gather every day.

text.NRSV: So Moses and Aaron said to all the Israelites, “In the evening you shall know that it was the Lord who brought you out of the land of Egypt,
text.Rotherham: So Moses and Aaron said unto all the sons of Israel,—At eventide, then shall ye know that it was, Yahweh, who brought you forth, out of the land of Egypt;
text.DRB: And Moses and Aaron said to the children of Israel: In the evening you shall know that the Lord hath brought you forth out of the land of Egypt:

text.NRSV: and in the morning you shall see the glory of the Lord, because he has heard your complaining against the Lord. For what are we, that you complain against us?”
text.Rotherham: and, in the morning, then shall ye see the glory of Yahweh, in that he hath heard your murmurings against Yahweh,—what then are, we, that ye should murmur against, us?
text.DRB: And in the morning you shall see the glory of the Lord: for he hath heard your murmuring against the Lord: but as for us, what are we, that you mutter against us?

text.NRSV: Moses said, “When the Lord gives you meat to eat in the evening and your fill of bread in the morning, because the Lord has heard the complaining that you utter against him—what are we? Your complaining is not against us but against the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—In that Yahweh giveth you in the evening flesh to eat, and bread in the morning, to the full, in that Yahweh heareth your murmurings wherewith ye are murmuring against him, what then are, we? Not against us, do ye murmur but against Yahweh.
text.DRB: And Moses said: In the evening the Lord will give you flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full: for he hath heard your murmurings, with which you have murmured against him, for what are we? your murmuring is not against us, but against the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to Aaron, “Say to the whole congregation of the Israelites, ‘Draw near to the Lord, for he has heard your complaining.’”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Aaron, Say unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, Draw near, before Yahweh,—for he hath heard your murmurings.
text.DRB: Moses also said to Aaron: Say to the whole congregation of the children of Israel: Come before the Lord: for he hath heard your murmuring.

text.NRSV: And as Aaron spoke to the whole congregation of the Israelites, they looked toward the wilderness, and the glory of the Lord appeared in the cloud.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Aaron spake unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, then turned they towards the desert,—and lo! the glory of Yahweh, had appeared in the cloud,
text.DRB: And when Aaron spoke to all the assembly of the children of Israel, they looked towards the wilderness: and behold the glory of the Lord appeared in a cloud.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and said,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses—saying,
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: “I have heard the complaining of the Israelites; say to them, ‘At twilight you shall eat meat, and in the morning you shall have your fill of bread; then you shall know that I am the Lord your God.’”
text.Rotherham: I have heard the murmurings of the sons of Israel,—speak unto them, saying—Between the two evenings, shall ye eat flesh, and in the morning, shall ye be filled with bread,—so shall ye know that I Yahweh am your God.
text.DRB: I have heard the murmuring of the children of Israel: say to them: In the evening you shall eat flesh, and in the morning you shall have your fill of bread: and you shall know that I am the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: In the evening quails came up and covered the camp; and in the morning there was a layer of dew around the camp.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the evening, that there came up quail, and covered the camp,—and in the morning was the outpouring of dew, round about the camp;
text.DRB: So it came to pass in the evening, that quails coming up, covered the camp: and in the morning, a dew lay round about the camp.

text.NRSV: When the layer of dew lifted, there on the surface of the wilderness was a fine flaky substance, as fine as frost on the ground.
text.Rotherham: and when the outpouring of dew went up, then lo! on the face of the desert, a thin flake, thin as hoar-frost on the earth,
text.DRB: And when it had covered the face of the earth, it appeared in the wilderness small, and as it were beaten with a pestle, like unto the hoar frost on the ground.

text.NRSV: When the Israelites saw it, they said to one another, “What is it?” For they did not know what it was. Moses said to them, “It is the bread that the Lord has given you to eat.
text.Rotherham: And when the sons of Israel saw it they said one to another—What is that? For they knew not what "that" was. Then said Moses unto them, "That" is the bread which Yahweh hath given you to eat.
text.DRB: And when the children of Israel saw it, they said one to another: Manhu! which signifieth: What is this! for they knew not what it was. And Moses said to them: This is the bread, which the Lord hath given you to eat.

text.NRSV: This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Gather as much of it as each of you needs, an omer to a person according to the number of persons, all providing for those in their own tents.’”
text.Rotherham: This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, Gather ye thereof, each man what he needeth for eating,—an omer a head, by the number of your souls, each man—for them who are in his tent, shall ye take.
text.DRB: This is the word, that the Lord hath commanded: Let every one gather of it as much as is enough to eat: a gomor for every man, according to the number of your souls that dwell in a tent, so shall you take of it.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did so, some gathering more, some less.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel did so,—and gathered, [some] more, and [some] less;
text.DRB: And the children of Israel did so: and they gathered, one more, another less.

text.NRSV: But when they measured it with an omer, those who gathered much had nothing over, and those who gathered little had no shortage; they gathered as much as each of them needed.
text.Rotherham: and when they measured it with the omer, he who had gathered more had nothing over, and he who had gathered less had no lack: each man what he needed for eating, did they gather.
text.DRB: And they measured by the measure of a gomor: neither had he more that had gathered more: nor did he find less that had provided less: but every one had gathered, according to what they were able to eat.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to them, “Let no one leave any of it over until morning.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them,—Let, no man leave thereof, until morning.
text.DRB: And Moses said to them: Let no man leave thereof till the morning.

text.NRSV: But they did not listen to Moses; some left part of it until morning, and it bred worms and became foul. And Moses was angry with them.
text.Rotherham: But they hearkened not unto Moses, but certain men left thereof until morning, and it swarmed with worms and become loathsome,—and Moses was wroth with them.
text.DRB: And they hearkened not to him, but some of them left until the morning, and it began to be full of worms, and it putrified, and Moses was angry with them.

text.NRSV: Morning by morning they gathered it, as much as each needed; but when the sun grew hot, it melted.
text.Rotherham: Thus then they gathered it morning by morning, each man what he needed for eating, and as soon as the sun waxed hot, so soon it melted.
text.DRB: Now every one of them gathered in the morning, as much as might suffice to eat: and after the sun grew hot, it melted.

text.NRSV: On the sixth day they gathered twice as much food, two omers apiece. When all the leaders of the congregation came and told Moses,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the sixth day, that they gathered food twice as much, two omers for each one,—so all the princes of the assembly came in, and told Moses.
text.DRB: But on the sixth day they gathered twice as much, that is, two gomors every man: and all the rulers of the multitude came, and told Moses.

text.NRSV: he said to them, “This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Tomorrow is a day of solemn rest, a holy sabbath to the Lord; bake what you want to bake and boil what you want to boil, and all that is left over put aside to be kept until morning.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—That is what Yahweh spake. The observance of a holy sabbath to Yahweh, is to-morrow,—what ye would bake, bake and what ye would boil, boil, and all that is left over, put by you to keep until the morning.
text.DRB: And he said to them: This is what the Lord hath spoken: Tomorrow is the rest of the sabbath sanctified to the Lord. Whatsoever work is to be done, do it: and the meats that are to be dressed, dress them: and whatsoever shall remain, lay it up until the morning.

text.NRSV: So they put it aside until morning, as Moses commanded them; and it did not become foul, and there were no worms in it.
text.Rotherham: So they put it by until the morning, as Moses had commanded,—and it gave no unpleasant odour, neither was there a worm, therein.
text.DRB: And they did so as Moses had commanded, and it did not putrify, neither was there worm found in it.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “Eat it today, for today is a sabbath to the Lord; today you will not find it in the field.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses—Eat ye it to-day, for a sabbath, is to-day, unto Yahweh,—to-day, ye shall not find it in the field.
text.DRB: And Moses said: Eat it today, because it is the sabbath of the Lord: today it shall not be found in the field.

text.NRSV: Six days you shall gather it; but on the seventh day, which is a sabbath, there will be none.”
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall ye gather it,—but on the seventh day, a sabbath, it shall not be therein.
text.DRB: Gather it six days: but on the seventh day is the sabbath of the Lord, therefore it shall not be found.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day some of the people went out to gather, and they found none.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the seventh day, that there went forth some of the people to gather,—but they found not.
text.DRB: And the seventh day came: and some of the people going forth to gather, found none.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “How long will you refuse to keep my commandments and instructions?
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses,—How long have ye refused to keep my commandments and my laws?
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: How long will you refuse to keep my commandments, and my law?

text.NRSV: See! The Lord has given you the sabbath, therefore on the sixth day he gives you food for two days; each of you stay where you are; do not leave your place on the seventh day.”
text.Rotherham: See because Yahweh hath given you the sabbath, for this cause, is, he, giving you on the sixth day, food for two days,—abide ye every man in his place, let no man go forth from his dwelling, on the seventh day,
text.DRB: See that the Lord hath given you the sabbath, and for this reason on the sixth day he giveth you a double provision: let each man stay at home, and let none go forth out of his place the seventh day.

text.NRSV: So the people rested on the seventh day.
text.Rotherham: So the people rested on the seventh day.
text.DRB: And the people kept the sabbath on the seventh day.

text.NRSV: The house of Israel called it manna; it was like coriander seed, white, and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
text.Rotherham: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna,—and the same, was like coriander seed white, and the taste thereof, like flat-cake with honey,
text.DRB: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna: and it was like coriander seed white, and the taste thereof like to flour with honey.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Let an omer of it be kept throughout your generations, in order that they may see the food with which I fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.’”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, Fill an omer therefrom as a thing to be preserved to your generations,—that they may see the bread wherewith I fed you in the desert, when I brought you forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And Moses said: This is the word, which the Lord hath commanded: Fill a gomor of it, and let it be kept unto generations to come hereafter, that they may know the bread, wherewith I fed you in the wilderness, when you were brought forth out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to Aaron, “Take a jar, and put an omer of manna in it, and place it before the Lord, to be kept throughout your generations.”
text.Rotherham: So Moses said unto Aaron—Take a single basket, and put therein an omer-full of manna,—and set it down before Yahweh, as a thing to be preserved to your generations.
text.DRB: And Moses said to Aaron: Take a vessel, and put manna into it, as much as a gomor can hold: and lay it up before the Lord to keep unto your generations,

text.NRSV: As the Lord commanded Moses, so Aaron placed it before the covenant, for safekeeping.
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh gave command unto Moses, so did Aaron set it down before the testimony, as a thing to be preserved,
text.DRB: As the Lord commanded Moses. And Aaron put it in the tabernacle to be kept.

text.NRSV: The Israelites ate manna forty years, until they came to a habitable land; they ate manna, until they came to the border of the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Israel did eat the manna forty years, until they entered into the land wherein they were to dwell,—the manna, they did eat until they entered into the bounds of the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel ate manna forty years, till they came to a habitable land: with this meat were they fed, until they reached the borders of the land of Chanaan.

text.NRSV: An omer is a tenth of an ephah.
text.Rotherham: Now, an omer, is, the tenth of the ephah.
text.DRB: Now a gomor is the tenth part of an ephi.

text.NRSV: From the wilderness of Sin the whole congregation of the Israelites journeyed by stages, as the Lord commanded. They camped at Rephidim, but there was no water for the people to drink.
text.Rotherham: And all the assembly of the sons of Israel set forward out of the desert of Sin, by their removings, at the bidding of Yahweh,—and encamped in Rephidim, and there was no water for the people to drink.
text.DRB: Then all the multitude of the children of Israel setting forward from the desert of Sin, by their mansions, according to the word of the Lord, encamped in Raphidim, where there was no water for the people to drink.

text.NRSV: The people quarreled with Moses, and said, “Give us water to drink.” Moses said to them, “Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you test the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And the people found fault with Moses, and said—Give us water, that we may drink. And Moses said to them, Why should ye find fault with me? Why should ye put Yahweh to the proof?
text.DRB: And they chode with Moses, and said: Give us water, that we may drink. And Moses answered them: Why chide you with me? Wherefore do you tempt the Lord?

text.NRSV: But the people thirsted there for water; and the people complained against Moses and said, “Why did you bring us out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and livestock with thirst?”
text.Rotherham: So the people thirsted, there for water, and the people murmured against Moses,—and said—Wherefore is it that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill me and my sons and my cattle, with thirst?
text.DRB: So the people were thirsty there for want of water, and murmured against Moses, saying: Why didst thou make us go forth out of Egypt, to kill us and our children, and our beasts with thirst?

text.NRSV: So Moses cried out to the Lord, “What shall I do with this people? They are almost ready to stone me.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses made outcry unto Yahweh saying, What am I to do, with this people? Yet a little, and they will stone me.
text.DRB: And Moses cried to the Lord, saying: What shall I do to this people? Yet a little more and they will stone me.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Go on ahead of the people, and take some of the elders of Israel with you; take in your hand the staff with which you struck the Nile, and go.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Pass over before the people, and take with thee some of the elders of Israel,—thy staff also wherewith thou didst smite the river, take thou in thy hand, so shalt thou go thy way.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Go before the people, and take with thee of the ancients of Israel: and take in thy hand the rod wherewith thou didst strike the river, and go.

text.NRSV: I will be standing there in front of you on the rock at Horeb. Strike the rock, and water will come out of it, so that the people may drink.” Moses did so, in the sight of the elders of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! standing before thee, there upon the rock in Horeb, then shalt thou smite the rock and there shall come forth therefrom water, and the people shall drink. And Moses did so, in the sight of the elders of Israel.
text.DRB: Behold I will stand there before thee, upon the rock Horeb: and thou shalt strike the rock, and water shall come out of it that the people may drink. Moses did so before the ancients of Israel:

text.NRSV: He called the place Massah and Meribah, because the Israelites quarreled and tested the Lord, saying, “Is the Lord among us or not?”
text.Rotherham: So he called the name of the place, Massah, and Meribah,—because of the fault-finding of the sons of Israel, and because of their putting Yahweh to the proof, saying, Is Yahweh in our midst or is he not?
text.DRB: And he called the name of that place Temptation, because the chiding of the children of Israel, and for that they tempted the Lord, saying: Is the Lord amongst us or not?

text.NRSV: Then Amalek came and fought with Israel at Rephidim.
text.Rotherham: Then came in Amalek,—and fought with Israel, in Rephidim.
text.DRB: And Amalec came, and fought against Israel in Raphidim.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Joshua, “Choose some men for us and go out, fight with Amalek. Tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the staff of God in my hand.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Joshua—Choose for us men, and go forth, fight with Amalek,—to-morrow, am, I, stationing myself upon the top of the hill, with the staff of God in my hand.
text.DRB: And Moses said to Josue: Choose out men: and go out and fight against Amalec: tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill having the rod of God in my hand.

text.NRSV: So Joshua did as Moses told him, and fought with Amalek, while Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua did as Moses had said to him, to fight with Amalek,—and Moses, Aaron and Hur, went up to the top of the hill.
text.DRB: Josue did as Moses had spoken, and he fought against Amalec; but Moses, and Aaron, and Hur went up upon the top of the hill.

text.NRSV: Whenever Moses held up his hand, Israel prevailed; and whenever he lowered his hand, Amalek prevailed.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be when Moses shall lift on high his hands, then shall Israel prevail, but when he shall let down his hands, then shall Amalek prevail.
text.DRB: And when Moses lifted up his hands, Israel overcame: but if he let them down a little, Amalec overcame.

text.NRSV: But Moses’ hands grew weary; so they took a stone and put it under him, and he sat on it. Aaron and Hur held up his hands, one on one side, and the other on the other side; so his hands were steady until the sun set.
text.Rotherham: But, the hands of Moses, were weary, so they took a stone, and put under him, and he sat thereupon,—and, Aaron and Hur, upheld his hands—on this side, one and on that side, one, and so his hands became steady, until the going in of the sun.
text.DRB: And Moses' hands were heavy: so they took a stone, and put under him, and he sat on it: and Aaron and Hur stayed up his hands on both sides. And it came to pass that his hands were not weary until sunset.

text.NRSV: And Joshua defeated Amalek and his people with the sword.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua overthrew Amalek and his people, with the edge of the sword.
text.DRB: And Josue put Amalec and his people to flight, by the edge of the sword.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Write this as a reminder in a book and recite it in the hearing of Joshua: I will utterly blot out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Write this as a remembrancer in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua—that I will, wipe out, the remembrance of Amalek, from under the heavens.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Write this for a memorial in a book, and deliver it to the ears of Josue: for I will destroy the memory of Amalec from under heaven.

text.NRSV: And Moses built an altar and called it, The Lord is my banner.
text.Rotherham: And Moses built an altar,—and called the name thereof, Yahweh-nissi.
text.DRB: And Moses built an altar: and called the name thereof, The Lord my exaltation, saying:

text.NRSV: He said, “A hand upon the banner of the Lord! The Lord will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Because of a hand against the throne of Yah, Yahweh hath war with Amalek,—from generation to generation!
text.DRB: Because the hand of the throne of the Lord, and the war of the Lord shall be against Amalec, from generation to generation.

text.NRSV: Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses’ father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses and for his people Israel, how the Lord had brought Israel out of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Jethro, priest of Midian, father-in-law of Moses, heard all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people,—in that Yahweh had brought forth Israel out of Egypt.
text.DRB: And when Jethro the priest of Madian, the kinsman of Moses, had heard all the things that God had done to Moses, and to Israel his people, and that the Lord had brought forth Israel out of Egypt,

text.NRSV: After Moses had sent away his wife Zipporah, his father-in-law Jethro took her back,
text.Rotherham: So Jethro, Moses father-in-law, took Zipporah, Moses wife,—after she had been sent home;
text.DRB: He took Sephora the wife of Moses whom he had sent back:

text.NRSV: along with her two sons. The name of the one was Gershom (for he said, “I have been an alien in a foreign land”),
text.Rotherham: and her two sons,—of whom, the name of the one, was Gershom, for, said he, A sojourner, am I in a strange land,
text.DRB: And her two sons, of whom one was called Gersam, his father saying: I have been a stranger in a foreign country.

text.NRSV: and the name of the other, Eliezer (for he said, “The God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh”).
text.Rotherham: and, the name of the other, Eliezer, for the God of my father, was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.
text.DRB: And the other Eliezer: For the God of my father, said he, is my helper, and hath delivered me from the sword of Pharao.

text.NRSV: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, came into the wilderness where Moses was encamped at the mountain of God, bringing Moses’ sons and wife to him.
text.Rotherham: And Jethro, Moses father-in-law, came in, with his two sons and his wife, unto Moses,—into the desert where, he, was encamping, the mountain of God;
text.DRB: And Jethro the kinsman of Moses came with his sons and his wife, to Moses into the desert, where he was camped by the mountain of God.

text.NRSV: He sent word to Moses, “I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you, with your wife and her two sons.”
text.Rotherham: and said unto Moses, I, thy father-in-law, Jethro, am coming in unto thee,—and thy wife, and her two sons, with her.
text.DRB: And he sent word to Moses, saying: I Jethro thy kinsman come to thee, and thy wife, and thy two sons with her.

text.NRSV: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law; he bowed down and kissed him; each asked after the other’s welfare, and they went into the tent.
text.Rotherham: So Moses went forth to meet his father-in-law, and bowed himself down to him and kissed him, and they asked each other of their welfare,—and came into the tent.
text.DRB: And he went out to meet his kinsman, and worshipped and kissed him: and they saluted one another with words of peace. And when he was come into the tent,

text.NRSV: Then Moses told his father-in-law all that the Lord had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the hardship that had beset them on the way, and how the Lord had delivered them.
text.Rotherham: Then Moses recounted to his father-in-law, all that Yahweh had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians, for Israel’s sake,—all the hardship which had befallen them by the way, and how Yahweh had delivered them.
text.DRB: Moses told his kinsman all that the Lord had done to Pharao, and the Egyptians, in favour of Israel: and all the labour which had befallen them in the journey, and that the Lord had delivered them.

text.NRSV: Jethro rejoiced for all the good that the Lord had done to Israel, in delivering them from the Egyptians.
text.Rotherham: Then did Jethro rejoice, for all the good which Yahweh had done to Israel,—whom he had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.
text.DRB: And Jethro rejoiced for all the good things that the Lord had done to Israel, because he had delivered them out of the hands of the Egyptians.

text.NRSV: Jethro said, “Blessed be the Lord, who has delivered you from the Egyptians and from Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: And Jethro said—Blessed be Yahweh, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of Pharaoh,—Who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians:
text.DRB: And he said: Blessed is the Lord, who hath delivered you out of the hand of Pharao, and out of the hand of the Egyptians, who hath delivered his people out of the hand of Egypt.

text.NRSV: Now I know that the Lord is greater than all gods, because he delivered the people from the Egyptians, when they dealt arrogantly with them.”
text.Rotherham: Now, I know, that, greater, is Yahweh than all the gods,—Even in the thing wherein they were arrogant over them.
text.DRB: Now I know that the Lord is great above all gods: because they dealt proudly against them.

text.NRSV: And Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, brought a burnt offering and sacrifices to God; and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses’ father-in-law in the presence of God.
text.Rotherham: Then took Jethro father-in-law of Moses, an ascending-offering and sacrifices to God,—and Aaron and all the elders of Israel came in to eat bread with the father-in-law of Moses before God.
text.DRB: So Jethro the kinsman of Moses offered holocausts and sacrifices to God: and Aaron and all the ancients of Israel came, to eat bread with them before God.

text.NRSV: The next day Moses sat as judge for the people, while the people stood around him from morning until evening.
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat, to judge the people,—and the people stood by Moses, from the morning, until the evening.
text.DRB: And the next day Moses sat, to judge the people, who stood by Moses from morning until night.

text.NRSV: When Moses’ father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he said, “What is this that you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone, while all the people stand around you from morning until evening?”
text.Rotherham: And when Moses father-in-law saw all that he, was doing for the people, he said—What is this thing which, thou, art doing for the people? Wherefore art, thou, sitting alone, with all the people, stationed by thee, from morning until evening?
text.DRB: And when his kinsman had seen all things that he did among the people, he said: What is it that thou dost among the people? Why sittest thou alone, and all the people wait from morning till night.

text.NRSV: Moses said to his father-in-law, “Because the people come to me to inquire of God.
text.Rotherham: And Moses said to his father-in-law,—Because the people come in unto me to seek God:
text.DRB: And Moses answered him: The people come to me to seek the judgment of God.

text.NRSV: When they have a dispute, they come to me and I decide between one person and another, and I make known to them the statutes and instructions of God.”
text.Rotherham: When they have a matter, they come in unto me, then do I judge between a man and his neighbour,—and make known the statutes of God and his laws.
text.DRB: And when any controversy falleth out among them, they come to me to judge between them, and to shew the precepts of God, and his laws.

text.NRSV: Moses’ father-in-law said to him, “What you are doing is not good.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses’ father-in-law unto him,—Not good, is the thing that thou art doing.
text.DRB: But he said: The thing thou dost is not good.

text.NRSV: You will surely wear yourself out, both you and these people with you. For the task is too heavy for you; you cannot do it alone.
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt get quite worn out, both thou and this people that is with thee,—for the thing is too heavy for thee, thou canst not do it, alone.
text.DRB: Thou are spent with foolish labour, both thou and this people that is with thee: the business is above thy strength, thou alone canst not bear it.

text.NRSV: Now listen to me. I will give you counsel, and God be with you! You should represent the people before God, and you should bring their cases before God;
text.Rotherham: Now, hearken thou to my voice—let me counsel thee, and may God be with thee: Be, thou, for the people, in front of God, so shalt, thou, bring the matters unto God;
text.DRB: But hear my words and counsels, and God shall be with thee. Be thou to the people in those things that pertain to God, to bring their words to him:

text.NRSV: teach them the statutes and instructions and make known to them the way they are to go and the things they are to do.
text.Rotherham: and shalt cause to shine upon them, the statutes and the laws,—and make known to them the way wherein they should go, and the work they should do.
text.DRB: And to shew the people the ceremonies and the manner of worshipping, and the way wherein they ought to walk, and the work that they ought to do.

text.NRSV: You should also look for able men among all the people, men who fear God, are trustworthy, and hate dishonest gain; set such men over them as officers over thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore shalt look out, from among all the people—men of ability, reverers of God, men of fidelity, haters of extortion,—and place them over [them] as rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
text.DRB: And provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, in whom there is truth, and that hate avarice, and appoint of them rulers of thousands, and of hundreds, and of fifties, and of tens.

text.NRSV: Let them sit as judges for the people at all times; let them bring every important case to you, but decide every minor case themselves. So it will be easier for you, and they will bear the burden with you.
text.Rotherham: So shall they judge the people at any time, and it shall be, all the great matters, shall they bring in unto thee, but all the small matters, shall, they themselves, judge,—so, lighten thou the burden for thyself, and let them bear it with thee.
text.DRB: Who may judge the people at all times: and when any great matter soever shall fall out, let them refer it to thee, and let them judge the lesser matters only: that so it may be lighter for thee, the burden being shared out unto others.

text.NRSV: If you do this, and God so commands you, then you will be able to endure, and all these people will go to their home in peace.”
text.Rotherham: If, this thing, thou wilt do, and God shall command thee, then shalt thou be able to endure,—yea, moreover, all this people, shall go in, unto their dwellings, contented.
text.DRB: If thou dost this, thou shalt fulfill the commandment of God, and shalt be able to bear his precepts: and all this people shall return to their places with peace.

text.NRSV: So Moses listened to his father-in-law and did all that he had said.
text.Rotherham: So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said;
text.DRB: And when Moses heard this, he did all things that he had suggested unto him.

text.NRSV: Moses chose able men from all Israel and appointed them as heads over the people, as officers over thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens.
text.Rotherham: and Moses chose men of ability out of all Israel, and set them to be heads over the people,—rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
text.DRB: And choosing able men out of all Israel, he appointed them rulers of the people, rulers over thousands, and over hundreds, and over fifties, and over tens.

text.NRSV: And they judged the people at all times; hard cases they brought to Moses, but any minor case they decided themselves.
text.Rotherham: And they shall judge the people, at any time,—the hard matters, shall they bring in unto Moses, but all the small matters, shall, they themselves, judge.
text.DRB: And they judged the people at all times: and whatsoever was of greater difficulty they referred to him, and they judged the easier cases only.

text.NRSV: Then Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went off to his own country.
text.Rotherham: And Moses sent forth his father-in-law,—and he went his way by himself unto his own land.
text.DRB: And he let his kinsman depart: and he returned and went into his own country.

text.NRSV: On the third new moon after the Israelites had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that very day, they came into the wilderness of Sinai.
text.Rotherham: In the third month, by the coming forth of the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, on this day, came they into the desert of Sinai:
text.DRB: In the third month of the departure of Israel out of the land of Egypt, on this day they came into the wilderness of Sinai:

text.NRSV: They had journeyed from Rephidim, entered the wilderness of Sinai, and camped in the wilderness; Israel camped there in front of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: then was it that they brake up out of Rephidim and came into the desert of Sinai, and encamped in the desert,—yea Israel encamped there before the mountain.
text.DRB: For departing out of Raphidim, and coming to the desert of Sinai, they camped in the same place, and there Israel pitched their tents over against the mountain.

text.NRSV: Then Moses went up to God; the Lord called to him from the mountain, saying, “Thus you shall say to the house of Jacob, and tell the Israelites:
text.Rotherham: And when Moses, had gone up unto God, then called Yahweh unto him out of the mountain saying, Thus, shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, And tell the sons of Israel:
text.DRB: And Moses went up to God : and the Lord called unto him from the mountain, and said: Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: You have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles’ wings and brought you to myself.
text.Rotherham: Ye, have seen what I did unto the Egyptians,—And how I bare you upon wings of eagles, And brought you in unto myself:—
text.DRB: You have seen what I have done to the Egyptians, how I have carried you upon the wings of eagles, and have taken you to myself.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, if you obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my treasured possession out of all peoples. Indeed, the whole earth is mine,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, if ye will, indeed hearken, to my voice, And keep my covenant, Then shall ye be mine as a treasure beyond all the peoples, For, mine, is all the earth;
text.DRB: If therefore you will hear my voice, and keep my covenant, you shall be my peculiar possession above all people: for all the earth is mine.

text.NRSV: but you shall be for me a priestly kingdom and a holy nation. These are the words that you shall speak to the Israelites.”
text.Rotherham: But, ye, shall be mine, As a kingdom of priests, And a holy nation. These, are the words, which thou shalt speak unto the sons of Israel.
text.DRB: And you shall be to me a priestly kingdom, and a holy nation. Those are the words thou shalt speak to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: So Moses came, summoned the elders of the people, and set before them all these words that the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: Then came Moses, and called for the elders of the people,—and put before them all these words which Yahweh had commanded him.
text.DRB: Moses came, and calling together the elders of the people, he declared all the words which the Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: The people all answered as one: “Everything that the Lord has spoken we will do.” Moses reported the words of the people to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And all the people responded together and said, All that Yahweh hath spoken, will we do. And Moses took back the words of the people, unto Yahweh.
text.DRB: And all the people answered together: All that the Lord hath spoken, we will do. And when Moses had related the people's words to the Lord,

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “I am going to come to you in a dense cloud, in order that the people may hear when I speak with you and so trust you ever after.” When Moses had told the words of the people to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses: Lo! I, am coming unto thee in the veiling of cloud, in order that the people may hear when I speak with thee, moreover also, that, in thee, they may trust to times age-abiding. Then told Moses the words of the people unto Yahweh.
text.DRB: The Lord said to him: Lo, now will I come to thee in the darkness of a cloud, that the people may hear me speaking to thee, and may believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people to the Lord.

text.NRSV: the Lord said to Moses: “Go to the people and consecrate them today and tomorrow. Have them wash their clothes
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses—Go unto the people, and thou shalt hallow them to-day, and to-morrow,—and they shall wash their clothes;
text.DRB: And he said to him: Go to the people, and sanctify them today, and tomorrow, and let them wash their garments.

text.NRSV: and prepare for the third day, because on the third day the Lord will come down upon Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people.
text.Rotherham: and shall be ready, by the third day,—for on the third day, will Yahweh come down in the sight of all the people upon Mount Sinai.
text.DRB: And let them be ready against the third day: for on the third day the Lord will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.

text.NRSV: You shall set limits for the people all around, saying, ‘Be careful not to go up the mountain or to touch the edge of it. Any who touch the mountain shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt set bounds for the people round about saying, Take heed to yourselves—that ye go not up into the mountain, nor touch the boundary thereof, whosoever toucheth the mountain, shall, surely die.
text.DRB: And thou shalt appoint certain limits to the people round about, and thou shalt say to them: Take heed you go not up into the mount, and that ye touch not the borders thereof: every one that toucheth the mount dying he shall die.

text.NRSV: No hand shall touch them, but they shall be stoned or shot with arrows; whether animal or human being, they shall not live.’ When the trumpet sounds a long blast, they may go up on the mountain.”
text.Rotherham: No hand shall touch it but he shall be, surely stoned, or be, surely shot, whether beast or man, he shall not live,—When the ram’s horn soundeth, they themselves, shall come up within the mount,
text.DRB: No hands shall touch him, but he shall be stoned to death, or shall be shot through with arrows: whether it be beast, or man, he shall not live. When the trumpet shall begin to sound, then let them go up into the mount.

text.NRSV: So Moses went down from the mountain to the people. He consecrated the people, and they washed their clothes.
text.Rotherham: Then Moses went down out of the mount, unto the people,—and hallowed the people, and they washed their clothes.
text.DRB: And Moses came down from the mount to the people, and sanctified them. And when they had washed their garments,

text.NRSV: And he said to the people, “Prepare for the third day; do not go near a woman.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto the people, Be ready, by the third day,—do not approach a woman.
text.DRB: He said to them: Be ready against the third day, and come not near your wives.

text.NRSV: On the morning of the third day there was thunder and lightning, as well as a thick cloud on the mountain, and a blast of a trumpet so loud that all the people who were in the camp trembled.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the third day, when the morning had come, that there were thunderings and lightnings, and a heavy cloud upon the mount, and the sound of a horn, loud exceedingly,—and all the people who were in the camp trembled.
text.DRB: And now the third day was come, and the morning appeared: and behold thunders began to be heard, and lightning to flash, and a very thick cloud to cover the mount, and the noise of the trumpet sounded exceeding loud, and the people that was in the camp, feared.

text.NRSV: Moses brought the people out of the camp to meet God. They took their stand at the foot of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: And Moses brought forth the people, to meet God out of the camp,—and they stationed themselves in the lower part of the mount,
text.DRB: And when Moses had brought them forth to meet God from the place of the camp, they stood at the bottom of the mount.

text.NRSV: Now Mount Sinai was wrapped in smoke, because the Lord had descended upon it in fire; the smoke went up like the smoke of a kiln, while the whole mountain shook violently.
text.Rotherham: And Mount Sinai, smoked, all over, because Yahweh had come down thereon in fire,—and the smoke thereof went up as the smoke of a furnace, and all the mountain trembled exceedingly.
text.DRB: And all mount Sinai was on a smoke: because the Lord was come down upon it in fire, and the smoke arose from it as out of a furnace: and all the mount was terrible.

text.NRSV: As the blast of the trumpet grew louder and louder, Moses would speak and God would answer him in thunder.
text.Rotherham: And as oft as the sound of the horn went on and became exceeding loud, Moses, spake and, God, responded to him with a voice.
text.DRB: And the sound of the trumpet grew by degrees louder and louder, and was drawn out to a greater length: Moses spoke, and God answered him.

text.NRSV: When the Lord descended upon Mount Sinai, to the top of the mountain, the Lord summoned Moses to the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.
text.Rotherham: Thus came Yahweh down upon Mount Sinai unto the top of the mount,—and Yahweh called Moses unto the top of the mount, and Moses went up.
text.DRB: And the Lord came down upon mount Sinai, in the very top of the mount, and he called Moses unto the top thereof. And when he was gone up thither,

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Go down and warn the people not to break through to the Lord to look; otherwise many of them will perish.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Go down, adjure the people,—lest they press through unto Yahweh to see, and so there fall from among them a multitude.
text.DRB: He said unto him: Go down, and charge the people: lest they should have a mind to pass the limits to see the Lord, and a very great multitude of them should perish.

text.NRSV: Even the priests who approach the Lord must consecrate themselves or the Lord will break out against them.”
text.Rotherham: Yea, even the priests who do approach unto Yahweh, must hallow themselves,—lest Yahweh break in upon them.
text.DRB: The priests also that come to the Lord, let them be sanctified, lest he strike them.

text.NRSV: Moses said to the Lord, “The people are not permitted to come up to Mount Sinai; for you yourself warned us, saying, ‘Set limits around the mountain and keep it holy.’”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Yahweh, The people cannot come up into Mount Sinai,—for, thou thyself, hast adjured us saying: Set bounds to the mountain and hallow it.
