text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a covering, for the tent, of rams’ skins dyed red,—and a covering of badger’s skins, above.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also another cover to the roof, of rams' skins dyed red; and over that again another cover of violet coloured skins.

text.NRSV: You shall make upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make boards for the habitation,—of acacia wood to stand up:
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also the boards of the tabernacle standing upright of setim wood.

text.NRSV: Ten cubits shall be the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the width of each frame.
text.Rotherham: ten cubits, the length of the board, and a cubit and a half cubit the breadth of each board:
text.DRB: Let every one of them be ten cubits in length, and in breadth one cubit and a half.

text.NRSV: There shall be two pegs in each frame to fit the frames together; you shall make these for all the frames of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: two tenons, to each board, united one to another,—thus, shalt thou make, for all the boards of the habitation,
text.DRB: In the sides of the boards shall be made two mortises, whereby one board may be joined to another board: and after this manner shall all the boards be prepared.

text.NRSV: You shall make the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side;
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt make the boards for the habitation, twenty boards, on the south side southwards.
text.DRB: Of which twenty shall be in the south side southward.

text.NRSV: and you shall make forty bases of silver under the twenty frames, two bases under the first frame for its two pegs, and two bases under the next frame for its two pegs;
text.Rotherham: And forty sockets of silver, shalt thou make under the twenty boards,—two sockets under the one board for its two tenons, and two sockets, under the next board, for its two tenons.
text.DRB: For which thou shalt cast forty sockets of silver, that under every board may be put two sockets at the two corners.

text.NRSV: and for the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side twenty frames,
text.Rotherham: And on the second side of the habitation, towards the north, twenty boards;
text.DRB: In the second side also the tabernacle that looketh to the north, there shall be twenty boards,

text.NRSV: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under the first frame, and two bases under the next frame;
text.Rotherham: and their forty sockets of silver,—two sockets, under the one board, and, two sockets, under the next board.
text.DRB: Having forty sockets of silver, two sockets shall be put under each board.

text.NRSV: and for the rear of the tabernacle westward you shall make six frames.
text.Rotherham: And for the hinderpart of the habitation, westward, shalt thou make six boards.
text.DRB: But on the west side of the tabernacle thou shalt make six boards.

text.NRSV: You shall make two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear;
text.Rotherham: And, two boards, shalt thou make, for the corners of the habitation,—in the hinderpart:
text.DRB: And again other two which shall be erected in the corners at the back of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: they shall be separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring; it shall be the same with both of them; they shall form the two corners.
text.Rotherham: that they may be double beneath, and at the same time, they shall be entire on the top thereof, into each ring,—thus, shall it be for them both, for the two corners, shall they be.
text.DRB: And they shall be joined together from beneath unto the top, and one joint shall hold them all. The like joining shall be observed for the two boards also that are to be put in the corners.

text.NRSV: And so there shall be eight frames, with their bases of silver, sixteen bases; two bases under the first frame, and two bases under the next frame.
text.Rotherham: So shall they be eight boards, and, their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets,—two sockets, under the one board, and, two sockets, under the next board.
text.DRB: And they shall be in all eight boards, and their silver sockets sixteen, reckoning two sockets for each board.

text.NRSV: You shall make bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make bars, of acacia wood,—five, for the boards of the one side of the habitation,
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also five bars of setim wood, to hold together the boards on one side of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the side of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
text.Rotherham: and five bars, for the boards of the other side of the habitation, and five bars, for the boards of the side of the habitation, at the hinderpart westward;
text.DRB: And five others on the other side, and as many at the west side:

text.NRSV: The middle bar, halfway up the frames, shall pass through from end to end.
text.Rotherham: with the middle bar in the midst of the boards,—running along from end to end.
text.DRB: And they shall be put along by the midst of the boards from one end to the other.

text.NRSV: You shall overlay the frames with gold, and shall make their rings of gold to hold the bars; and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
text.Rotherham: And the boards, shalt thou overlay with gold, and their rings, shalt thou make of gold, as receptacles for the bars,—and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold.
text.DRB: The boards also themselves thou shalt overlay with gold, and shall cast rings of gold to be set upon them, for places for the bars to hold together boardwork: which bars thou shalt cover with plates of gold.

text.NRSV: Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to the plan for it that you have been shown on the mountain.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou rear up the habitation, according to the fashion thereof, which thou wast caused to see in the mount.
text.DRB: And thou shalt rear up the tabernacle according to the pattern that was shewn thee in the mount.

text.NRSV: You shall make a curtain of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen; it shall be made with cherubim skillfully worked into it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a veil, of blue and purple, and crimson and fine-twined linen,—of the work of a skilful weaver, shall it be made, with cherubim;
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a veil of violet and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen, wrought with embroidered work, and goodly variety:

text.NRSV: You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, which have hooks of gold and rest on four bases of silver.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, with their hooks of gold,—upon four sockets of silver,
text.DRB: And thou shalt hang it up before four pillars of setim wood, which themselves also shall be overlaid with gold, and shall have heads of gold, but sockets of silver.

text.NRSV: You shall hang the curtain under the clasps, and bring the ark of the covenant in there, within the curtain; and the curtain shall separate for you the holy place from the most holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt hang up the veil under the clasps,—and shalt bring in thither, within the veil the ark of the testimony; and the veil shall make a division for you between the holy [place] and the most holy.
text.DRB: And the veils shall be hanged on with rings, and within it thou shalt put the ark of the testimony, and the sanctuary , and the holy of holies shall be divided with it.

text.NRSV: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the covenant in the most holy place.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place the propitiatory, upon the ark of the testimony,—in the most holy [place] .
text.DRB: And thou shalt set the propitiatory upon the ark of the testimony in the holy of holies.

text.NRSV: You shall set the table outside the curtain, and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle opposite the table; and you shall put the table on the north side.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt set the table outside the veil, and the lampstand over against the table, on the side of the habitation, southward,—and the table, shalt thou place on the north side.
text.DRB: And the table without the veil: and over against the table the candlestick in the south side of the tabernacle; for the table shall stand in the north side.

text.NRSV: You shall make a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a screen for the opening of the tent, of blue, and purple and crimson, and fine-twined linen,—the work of an embroiderer.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a hanging in the entrance of the tabernacle of violet and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen with embroidered work.

text.NRSV: You shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast five bases of bronze for them.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and shalt overlay them with gold, and their hooks [shall be] of gold,—and thou shalt cast for them five sockets of bronze.
text.DRB: And thou shalt overlay with gold five pillars of setim wood, before which the hanging shall be drawn: their heads shall be of gold, and the sockets of brass.

text.NRSV: You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square, and it shall be three cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the altar of acacia wood,—of five cubits length and five cubits breadth four square, shall the altar be, and three cubits the height thereof.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also an altar of setim wood, which shall be five cubits long and as many broad, that is, foursquare, and three cubits high.

text.NRSV: You shall make horns for it on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it, and you shall overlay it with bronze.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make its horns upon its four corners, of the same, shall be its horns,—and thou shalt overlay it with copper.
text.DRB: And there shall be horns at the four corners of the same: and thou shalt cover it with brass.

text.NRSV: You shall make pots for it to receive its ashes, and shovels and basins and forks and firepans; you shall make all its utensils of bronze.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make its pans for removing its ashes, and its shovels and its sprinkling bowls, and its flesh-hooks and its fire-pans,—all its vessels, shalt thou make of bronze.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make for the uses thereof pans to receive the ashes, and tongs and fleshhooks, and firepans: all its vessels thou shalt make of brass.

text.NRSV: You shall also make for it a grating, a network of bronze; and on the net you shall make four bronze rings at its four corners.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make to it a grating, a network of bronze,—and thou shalt make upon the network, four rings of bronze, upon the four corners thereof.
text.DRB: And a grate of brass in manner of a net: at the four corners of which shall be four rings of brass,

text.NRSV: You shall set it below, under the ledge of the altar, so that the net shall extend halfway down the altar.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place it under the margin of the altar beneath,—so shall the network reach unto the midst of the altar.
text.DRB: Which thou shalt put under the hearth of the altar: and the grate shall be even to the midst of the altar.

text.NRSV: You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of acacia wood,—and shalt overlay them with copper.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also two bars for the altar of setim wood, which thou shalt cover with plates of brass:

text.NRSV: the poles shall be put through the rings, so that the poles shall be on the two sides of the altar when it is carried.
text.Rotherham: And the staves shall be brought into the rings,—so shall the staves be on the two sides of the altar in lifting it:
text.DRB: And thou shalt draw them through rings, and they shall be on both sides of the altar to carry it.

text.NRSV: You shall make it hollow, with boards. They shall be made just as you were shown on the mountain.
text.Rotherham: Hollow with boards, shalt thou make it,—as it was showed thee in the mount, so, shall they make it.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not make it solid, but empty and hollow in the inside, as it was shewn thee in the mount.

text.NRSV: You shall make the court of the tabernacle. On the south side the court shall have hangings of fine twisted linen one hundred cubits long for that side;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the court of the habitation,—on the south side southward, hangings for the court of fine-twined linen, a hundred cubits in length to the one side;
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also the court of the tabernacle, in the south side whereof southward there shall be hangings of fine twisted linen of a hundred cubits long for one side.

text.NRSV: its twenty pillars and their twenty bases shall be of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands shall be of silver.
text.Rotherham: and the pillars thereof twenty, with their sockets twenty, of bronze,—and the hooks of the pillars and their cross-rods of silver.
text.DRB: And twenty pillars with as many sockets of brass, the heads of which with their engraving of silver.

text.NRSV: Likewise for its length on the north side there shall be hangings one hundred cubits long, their pillars twenty and their bases twenty, of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands shall be of silver.
text.Rotherham: And, so, for the north side in length, hangings a hundred cubits,—and the pillars thereof twenty, and their sockets twenty. of bronze, and the hooks of the pillars and their cross-rods of silver.
text.DRB: In like manner also on the north side there shall be hangings of a hundred cubits long, twenty pillars, and as many sockets of brass, and their heads with their engraving of silver.

text.NRSV: For the width of the court on the west side there shall be fifty cubits of hangings, with ten pillars and ten bases.
text.Rotherham: And, the breadth of the court on the west side, hangings fifty cubits,—their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.
text.DRB: But in the breadth of the court, that looketh to the west, there shall be hangings of fifty cubits, and ten pillars, and as many sockets.

text.NRSV: The width of the court on the front to the east shall be fifty cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, the breadth of the court on the east side eastward, fifty cubits;
text.DRB: In that breadth also of the court, which looketh to the east, there shall be fifty cubits.

text.NRSV: There shall be fifteen cubits of hangings on the one side, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: and fifteen cubits the hangings for the side-piece,—their pillars three, and their sockets three;
text.DRB: In which there shall be for one side hangings of fifteen cubits, and three pillars and as many sockets.

text.NRSV: There shall be fifteen cubits of hangings on the other side, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: and, for the second side-piece, fifteen hangings,—their pillars three, and their sockets three,
text.DRB: And in the other side there shall be hangings of fifteen cubits, with three pillars and as many sockets.

text.NRSV: For the gate of the court there shall be a screen twenty cubits long, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework; it shall have four pillars and with them four bases.
text.Rotherham: And for the gate of the court, a screen of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen, the work of an embroiderer,—their pillars four, and their sockets four.
text.DRB: And in the entrance of the court there shall be made a hanging of twenty cubits of violet and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen, with embroidered work: it shall have four pillars with as many sockets.

text.NRSV: All the pillars around the court shall be banded with silver; their hooks shall be of silver, and their bases of bronze.
text.Rotherham: All the pillars of the court round about, to be joined with cross-rods of silver, and their hooks of silver,—and their sockets of bronze,
text.DRB: All the pillars of the court round about shall be garnished with plates of silver, silver heads and sockets of brass.

text.NRSV: The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits, the width fifty, and the height five cubits, with hangings of fine twisted linen and bases of bronze.
text.Rotherham: The length of the court, a hundred cubits and the breadth fifty throughout and the height five cubits of fine-twined linen,—and their sockets, of bronze.
text.DRB: In length the court shall take up a hundred cubits, in breadth fifty, the height shall be of five cubits, and it shall be made of fine twisted linen, and shall have sockets of brass.

text.NRSV: All the utensils of the tabernacle for every use, and all its pegs and all the pegs of the court, shall be of bronze.
text.Rotherham: All the utensils of the habitation, throughout all the service thereof,—and all the pins thereof, and all the pins of the court, of bronze.
text.DRB: All the vessels of the tabernacle for all uses and ceremonies, and the pins both of it, and of the court, thou shalt make of brass.

text.NRSV: You shall command the Israelites that they bring to you pure beaten olive oil for the light, for the lamp to burn regularly.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore, shalt command the sons of Israel that they bring unto thee—pure, beaten olive-oil, for giving light,—that the lamp may lift up its flame continually.
text.DRB: Command the children of Israel that they bring thee the purest oil of the olives, and beaten with a pestle: that a lamp may burn always,

text.NRSV: In the tent of meeting, outside the curtain that is before the covenant, Aaron and his sons shall tend it from evening to morning before the Lord. It shall be a perpetual ordinance to be observed throughout their generations by the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: In the tent of meeting, outside the veil which is by the testimony, shall Aaron and his sons order it from evening until morning, before Yahweh,—a statute age-abiding, to their generations, from the sons of Israel.
text.DRB: In the tabernacle of the testimony without the veil that hangs before the testimony. And Aaron and his sons shall order it, that it may give light before the Lord until the morning. It shall be a perpetual observance throughout their successions among the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then bring near to you your brother Aaron, and his sons with him, from among the Israelites, to serve me as priests—Aaron and Aaron’s sons, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore bring near unto thee—Aaron thy brother and his sons with him out of the midst of the sons of Israel, that they may minister as priests unto me,—Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar sons of Aaron.
text.DRB: Take unto thee also Aaron thy brother with his sons, from among the children of Israel, that they may minister to me in the priest's office: Aaron, Nadab, and Abiu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

text.NRSV: You shall make sacred vestments for the glorious adornment of your brother Aaron.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make holy garments for Aaron thy brother,—for glory and for beauty.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make a holy vesture for Aaron thy brother for glory and for beauty.

text.NRSV: And you shall speak to all who have ability, whom I have endowed with skill, that they make Aaron’s vestments to consecrate him for my priesthood.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore, shalt speak unto all the wise-hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom,—and they shall make Aaron’s garments, to hallow him for ministering as priest unto me.
text.DRB: And thou shalt speak to all the wise of heart, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they may make Aaron's vestments, in which he being consecrated may minister to me.

text.NRSV: These are the vestments that they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a checkered tunic, a turban, and a sash. When they make these sacred vestments for your brother Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests,
text.Rotherham: These, therefore are the garments which they shall make—a breastpiece, and an ephod and a robe, and a tunic of checker work, a turban and a girdle,—so shall they make holy garments for Aaron thy brother and for his sons, for ministering as priests unto me.
text.DRB: And these shall be the vestments that they shall make: A rational and an ephod, a tunick and a strait linen garment, a mitre and a girdle. They shall make the holy vestments for thy brother Aaron and his sons, that they may do the office of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: they shall use gold, blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine linen.
text.Rotherham: They themselves, therefore, shall take the gold, and the blue and the purple,—and the crimson, and the fine linen.
text.DRB: And they shall take gold, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine linen.

text.NRSV: They shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen, skillfully worked.
text.Rotherham: And they shall make the ephod,—of gold, blue and purple crimson and fine-twined linen, the work of a skilful weaver.
text.DRB: And they shall make the ephod of gold, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen, embroidered with divers colours.

text.NRSV: It shall have two shoulder-pieces attached to its two edges, so that it may be joined together.
text.Rotherham: Two joining shoulderpieces, shall there be to it upon the two extremities thereof that it may be joined together,
text.DRB: It shall have the two edges joined in the top on both sides, that they may be closed together.

text.NRSV: The decorated band on it shall be of the same workmanship and materials, of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: And the curious band for fastening it, that is upon it, like the work thereof of the same, shall it be,—of gold blue and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen,
text.DRB: The very workmanship also and all the variety of the work shall be of gold, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen.

text.NRSV: You shall take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take two onyx stones,—and engrave upon them the names of the sons of Israel:
text.DRB: And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and shalt grave on them the names of the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the remaining six on the other stone, in the order of their birth.
text.Rotherham: six of their names, on the one stone,—and the names of the six that remain, on the second stone according to their births:
text.DRB: Six names on one stone, and the other six on the other, according to the order of their birth.

text.NRSV: As a gem-cutter engraves signets, so you shall engrave the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel; you shall mount them in settings of gold filigree.
text.Rotherham: with the work of a stone engraver, like seal-ring engravings, shalt thou engrave the two stones, after the names of the sons of Israel,—to be encircled with settings of gold, shalt thou make them.
text.DRB: With the work of an engraver and the graving of a jeweller, thou shalt engrave them with the names of the children of Israel, set in gold and compassed about:

text.NRSV: You shall set the two stones on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, as stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel; and Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord on his two shoulders for remembrance.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulderpieces of the ephod, as stones of memorial for the sons of Israel,—so shall Aaron bear their names before Yahweh upon his two shoulders, for a memorial.
text.DRB: And thou shalt put them in both sides of the ephod, a memorial for the children of Israel. And Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord upon both shoulders, for a remembrance.

text.NRSV: You shall make settings of gold filigree,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make ouches of gold;
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also hooks of gold.

text.NRSV: and two chains of pure gold, twisted like cords; and you shall attach the corded chains to the settings.
text.Rotherham: and two chains of pure gold, like cord, shalt thou make them, of wreathen work, and thou shalt put the wreathen chains on the ouches.
text.DRB: And two little chains of the purest gold linked one to another, which thou shalt put into the hooks.

text.NRSV: You shall make a breastpiece of judgment, in skilled work; you shall make it in the style of the ephod; of gold, of blue and purple and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen you shall make it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a breastpiece for giving sentence the work of a skilful weaver, like the work of the ephod, shalt thou make it, of gold, blue, and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen, shalt thou make it.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make the rational of judgment with embroidered work of divers colours, according to the workmanship of the ephod, of gold, violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen.

text.NRSV: It shall be square and doubled, a span in length and a span in width.
text.Rotherham: Four-square, shall it be double,—a span the length thereof and a span the breadth thereof.
text.DRB: It shall be foursquare and doubled: it shall be the measure of a span both in length and in breadth.

text.NRSV: You shall set in it four rows of stones. A row of carnelian, chrysolite, and emerald shall be the first row;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt set therein a setting of stones, four rows of stones,—one row—a sardius, a topaz and an emerald, the first row;
text.DRB: And thou shalt set in it four rows of stones: in the first row shall be a sardius stone, and a topaz, and an emerald:

text.NRSV: and the second row a turquoise, a sapphire, and a moonstone;
text.Rotherham: and the second row a carbuncle a sapphire and a diamond;
text.DRB: In the second a carbuncle, a sapphire and a jasper.

text.NRSV: and the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.Rotherham: and the third row an opal, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.DRB: In the third a ligurius, an agate, and an amethyst:

text.NRSV: and the fourth row a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper; they shall be set in gold filigree.
text.Rotherham: and the fourth row, a Tarshish stone, a sardonyx, and a jasper,—enclosed with gold, shall they be when they are set.
text.DRB: In the fourth a chrysolite, an onyx, and a beryl. They shall be set in gold by their rows.

text.NRSV: There shall be twelve stones with names corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel. They shall be like signets, each engraved with its name, for the twelve tribes.
text.Rotherham: And the stones shall be after the names of the sons of Israel—twelve, after their names,—as the engravings of a seal-ring everyone after his name, shall they be, for the twelve tribes,
text.DRB: And they shall have the names of the children of Israel: with twelve names shall they be engraved, each stone with the name of one according to the twelve tribes.

text.NRSV: You shall make for the breastpiece chains of pure gold, twisted like cords;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make, upon the breastpiece, chains like cords of wreathen work,—of pure gold.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make on the rational chains linked one to another of the purest gold:

text.NRSV: and you shall make for the breastpiece two rings of gold, and put the two rings on the two edges of the breastpiece.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make, upon the breastpiece, two rings of gold,—and thou shalt place the two rings, upon the two ends of the breastpiece.
text.DRB: And two rings of gold, which thou shalt put in the two ends at the top of the rational.

text.NRSV: You shall put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt hang the two wreathen chains of gold upon the two rings,—upon the ends of the breastpiece;
text.DRB: And the golden chains thou shalt join to the rings, that are in the ends thereof:

text.NRSV: the two ends of the two cords you shall attach to the two settings, and so attach it in front to the shoulder-pieces of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: and the other two ends of the two wreathen chains, shalt thou fasten upon the two ouches, so shalt thou hang them upon the shoulderpieces of the ephod, in the forefront thereof.
text.DRB: And the ends of the chains themselves thou shalt join together with two hooks on both sides of the ephod, which is towards the rational.

text.NRSV: You shall make two rings of gold, and put them at the two ends of the breastpiece, on its inside edge next to the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and put them on the two ends of the breastpiece upon the border thereof, which is on the side of the ephod inwards.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also two rings of gold which thou shalt put in the top parts of the rational, in the borders that are over against the ephod, and look towards the back parts thereof.

text.NRSV: You shall make two rings of gold, and attach them in front to the lower part of the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod, at its joining above the decorated band of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and place them upon the two shoulderpieces of the ephod beneath, on the front of the face thereof, over against its joining, above the curious band of the ephod:
text.DRB: Moreover also other two rings of gold, which are to be set on each side of the ephod beneath, that looketh towards the nether joining, that the rational may be fitted with the ephod,

text.NRSV: They shall bind the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord, so that it may lie on the decorated band of the ephod, and so that the breastpiece shall not come loose from the ephod.
text.Rotherham: that they may bind the breastpiece by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue, so that it may remain upon the curious band of the ephod, and the breastpiece not be removed from of the ephod.
text.DRB: And may be fastened by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a violet fillet, that the joining artificially wrought may continue, and the rational and the ephod may not be loosed one from the other.

text.NRSV: So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart whenever he goes into the holy place, for a continual remembrance before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall Aaron bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece for giving sentence upon his heart when he goeth into the holy place,—for a memorial before Yahweh continually.
text.DRB: And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the rational of judgment upon his breast, when he shall enter into the sanctuary, a memorial before the Lord for ever.

text.NRSV: In the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be on Aaron’s heart when he goes in before the Lord; thus Aaron shall bear the judgment of the Israelites on his heart before the Lord continually.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou place, in the breastpiece for giving sentence, the lights and the perfections, so shall they be upon the heart of Aaron when he goeth in before Yahweh,—so shall Aaron bear the sentence of the sons of Israel, upon his heart before Yahweh continually.
text.DRB: And thou shalt put in the rational of judgment doctrine and truth , which shall be on Aaron's breast, when he shall go in before the Lord: and he shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel on his breast, in the sight of the Lord always.

text.NRSV: You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the robe of the ephod wholly of blue;
text.DRB: And thou shalt make the tunick of the ephod all of violet,

text.NRSV: It shall have an opening for the head in the middle of it, with a woven binding around the opening, like the opening in a coat of mail, so that it may not be torn.
text.Rotherham: and the opening for his head shall be in the midst thereof,—a border, shall there be to the opening thereof round about the work of a weaver, like the opening of a coat of mail, shall there be to it, it must not be rent.
text.DRB: In the midst whereof above shall be a hole for the head, and a border round about it woven, as is wont to be made in the outmost parts of garments, that it may not easily be broken.

text.NRSV: On its lower hem you shall make pomegranates of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, all around the lower hem, with bells of gold between them all around—
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make upon the skirts thereof pomegranates of blue and purple and crimson, upon the skirts thereof round about—and bells of gold in the midst of them round about:
text.DRB: And beneath at the feet of the same tunick round about, thou shalt make as it were pomegranates, of violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, with little bells set between:

text.NRSV: a golden bell and a pomegranate alternating all around the lower hem of the robe.
text.Rotherham: a bell of gold and a pomegranate, a bell of gold, and a pomegranate,—upon the skirts of the robe, round about.
text.DRB: So that there shall be a golden bell and a pomegranate, and again another golden bell and a pomegranate.

text.NRSV: Aaron shall wear it when he ministers, and its sound shall be heard when he goes into the holy place before the Lord, and when he comes out, so that he may not die.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be upon Aaron, for ministering,—so shall be heard the sound of him—when he goeth into the holy place, before Yahweh, and cometh out, and dieth not.
text.DRB: And Aaron shall be vested with it in the office of his ministry, that the sound may be heard, when he goeth in and cometh out of the sanctuary, in the sight of the Lord, and that he may not die.

text.NRSV: You shall make a rosette of pure gold, and engrave on it, like the engraving of a signet, “Holy to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a burnished plate, of pure gold,—and shalt engrave thereupon, like the engravings of a seal-ring, Holiness to Yahweh.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a plate of the purest gold: wherein thou shalt grave with engraver's work, Holy to the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall fasten it on the turban with a blue cord; it shall be on the front of the turban.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put it upon a cord of blue, so shall it be upon the turban,—upon the forefront of the turban, shall it be.
text.DRB: And thou shalt tie it with a violet fillet, and it shall be upon the mitre,

text.NRSV: It shall be on Aaron’s forehead, and Aaron shall take on himself any guilt incurred in the holy offering that the Israelites consecrate as their sacred donations; it shall always be on his forehead, in order that they may find favor before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be upon the forehead of Aaron, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the hallowed things, which the sons of Israel shall hallow, for all their hallowed gift,—and it shall be upon his forehead continually, that there may be acceptance for them before Yahweh.
text.DRB: Hanging over the forehead of the high priest. And Aaron shall bear the iniquities of those things, which the children of Israel have offered and sanctified, in all their gifts and offerings. And the plate shall be always on his forehead, that the Lord may be well pleased with them.

text.NRSV: You shall make the checkered tunic of fine linen, and you shall make a turban of fine linen, and you shall make a sash embroidered with needlework.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt weave, in checker work the tunic of fine linen, and shalt make a turban of fine linen,—and, a girdle, shalt thou make the work of an embroiderer.
text.DRB: And thou shalt gird the tunick with fine linen, and thou shalt make a fine linen mitre, and a girdle of embroidered work.

text.NRSV: For Aaron’s sons you shall make tunics and sashes and headdresses; you shall make them for their glorious adornment.
text.Rotherham: And, for the sons of Aaron, shalt thou make tunics, and shalt make for them girdles,—and caps, shalt thou make for them, for glory and for beauty.
text.DRB: Moreover for the sons of Aaron thou shalt prepare linen tunicks, and girdles and mitres for glory and beauty:

text.NRSV: You shall put them on your brother Aaron, and on his sons with him; and you shall anoint them and ordain them and consecrate them, so that they may serve me as priests.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt clothe therewith Aaron thy brother, and his sons, with him,—and shalt anoint them and install them, and hallow them, so shall they minister as priests unto me.
text.DRB: And with all these things thou shalt vest Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him. And thou shalt consecrate the hands of them all, and shalt sanctify them, that they may do the office of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: You shall make for them linen undergarments to cover their naked flesh; they shall reach from the hips to the thighs;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make for them breeches of linen, to cover their unseemliness, from the loins even unto the thighs, shall they be;
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also linen breeches, to cover the flesh of their nakedness from the reins to the thighs:

text.NRSV: Aaron and his sons shall wear them when they go into the tent of meeting, or when they come near the altar to minister in the holy place; or they will bring guilt on themselves and die. This shall be a perpetual ordinance for him and for his descendants after him.
text.Rotherham: so shall they be upon Aaron and upon his sons, when they go into the tent of meeting, or when they approach unto the altar, to minister in the holy place, lest they bear iniquity, and die,—a statute age-abiding, to him and to his seed after him.
text.DRB: And Aaron and his sons shall use them when they shall go in to the tabernacle of the testimony, or when they approach the altar to minister in the sanctuary, lest being guilty of iniquity they die. It shall be a law for ever to Aaron, and to his seed after him.

text.NRSV: Now this is what you shall do to them to consecrate them, to serve me as priests. Take one young bull and two rams without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the thing which thou shalt do to them to hallow them, that they may minister as priests unto me,—Take one young bullock and two rams without defect;
text.DRB: And thou shalt also do this, that they may be consecrated to me in priesthood. Take a calf from the herd, and two rams without blemish,

text.NRSV: and unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers spread with oil. You shall make them of choice wheat flour.
text.Rotherham: and bread unleavened and cakes unleavened with oil poured over, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil,—of fine wheaten meal, shalt thou make them;
text.DRB: And unleavened bread, and a cake without leaven, tempered with oil, wafers also unleavened anointed with oil: thou shalt make them all of wheaten flour.

text.NRSV: You shall put them in one basket and bring them in the basket, and bring the bull and the two rams.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place them in one basket, and bring them near in the basket,—and the bullock, and the two rams.
text.DRB: And thou shalt put them in a basket and offer them: and the calf and the two rams.

text.NRSV: You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and wash them with water.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron and his sons, shalt thou bring near, unto the opening of the tent of meeting,—and shalt bathe them in water.
text.DRB: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tabernacle of the testimony. And when thou hast washed the father and his sons with water,

text.NRSV: Then you shall take the vestments, and put on Aaron the tunic and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastpiece, and gird him with the decorated band of the ephod;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take the garments and clothe Aaron—with the tunic, and with the robe of the ephod, and with the ephod, and with the breastpiece,—and shalt gird him with the curious band of the ephod;
text.DRB: Thou shalt clothe Aaron with his vestments, that is, with the linen garment and the tunick, and the ephod and the rational, which thou shalt gird with the girdle.

text.NRSV: and you shall set the turban on his head, and put the holy diadem on the turban.
text.Rotherham: and shalt put the turban upon his head,—and place the holy crown upon the turban.
text.DRB: And thou shalt put the mitre upon his head, and the holy plate upon the mitre,

text.NRSV: You shall take the anointing oil, and pour it on his head and anoint him.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour upon his head,—so shalt thou anoint him.
text.DRB: And thou shalt pour the oil of unction upon his head: and by this rite shall he be consecrated.

text.NRSV: Then you shall bring his sons, and put tunics on them,
text.Rotherham: His sons also, shalt thou bring near,—and clothe them with tunics;
text.DRB: Thou shalt bring his sons also and shalt put on them the linen tunicks, and gird them with a girdle:

text.NRSV: and you shall gird them with sashes and tie headdresses on them; and the priesthood shall be theirs by a perpetual ordinance. You shall then ordain Aaron and his sons.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt gird them with girdles, Aaron and his sons, and twist round for them caps, so shall they have priesthood by a statute age-abiding,—so shalt thou install, Aaron and his sons.
text.DRB: To wit, Aaron and his children, and thou shalt put mitres upon them: and they shall be priests to me by a perpetual ordinance. After thou shalt have consecrated their hands,

text.NRSV: You shall bring the bull in front of the tent of meeting. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt bring near the bullock, before the tent of meeting,—and Aaron and his sons shall lean their hands upon the head of the bullock;
text.DRB: Thou shalt present also the calf before the tabernacle of the testimony. And Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon his head,

text.NRSV: and you shall slaughter the bull before the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou slay the bullock before Yahweh,—at the opening of the tent of meeting:
text.DRB: And thou shalt kill him in the sight of the Lord, beside the door of the tabernacle of the testimony.

text.NRSV: and shall take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger, and you shall pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and place upon the horns of the altar, with thy finger,—and all the remaining blood, shalt thou pour out at the foundation of the altar;
text.DRB: And taking some of the blood of the calf, thou shalt put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger, and the rest of the blood thou shalt pour at the bottom thereof.

text.NRSV: You shall take all the fat that covers the entrails, and the appendage of the liver, and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and turn them into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inner part, and the caul upon the liver, and the two kidneys, with the fat which is upon them, and burn as incense on the altar:
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also all the fat that covereth the entrails, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and shalt offer a burnt offering upon the altar:

text.NRSV: But the flesh of the bull, and its skin, and its dung, you shall burn with fire outside the camp; it is a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: but the flesh of the bullock and its skin, and its dung, shalt thou burn up with fire, outside the camp,—a sin-bearer, it is.
text.DRB: But the flesh of the calf and the hide and the dung, thou shalt burn abroad, without the camp, because it is for sin.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take one of the rams, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: One ram also, shalt thou take, and Aaron and his sons shall lean their hands, upon the head of the ram;
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also one ram upon the head whereof Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands.

text.NRSV: and you shall slaughter the ram, and shall take its blood and dash it against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou slay the ram,—and take its blood, and dash upon the altar round about;
text.DRB: And when thou hast killed him, thou shalt take of the blood thereof, and pour round about the altar:

text.NRSV: Then you shall cut the ram into its parts, and wash its entrails and its legs, and put them with its parts and its head,
text.Rotherham: but the ram itself, shalt thou cut up into its pieces,—and shalt bathe its inner part, and its legs, and lay upon its pieces and upon its head;
text.DRB: And thou shalt cut the ram in pieces, and having washed his entrails and feet, thou shalt put them upon the flesh that is cut in pieces, and upon his head.

text.NRSV: and turn the whole ram into smoke on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the Lord; it is a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and shalt burn the whole ram as incense on the altar, an ascending-sacrifice, it is to Yahweh,—a satisfying odour, an altar-flame to Yahweh, it is.
text.DRB: And thou shalt offer the whole ram for a burnt offering upon the altar: it is an oblation to the Lord, a most sweet savour of the victim of the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take the second ram,—and Aaron and his sons shall lean their hands upon the head of the ram;
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also the other ram, upon whose head Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands.

text.NRSV: and you shall slaughter the ram, and take some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear and on the lobes of his sons’ right ears, and on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet, and dash the rest of the blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou slay the ram, and take of its blood, and place upon the tip of the ear of Aaron and upon the tip of the ear of his sons—the right ear, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot,—and shalt dash the blood upon the altar round about.
text.DRB: And when thou hast sacrificed him, thou shalt take of his blood, and put upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron and of his sons, and upon the thumbs and great toes of their right hand and foot, and thou shalt pour the blood upon the altar round about.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take some of the blood that is on the altar, and some of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it on Aaron and his vestments and on his sons and his sons’ vestments with him; then he and his vestments shall be holy, as well as his sons and his sons’ vestments.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take of the blood that is upon the altar and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle upon Aaron and upon his garments, and upon his sons and upon the garments of his sons with him,—so shall he be hallowed—he, and his garments, and his sons and the garments of his sons, with him.
text.DRB: And when thou hast taken of the blood, that is upon the altar, and of the oil of unction, thou shalt sprinkle Aaron and his vesture, his sons and their vestments. And after they and their vestments are consecrated,

text.NRSV: You shall also take the fat of the ram, the fat tail, the fat that covers the entrails, the appendage of the liver, the two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and the right thigh (for it is a ram of ordination),
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take from the ram—the fat and the fat-tail and the fat that covereth the inner part and the caul of the liver and the two kidneys with the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder,—for, a ram of installation, it is,—
text.DRB: Thou shalt take the fat of the ram, and the rump, and the fat that covereth the lungs, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder, because it is the ram of consecration.

text.NRSV: and one loaf of bread, one cake of bread made with oil, and one wafer, out of the basket of unleavened bread that is before the Lord;
text.Rotherham: and one round-cake of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer,—out of the basket of unleavened cakes, which is before Yahweh;
text.DRB: And one roll of bread, a cake tempered with oil, a wafer out of the basket of unleavened bread, which is set in the sight of the Lord.

text.NRSV: and you shall place all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons, and raise them as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and shalt lay the whole upon the heads of Aaron, and upon the hands of his sons,—then shalt thou wave them to and fro as a wave-offering, before Yahweh;
text.DRB: And thou shalt put all upon the hands of Aaron and of his sons, and shalt sanctify them elevating before the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take them from their hands, and turn them into smoke on the altar in addition to the burnt offering, as a pleasing odor before the Lord; it is an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and shalt take them from their hand, and burn them as incense on the altar upon the ascending-sacrifice,—as a satisfying odour, before Yahweh, an altar-flame, it is to Yahweh.
text.DRB: And thou shalt take all from their hands, and shalt burn them upon the altar for a holocaust, a most sweet savour in the sight of the Lord, because it is his oblation.

text.NRSV: You shall take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s ordination and raise it as an elevation offering before the Lord; and it shall be your portion.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take the breast from the ram of installation which is for Aaron, and shalt wave it as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—so shall it be thy share,
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also the breast of the ram, wherewith Aaron was consecrated, and elevating it thou shalt sanctify it before the Lord, and it shall fall to thy share.

text.NRSV: You shall consecrate the breast that was raised as an elevation offering and the thigh that was raised as an elevation offering from the ram of ordination, from that which belonged to Aaron and his sons.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt hallow the breast of the wave-offering, and the shoulder of the heave-offering, which is waved to and fro, and which is heaved up, from the ram of installation, from that which is for Aaron, and from that which is for his sons;
text.DRB: And thou shalt sanctify both the consecrated breast, and the shoulder that thou didst separate of the ram,

text.NRSV: These things shall be a perpetual ordinance for Aaron and his sons from the Israelites, for this is an offering; and it shall be an offering by the Israelites from their sacrifices of well-being, their offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: so shall it belong to Aaron and to his sons for an age-abiding statute from the sons of Israel, for a heave-offering, it is,—and, a heave-offering, shall it remain, from the sons of Israel out of their peace-offerings, their heave-offering to Yahweh.
text.DRB: Wherewith Aaron was consecrated and his sons, and they shall fall to Aaron's share and his sons' by a perpetual right from the children of Israel: because they are the choicest and the beginnings of their peace victims which they offer to the Lord.

text.NRSV: The sacred vestments of Aaron shall be passed on to his sons after him; they shall be anointed in them and ordained in them.
text.Rotherham: And, the holy garments which belong to Aaron, shall belong to his sons, after him,-to be anointed therein, and to be installed therein,
text.DRB: And the holy vesture, which Aaron shall use, his sons shall have after him, that they may be anointed, and their hands consecrated to it.

text.NRSV: The son who is priest in his place shall wear them seven days, when he comes into the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shall they be put on by him from among his sons who is priest in his stead,—by him who goeth into the tent of meeting, to minister in the holy place.
text.DRB: He of his sons that shall be appointed high priest in his stead, and that shall enter into the tabernacle of the testimony to minister in the sanctuary, shall wear it seven days.

text.NRSV: You shall take the ram of ordination, and boil its flesh in a holy place;
text.Rotherham: And, the ram of installation, shalt thou take,—and shalt boil its flesh, in a holy place;
text.DRB: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and shalt boil the flesh thereof in the holy place:

text.NRSV: and Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: and Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket,—at the opening of the tent of meeting:
text.DRB: And Aaron and his sons shall eat it. The loaves also, that are in the basket, they shall eat in the entry of the tabernacle of the testimony,

text.NRSV: They themselves shall eat the food by which atonement is made, to ordain and consecrate them, but no one else shall eat of them, because they are holy.
text.Rotherham: thus shall they eat those things wherewith a propitiatory-covering hath been made, to install them, to hallow them, but, a stranger, shall not eat, for holy, they are.
text.DRB: That it may be an atoning sacrifice, and the hands of the offerers may be sanctified. A stranger shall not eat of them, because they are holy.

text.NRSV: If any of the flesh for the ordination, or of the bread, remains until the morning, you shall burn the remainder with fire; it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
text.Rotherham: And, if there be left over of the flesh of installation, or of the bread until the morning, then shalt thou burn up what is left over, in the fire, it shall not be eaten for holy, it is.
text.DRB: And if there remain of the consecrated flesh, or of the bread till the morning, thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: they shall not be eaten, because they are sanctified.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons, just as I have commanded you; through seven days you shall ordain them.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt do for Aaron and for his sons, in this manner, according to all which I have commanded, thee,—seven days, shalt thou install them.
text.DRB: All that I have commanded thee, thou shalt do unto Aaron and his sons. Seven days shalt thou consecrate their hands:

text.NRSV: Also every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Also you shall offer a sin offering for the altar, when you make atonement for it, and shall anoint it, to consecrate it.
text.Rotherham: And a sin-bearing bullock, shalt thou offer daily, for them who are to be covered by propitiation, and shalt make a sin-cleansing for the altar, when thou puttest a propitiatory-covering thereon,—and shalt anoint it to hallow it.
text.DRB: And thou shalt offer a calf for sin every day for expiation. And thou shalt cleanse the altar when thou hast offered the victim of expiation, and shalt anoint it to sanctify it.

text.NRSV: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar, and consecrate it, and the altar shall be most holy; whatever touches the altar shall become holy.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shalt thou put a propitiatory-covering over the altar, and so shalt hallow it, thus shall the altar become most holy; whosoever toucheth the altar, must be holy.
text.DRB: Seven days shalt thou expiate the altar and sanctify it, and it shall be most holy. Every one that shall touch it shall be holy.

text.NRSV: Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old regularly each day.
text.Rotherham: This, moreover, is what thou shalt offer upon the altar, two lambs of a year old day by day, continually:
text.DRB: This is what thou shalt sacrifice upon the altar: Two lambs of a year old every day continually.

text.NRSV: One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer in the evening;
text.Rotherham: The one lamb, shalt thou offer in the morning, and the second lamb, shalt thou offer between the evenings;
text.DRB: One lamb in the morning and another in the evening.

text.NRSV: and with the first lamb one-tenth of a measure of choice flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil, and one-fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering.
text.Rotherham: and a tenth part of fine meal mingled with a fourth part of the hin of beaten oil, and, as a drink-offering, a fourth part of the hin of wine,—with the one lamb.
text.DRB: With one lamb a tenth part of flour tempered with beaten oil, of the fourth part of a hin, and wine for libation of the same measure.

text.NRSV: And the other lamb you shall offer in the evening, and shall offer with it a grain offering and its drink offering, as in the morning, for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the second lamb, shalt thou offer between the evenings,-according to the meal-offering of the morning, and according to the drink-offering thereof, shalt thou offer with it, for a satisfying odour, an altar-flame, to Yahweh:
text.DRB: And the other lamb thou shalt offer in the evening, according to the rite of the morning oblation, and according to what we have said, for a savour of sweetness:

text.NRSV: It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the Lord, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.
text.Rotherham: a continual ascending-sacrifice to your generations, at the opening of the tent of meeting, before Yahweh, where will meet with you, to speak unto thee there.
text.DRB: It is a sacrifice to the Lord, by perpetual oblation unto your generations, at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony before the Lord, where I will appoint to speak unto thee.

text.NRSV: I will meet with the Israelites there, and it shall be sanctified by my glory;
text.Rotherham: So will I meet there with the sons of Israel, and it shall be hallowed by my glory.
text.DRB: And there will I command the children of Israel, and the altar shall be sanctified by my glory.

text.NRSV: I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; Aaron also and his sons I will consecrate, to serve me as priests.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I hallow the tent of meeting, and the altar,—and, Aaron and his sons, will I hallow, to minister as priests unto me.
text.DRB: I will sanctify also the tabernacle of the testimony with the altar, and Aaron with his sons, to do the office of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: I will dwell among the Israelites, and will be their God.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I make my habitation in the midst of the sons of Israel, and will be their God;
text.DRB: And I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel, and will be their God:

text.NRSV: And they shall know that I am the Lord their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt that I might dwell among them; I am the Lord their God.
text.Rotherham: and they shall know that, I—Yahweh, am their God, who brought them forth out of the land of Egypt that I might make my habitation in their midst, I—Yahweh, their God.
text.DRB: And they shall know that I am the Lord their God, who have brought them out of the land of Egypt, that I might abide among them, I the Lord their God.

text.NRSV: You shall make an altar to burn incense upon; of acacia wood you shall make it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make an altar, for burning incense, of acacia wood, shalt thou make it:
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also an altar to burn incense , of setim wood.

text.NRSV: It shall be square, a cubit long and a cubit wide, and two cubits high; its horns shall be of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: a cubit, the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof four square, shall it be,—and two cubits, the height thereof, of the same, [shall be] the horns thereof,
text.DRB: It shall be a cubit in length, and another in breadth, that is, foursquare, and two in height. Horns shall go out of the same.

text.NRSV: You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top, and its sides all around and its horns; and you shall make for it a molding of gold all around.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof,-and thou shalt make to it a border of gold round about.
text.DRB: And thou shalt overlay it with the purest gold, as well as the grate thereof, as the walls round about and the horns. And thou shalt make to it a crown of gold round about,

text.NRSV: And you shall make two golden rings for it; under its molding on two opposite sides of it you shall make them, and they shall hold the poles for carrying it.
text.Rotherham: And, two rings of gold, shalt thou make to it, under the border thereof upon the two corners thereof, shalt thou make them on the two sides thereof,—so shall they become receptacles for the staves, to lift it therewith.
text.DRB: And two golden rings under the crown on either side, that the bars may be put into them, and the altar be carried.

text.NRSV: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the staves of acacia wood,—and overlay them with gold.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make the bars also of setim wood, and shalt overlay them with gold.

text.NRSV: You shall place it in front of the curtain that is above the ark of the covenant, in front of the mercy seat that is over the covenant, where I will meet with you.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place it before the veil, which is by the ark of the testimony,—before the propitiatory which is upon the testimony, where I will meet thee.
text.DRB: And thou shalt set the altar over against the veil, that hangeth before the ark of the testimony before the propitiatory wherewith the testimony is covered, where I will speak to thee.

text.NRSV: Aaron shall offer fragrant incense on it; every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall offer it,
text.Rotherham: And Aaron shall burn thereon fragrant incense,—morning by morning, when he trimmeth the lamps, shall he burn it;
text.DRB: And Aaron shall burn sweet smelling incense upon it in the morning. When he shall dress the lamps, he shall burn it:

text.NRSV: and when Aaron sets up the lamps in the evening he shall offer it, a regular incense offering before the Lord throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: and, when Aaron lighteth the lamps, between the evenings, shall he burn it,—a continual incense before Yahweh, to your generations.
text.DRB: And when he shall place them in the evening, he shall burn an everlasting incense before the Lord throughout your generations.

text.NRSV: You shall not offer unholy incense on it, or a burnt offering, or a grain offering; and you shall not pour a drink offering on it.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not cause to ascend thereupon strange incense, nor an ascending-sacrifice, nor a meal-offering,—and a drink offering, shall ye not pour out thereupon.
text.DRB: You shall not offer upon it incense of another composition nor oblation, and victim, neither shall you offer libations.

text.NRSV: Once a year Aaron shall perform the rite of atonement on its horns. Throughout your generations he shall perform the atonement for it once a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonement. It is most holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But Aaron shall put a propitiatory-covering upon the horns thereof, once in the year: of the blood of the sin-bearer for propitiatory-coverings, once in the year, shall he put a propitiatory-covering thereupon to your generations, most holy, it is to Yahweh.
text.DRB: And Aaron shall pray upon the horns thereof once a year, with the blood of that which was offered for sin, and shall make atonement upon it in your generations. It shall be most holy to the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: When you take a census of the Israelites to register them, at registration all of them shall give a ransom for their lives to the Lord, so that no plague may come upon them for being registered.
text.Rotherham: When thou takest the sum of the sons of Israel, by their numberings, then shall they give every man a propitiatory-covering for his soul to Yahweh, when they are numbered,—that there may be among them no plague when they are numbered.
text.DRB: When thou shalt take the sum of the children of Israel according to their number, every one of them shall give a price for their souls to the Lord, and there shall be no scourge among them, when they shall be reckoned.

text.NRSV: This is what each one who is registered shall give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs), half a shekel as an offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: This, shall they give—every one that passeth over to them that have been numbered—a half-shekel by the shekel of the sanctuary,—the shekel is twenty gerahs—the half-shekel, shall be a heave-offering to Yahweh.
text.DRB: And this shall every one give that passeth at the naming, half a sicle according to the standard of the temple. A sicle hath twenty obols. Half a sicle shall be offered to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Each one who is registered, from twenty years old and upward, shall give the Lord’s offering.
text.Rotherham: All who pass over to the numbered, from twenty years old and upwards, shall give the heave-offering of Yahweh.
text.DRB: He that is counted in the number from twenty years and upwards, shall give the price.

text.NRSV: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less, than the half shekel, when you bring this offering to the Lord to make atonement for your lives.
text.Rotherham: The rich, shall not give more, and the poor, shall not give less, than the half-shekel,—when they give the heave-offering of Yahweh, to put a propitiatory-covering over your souls.
text.DRB: The rich man shall not add to half a sicle, and the poor man shall diminish nothing.

text.NRSV: You shall take the atonement money from the Israelites and shall designate it for the service of the tent of meeting; before the Lord it will be a reminder to the Israelites of the ransom given for your lives.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt take the silver for the propitiatory-coverings from the sons of Israel, and shalt expend it upon the service of the tent of meeting,—thus shall it be for the sons of Israel as a memorial before Yahweh, to put a propitiatory-covering over your souls.
text.DRB: And the money received which was contributed by the children of Israel, thou shalt deliver unto the uses of the tabernacle of the testimony, that it may be a memorial of them before the Lord, and he may be merciful to their souls.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: You shall make a basin of bronze, with a stand of bronze, for washing. You shall put it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and you shall put water in it;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a laver of bronze, and its stand of bronze for bathing,—and thou shalt place it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and shalt put therein water.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a brazen laver with its foot, to wash in: and thou shalt set it between the tabernacle of the testimony and the altar. And water being put into it,

text.NRSV: with the water Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet.
text.Rotherham: So shall Aaron and his sons bathe thereat,—their hands, and their feet.
text.DRB: Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and feet in it:

text.NRSV: When they go into the tent of meeting, or when they come near the altar to minister, to make an offering by fire to the Lord, they shall wash with water, so that they may not die.
text.Rotherham: When they enter the tent of meeting, they shall bathe with water so shall they not die,—or when they approach unto the altar to minister, to burn as incense an altar-flame to Yahweh:
text.DRB: When they are going into the tabernacle of the testimony, and when they are to come to the altar, to offer on it incense to the Lord,

text.NRSV: They shall wash their hands and their feet, so that they may not die: it shall be a perpetual ordinance for them, for him and his descendants throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: so then they shall bathe their hands and their feet and shall not die,—and it shall be to them an age-abiding statute, to him and to his seed to their generations.
text.DRB: Lest perhaps they die. It shall be an everlasting law to him, and to his seed by successions.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses,

text.NRSV: Take the finest spices: of liquid myrrh five hundred shekels, and of sweet-smelling cinnamon half as much, that is, two hundred fifty, and two hundred fifty of aromatic cane,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, take to thee—principal spices,—self-flowing myrrh, five hundred, and fragrant cinnamon, half as much, two hundred and fifty, and, fragrant cane, two hundred and fifty;
text.DRB: Saying: Take spices, of principal and chosen myrrh five hundred sicles, and of cinnamon half so much, that is, two hundred and fifty sicles, of calamus in like manner two hundred and fifty.

text.NRSV: and five hundred of cassia—measured by the sanctuary shekel—and a hin of olive oil;
text.Rotherham: and, cassia, five hundred, by the shekel of the sanctuary,—and, oil olive, a hin.
text.DRB: And of cassia five hundred sicles by the weight of the sanctuary, of oil of olives the measure hin:

text.NRSV: and you shall make of these a sacred anointing oil blended as by the perfumer; it shall be a holy anointing oil.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make it an oil for holy anointing, a compounded perfume the work of a perfumer,—an oil for holy anointing, shall it be.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make the holy oil of unction, an ointment compounded after the art of the perfumer,

text.NRSV: With it you shall anoint the tent of meeting and the ark of the covenant,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt anoint therewith the tent of meeting,—and the ark of the testimony;
text.DRB: And therewith thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the testimony, and the ark of the testament,

text.NRSV: and the table and all its utensils, and the lampstand and its utensils, and the altar of incense,
text.Rotherham: and the table and all its utensils, and the lampstand, and its utensils,—and the incense altar,
text.DRB: And the table with the vessels thereof, the candlestick and furniture thereof, the altars of incense,

text.NRSV: and the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the basin with its stand;
text.Rotherham: and the altar for the ascending-sacrifice, and all its utensils,-and the laver, and its stand.
text.DRB: And of holocaust, and all the furniture that belongeth to the service of them.

text.NRSV: you shall consecrate them, so that they may be most holy; whatever touches them will become holy.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou hallow them, and they shall be most holy,—whosoever toucheth them, shall be holy.
text.DRB: And thou shalt sanctify all, and they shall be most holy: he that shall touch them shall be sanctified.

text.NRSV: You shall anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, in order that they may serve me as priests.
text.Rotherham: Aaron also, and his sons, shalt thou anoint,—so shalt thou hallow them to minister as priests unto me.
text.DRB: Thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and shalt sanctify them, that they may do the office of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: You shall say to the Israelites, “This shall be my holy anointing oil throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: And unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak, saying, An oil for holy anointing, shall this be to me, to your generations.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to the children of Israel: This oil of unction shall be holy unto me throughout your generations.

text.NRSV: It shall not be used in any ordinary anointing of the body, and you shall make no other like it in composition; it is holy, and it shall be holy to you.
text.Rotherham: Upon the flesh of a common man, shall it not be poured, and according to the proportions thereof, shall ye not make any like it,—holy, it is, holy, shall it be to you.
text.DRB: The flesh of man shall not be anointed therewith, and you shall make none other of the same composition, because it is sanctified, and shall be holy unto you.

text.NRSV: Whoever compounds any like it or whoever puts any of it on an unqualified person shall be cut off from the people.”
text.Rotherham: Whosoever compoundeth any like it, and whosoever bestoweth thereof upon a stranger, shall be cut off from among his people.
text.DRB: What man soever shall compound such, and shall give thereof to a stranger, he shall be cut off from his people.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Take sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum, sweet spices with pure frankincense (an equal part of each),
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Take to thee aromatic spices stacte and onycha, and galbanum,—aromatic spices, and pure frankincense,—weight for weight, shall it be.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Take unto thee spices, stacte, and onycha, galbanum of sweet savour, and the clearest frankincense, all shall be of equal weight.

text.NRSV: and make an incense blended as by the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make of it an incense, a perfume the work of a perfumer,—salted, pure, holy.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make incense compounded by the work of the perfumer, well tempered together, and pure, and most worthy of sanctification.

text.NRSV: and you shall beat some of it into powder, and put part of it before the covenant in the tent of meeting where I shall meet with you; it shall be for you most holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt beat some of it to powder, and place thereof before the testimony, in the tent of meeting, where I am to meet with thee,—most holy, shall it be to you.
text.DRB: And when thou has beaten all into very small powder, thou shalt set of it before the tabernacle of the testimony, in the place where I will appear to thee. Most holy shall this incense be to you.

text.NRSV: When you make incense according to this composition, you shall not make it for yourselves; it shall be regarded by you as holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the incense which thou shalt make, according to the proportions thereof, ye shall not make for yourselves,—holy, shall it be to thee, to Yahweh.
text.DRB: You shall not make such a composition for your own uses, because it is holy to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Whoever makes any like it to use as perfume shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever shall make like it, to smell of it shall be cut off from among his people.
text.DRB: What man soever shall make the like, to enjoy the smell thereof, he shall perish out of his people.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: See, I have called by name Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah:
text.Rotherham: See, I have called by name, Bezaleel, son of Uri, son of Hur of the tribe of Judah;
text.DRB: Behold, I have called by name Beseleel the son of Uri the son of Hur of the tribe of Juda,

text.NRSV: and I have filled him with divine spirit, with ability, intelligence, and knowledge in every kind of craft,
text.Rotherham: and have filled him with the Spirit of God, in wisdom and in understanding, and in knowledge and in all manner of workmanship;
text.DRB: And I have filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom and understanding, and knowledge in all manner of work.

text.NRSV: to devise artistic designs, to work in gold, silver, and bronze,
text.Rotherham: to devise skilful designs, to work in gold and in silver, and in bronze;
text.DRB: To devise whatsoever may be artificially made of gold, and silver, and brass,

text.NRSV: in cutting stones for setting, and in carving wood, in every kind of craft.
text.Rotherham: and in the cutting of stones for setting, and in the carving of wood,—to work in all manner of workmanship,
text.DRB: Of marble, and precious stones, and variety of wood.

text.NRSV: Moreover, I have appointed with him Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan; and I have given skill to all the skillful, so that they may make all that I have commanded you:
text.Rotherham: I myself, therefore, lo! I have given with him Oholiab, son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan, and in the heart of every one that is wise-hearted, have I put wisdom,—so shall they make all that I have commanded thee—
text.DRB: And I have given him for his companion Ooliab the son of Achisamech of the tribe of Dan. And I have put wisdom in the heart of every skillful man, that they may make all things which I have commanded thee,

text.NRSV: the tent of meeting, and the ark of covenant law, and the mercy seat that is on it, and all the furnishings of the tent,
text.Rotherham: the tent of meeting, and the ark of the testimony; and the propitiatory which is thereupon, and all the utensils of the tent;
text.DRB: The tabernacle of the covenant, and the ark of the testimony, and the propitiatory that is over it, and all the vessels of the tabernacle,

text.NRSV: the table and its utensils, and the pure lampstand with all its utensils, and the altar of incense,
text.Rotherham: and the table, and its utensils, and the pure lampstand, and all its utensils,—and the incense altar;
text.DRB: And the table and the vessels thereof, the most pure candlestick with the vessels thereof, and the altars of incense,

text.NRSV: and the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the basin with its stand,
text.Rotherham: and the altar for the ascending-sacrifice, and all its utensils, and the laver, and its stand;
text.DRB: And of holocaust, and all their vessels, the laver with its foot,

text.NRSV: and the finely worked vestments, the holy vestments for the priest Aaron and the vestments of his sons, for their service as priests,
text.Rotherham: and the cloths of variegated stuff, and the holy garments, for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, for ministering as priests;
text.DRB: The holy vestments in the ministry for Aaron the priest, and for his sons, that they may execute their office about the sacred things:
