text.NRSV: and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense for the holy place. They shall do just as I have commanded you.
text.Rotherham: and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense for the holy place, according to all which I have commanded thee, shall they do.
text.DRB: The oil of unction, and the incense of spices in the sanctuary, all things which I have commanded thee, shall they make.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses saying—
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: You yourself are to speak to the Israelites: “You shall keep my sabbaths, for this is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, given in order that you may know that I, the Lord, sanctify you.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore, speak unto the sons of Israel, saying, Surely, my sabbaths, shall ye keep, for, a sign it is betwixt me and you to your generations, that ye may know that I Yahweh do hallow you.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: See that thou keep my sabbath: because it is a sign between me and you in your generations: that you may know that I am the Lord, who sanctify you.

text.NRSV: You shall keep the sabbath, because it is holy for you; everyone who profanes it shall be put to death; whoever does any work on it shall be cut off from among the people.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye keep the sabbath, for holy, it is unto you, he that profaneth it shall be surely put to death; verily whosoever doeth therein any work that soul shall be cut off from the midst of its people:
text.DRB: Keep you my sabbath: for it is holy unto you: he that shall profane it, shall be put to death: he that shall do any work in it, his soul shall perish out of the midst of his people.

text.NRSV: Six days shall work be done, but the seventh day is a sabbath of solemn rest, holy to the Lord; whoever does any work on the sabbath day shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall work be done, but on the seventh day, is a holy sabbath-keeping of rest unto Yahweh,—whosoever doeth work on the sabbath day, shall be, surely put to death.
text.DRB: Six days shall you do work: in the seventh day is the sabbath, the rest holy to the Lord. Every one that shall do any work on this day, shall die.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Israelites shall keep the sabbath, observing the sabbath throughout their generations, as a perpetual covenant.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the sons of Israel keep the sabbath,—by making it a Day of Rest to your generations, as an age-abiding covenant:
text.DRB: Let the children of Israel keep the sabbath, and celebrate it in their generations. It is an everlasting covenant

text.NRSV: It is a sign forever between me and the people of Israel that in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.”
text.Rotherham: between me and the sons of Israel, a sign it is unto times age-abiding,—for in six days, did Yahweh make the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day, he rested, and was refreshed.
text.DRB: Between me and the children of Israel, and a perpetual sign. For in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and in the seventh he ceased from work.

text.NRSV: When God finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of the covenant, tablets of stone, written with the finger of God.
text.Rotherham: Then gave he unto Moses, as he finished speaking with him in Mount Sinai, the two tables of the testimony,—tables of stone, written with the finger of God,
text.DRB: And the Lord, when he had ended these words in mount Sinai, gave to Moses two stone tables of testimony, written with the finger of God.

text.NRSV: When the people saw that Moses delayed to come down from the mountain, the people gathered around Aaron, and said to him, “Come, make gods for us, who shall go before us; as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.”
text.Rotherham: And, when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down out of the mount, the people gathered themselves together unto Aaron, and said unto him—Up, make for us gods, who shall go before us, for, as for this Moses—the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we know not what hath befallen him.
text.DRB: And the people seeing that Moses delayed to come down from the mount, gathering together against Aaron, said: Arise, make us gods, that may go before us: for as to this Moses, the man that brought us out of the land of Egypt, we know not what has befallen him.

text.NRSV: Aaron said to them, “Take off the gold rings that are on the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me.”
text.Rotherham: And Aaron said unto them, Break off the rings of gold, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons and of your daughters,—and bring them unto me.
text.DRB: And Aaron said to them: Take the golden earrings from the ears of your wives, and your sons and daughters, and bring them to me.

text.NRSV: So all the people took off the gold rings from their ears, and brought them to Aaron.
text.Rotherham: And all the people of themselves brake off the rings of gold which were in their ears, and brought them unto Aaron.
text.DRB: And the people did what he had commanded, bringing the earrings to Aaron.

text.NRSV: He took the gold from them, formed it in a mold, and cast an image of a calf; and they said, “These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt!”
text.Rotherham: And he received [the gold] at their hand, and fashioned it with a graving tool, and made it a molten calf, and they said—These, are thy gods, O Israel, who brought thee up, out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And when he had received them, he fashioned them by founders' work, and made of them a molten calf. And they said: These are thy gods, O Israel, that have brought thee out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: When Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it; and Aaron made proclamation and said, “Tomorrow shall be a festival to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And when Aaron saw it, he built an altar before it. And Aaron made proclamation, and said, A festival to Yahweh, to-morrow!
text.DRB: And when Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it, and made proclamation by a crier's voice, saying: Tomorrow is the solemnity of the Lord.

text.NRSV: They rose early the next day, and offered burnt offerings and brought sacrifices of well-being; and the people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to revel.
text.Rotherham: So they rose up early, on the morrow, and offered ascending-sacrifices, and brought near peace-offerings, and the people sat down, to eat and to drink, and then rose up—to make sport!
text.DRB: And rising in the morning, they offered holocausts, and peace victims, and the people sat down to eat, and drink, and they rose up to play.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Go down at once! Your people, whom you brought up out of the land of Egypt, have acted perversely;
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses—Go get thee down, for thy people whom thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt have corrupted themselves.
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying: Go, get thee down: thy people, which thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, hath sinned.

text.NRSV: they have been quick to turn aside from the way that I commanded them; they have cast for themselves an image of a calf, and have worshiped it and sacrificed to it, and said, ‘These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt!’”
text.Rotherham: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them, they have made for themselves—a molten calf, and have bowed themselves down thereto and have sacrificed thereto, and have said. These, are thy gods, O Israel, who have brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: They have quickly strayed from the way which thou didst shew them: and they have made to themselves a molten calf, and have adored it, and sacrificing victims to it, have said: These are thy gods, O Israel, that have brought thee out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “I have seen this people, how stiff-necked they are.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh said unto Moses,—I have looked on this people, and lo! a stiff-necked people, it is.
text.DRB: And again the Lord said to Moses: See that this people is stiffnecked:

text.NRSV: Now let me alone, so that my wrath may burn hot against them and I may consume them; and of you I will make a great nation.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let me alone, that mine anger may kindle upon them and that I may consume them, and may make of thee a great nation.
text.DRB: Let me alone, that my wrath may be kindled against them, and that I may destroy them, and I will make of thee a great nation.

text.NRSV: But Moses implored the Lord his God, and said, “O Lord, why does your wrath burn hot against your people, whom you brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a mighty hand?
text.Rotherham: And Moses besought the face of Yahweh his God,—and said—Wherefore, O Yahweh, should thine anger kindle upon thy people, whom thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great might and with a firm hand?
text.DRB: But Moses besought the Lord his God, saying: Why, O Lord, is thy indignation kindled against thy people, whom thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, with great power, and with a mighty hand?

text.NRSV: Why should the Egyptians say, ‘It was with evil intent that he brought them out to kill them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth’? Turn from your fierce wrath; change your mind and do not bring disaster on your people.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore should the Egyptians speak saying—For mischief, hath he taken them forth to slay them among the mountains, and to make an end of them from off the face of the ground? Turn thou from the kindling of thine anger, and be grieved over the calamity to thy people,
text.DRB: Let not the Egyptians say, I beseech thee: He craftily brought them out, that he might kill them in the mountains, and destroy them from the earth: let thy anger cease, and be appeased upon the wickedness of thy people.

text.NRSV: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, your servants, how you swore to them by your own self, saying to them, ‘I will multiply your descendants like the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have promised I will give to your descendants, and they shall inherit it forever.’”
text.Rotherham: Have remembrance of Abraham, of Isaac and of Israel thy servants to whom thou didst swear by thyself, and didst speak unto them saying, I will multiply your seed, as the stars of the heavens,—and, all this land of which I have spoken, will I give to your seed, and they shall inherit it to times age-abiding.
text.DRB: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou sworest by thy own self, saying: I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven: and this whole land that I have spoken of, I will give to your seed, and you shall possess it for ever.

text.NRSV: And the Lord changed his mind about the disaster that he planned to bring on his people.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh was grieved,—over the calamity—which he had spoken of inflicting on his people.
text.DRB: And the Lord was appeased from doing the evil which he had spoken against his people.

text.NRSV: Then Moses turned and went down from the mountain, carrying the two tablets of the covenant in his hands, tablets that were written on both sides, written on the front and on the back.
text.Rotherham: And Moses turned and went down out of the mount, with the two tables of testimony in his hand,—tables written upon on both sides of them, on this and on that, were they written.
text.DRB: And Moses returned from the mount, carrying the two tables of the testimony in his hand, written on both sides,

text.NRSV: The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved upon the tablets.
text.Rotherham: Now as for, the tables, the work of God, they were,—and, as for the writing, the writing of God, it was, graven upon the tables.
text.DRB: And made by the work of God: the writing also of God was graven in the tables.

text.NRSV: When Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said to Moses, “There is a noise of war in the camp.”
text.Rotherham: And Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted,—so he said unto Moses—The noise of war, in the camp!
text.DRB: And Josue hearing the noise of the people shouting, said to Moses: The noise of battle is heard in the camp.

text.NRSV: But he said, “It is not the sound made by victors, or the sound made by losers; it is the sound of revelers that I hear.”
text.Rotherham: But he said—Not the noise of the cry of heroism, nor yet the noise of the cry of defeat,—the noise of alternate song, do I hear.
text.DRB: But he answered: It is not the cry of men encouraging to fight, nor the shout of men compelling to flee: but I hear the voice of singers.

text.NRSV: As soon as he came near the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, Moses’ anger burned hot, and he threw the tablets from his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when he drew near unto the camp, and saw the calf, and the dancings, that the anger of Moses kindled, and he cast out of his hands the tables, and brake them in pieces at the foot of the mountain.
text.DRB: And when he came nigh to the camp, he saw the calf, and the dances: and being very angry, he threw the tables out of his hand, and broke them at the foot of the mount:

text.NRSV: He took the calf that they had made, burned it with fire, ground it to powder, scattered it on the water, and made the Israelites drink it.
text.Rotherham: Then took he the calf which they had made and burned it in the fire, and ground it till it became powder, and strewed it on the face of the waters, and made the sons of Israel drink.
text.DRB: And laying hold of the calf which they had made, he burnt it, and beat it to powder, which he strowed into water, and gave thereof to the children of Israel to drink.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Aaron, “What did this people do to you that you have brought so great a sin upon them?”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Aaron, What had this people done to thee,—that thou shouldst have brought upon them a great sin?
text.DRB: And he said to Aaron: What has this people done to thee, that thou shouldst bring upon them a most heinous sin?

text.NRSV: And Aaron said, “Do not let the anger of my lord burn hot; you know the people, that they are bent on evil.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord kindle,—thou thyself, knowest the people, that ready for mischief, they are.
text.DRB: And he answered him: Let not my lord be offended: for thou knowest this people, that they are prone to evil.

text.NRSV: They said to me, ‘Make gods for us, who shall go before us; as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.’
text.Rotherham: So they said to me—Make for us gods, who shall go before us,—for, as for this, Moses—the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we know not what hath befallen him.
text.DRB: They said to me: Make us gods, that may go before us: for as to this Moses, who brought us forth out of the land of Egypt, we know not what is befallen him.

text.NRSV: So I said to them, ‘Whoever has gold, take it off’; so they gave it to me, and I threw it into the fire, and out came this calf!”
text.Rotherham: And I said to them—Whosoever hath gold let them break it off, So they gave it to me,—and I cast it into the fire, and there came out—this calf.
text.DRB: And I said to them: Which of you hath any gold? and they took and brought it to me: and I cast it into the fire, and this calf came out.

text.NRSV: When Moses saw that the people were running wild (for Aaron had let them run wild, to the derision of their enemies),
text.Rotherham: And Moses saw the people, that unbridled, they were,—for Aaron had given them the rein, for a whispering, among their enemies.
text.DRB: And when Moses saw that the people were naked , (for Aaron had stripped them by occasion of the shame of the filth , and had set them naked among their enemies,)

text.NRSV: then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, “Who is on the Lord’s side? Come to me!” And all the sons of Levi gathered together to him.
text.Rotherham: So Moses took his stand in the gate of the camp, and said—Who is for Yahweh? …To me! Then gathered unto him all the sons of Levi,
text.DRB: Then standing in the gate of the camp, he said: If any man be on the Lord's side let him join with me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him:

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, ‘Put your sword on your side, each of you! Go back and forth from gate to gate throughout the camp, and each of you kill your brother, your friend, and your neighbor.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said to them—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, Put ye every man his sword upon his thigh,—pass through and return from gate to gate in the camp, and slay ye every man his brother and every man his friend and every man his neighbour.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Thus saith the Lord God of Israel: Put every man his sword upon his thigh: go, and return from gate to gate through the midst of the camp, and let every man kill his brother, and friend, and neighbour.

text.NRSV: The sons of Levi did as Moses commanded, and about three thousand of the people fell on that day.
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Levi did, according to the word of Moses,—and there fell from among the people on that day, about three thousand men.
text.DRB: And the sons of Levi did according to the words of Moses, and there were slain that day about three and twenty thousand men.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “Today you have ordained yourselves for the service of the Lord, each one at the cost of a son or a brother, and so have brought a blessing on yourselves this day.
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—Install yourselves to-day, unto Yahweh, yea every man for his son and for his brother,—so will he bestow upon you to-day, a blessing.
text.DRB: And Moses said: You have consecrated your hands this day to the Lord, every man in his son and in his brother, that a blessing may be given to you.

text.NRSV: On the next day Moses said to the people, “You have sinned a great sin. But now I will go up to the Lord; perhaps I can make atonement for your sin.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the morrow, that Moses said unto the people, Ye, have sinned a great sin,—Now, therefore, I must go up unto Yahweh, Peradventure, I may make a propitiatory-covering for your sin.
text.DRB: And when the next day was come, Moses spoke to the people: You have sinned a very great sin: I will go up to the Lord, if by any means I may be able to entreat him for your crime.

text.NRSV: So Moses returned to the Lord and said, “Alas, this people has sinned a great sin; they have made for themselves gods of gold.
text.Rotherham: So Moses returned unto Yahweh, and said,—Oh now, this people hath sinned a great sin, and they have made for themselves—gods of gold.
text.DRB: And returning to the Lord, he said: I beseech thee: this people hath sinned a heinous sin, and they have made to themselves gods of gold: either forgive them this trespass,

text.NRSV: But now, if you will only forgive their sin—but if not, blot me out of the book that you have written.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, if thou wilt forgive their sin, …but if not, blot me I pray thee out of thy book, which thou hast written,
text.DRB: Or if thou do not, strike me out of the book that thou hast written.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Moses, “Whoever has sinned against me I will blot out of my book.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses,—Him who hath sinned against me, must I blot out of my book.
text.DRB: And the Lord answered him: He that hath sinned against me, him will I strike out of my book:

text.NRSV: But now go, lead the people to the place about which I have spoken to you; see, my angel shall go in front of you. Nevertheless, when the day comes for punishment, I will punish them for their sin.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, go lead the people to the place of which I spake to thee, lo! my messenger, shall go before thee,—But, in the day when I do visit, then will I visit upon them their sin.
text.DRB: But go thou, and lead this people whither I have told thee: my angel shall go before thee. And I in the day of revenge will visit this sin also of theirs.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord sent a plague on the people, because they made the calf—the one that Aaron made.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh plagued the people,—for what they had done with the calf, which Aaron made.
text.DRB: The Lord therefore struck the people for the guilt on occasion of the calf which Aaron had made.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Go, leave this place, you and the people whom you have brought up out of the land of Egypt, and go to the land of which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying, ‘To your descendants I will give it.’
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses—Get thee away, go up from hence, thou, and the people whom thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt,—into the land of which I sware to Abraham to Isaac and to Jacob, saying, To thy seed, will I give it;
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying: Go, get thee up from this place, thou and thy people which thou has brought out of the land of Egypt, into the land concerning which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying: To thy seed I will give it.

text.NRSV: I will send an angel before you, and I will drive out the Canaanites, the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
text.Rotherham: And I will send before thee a messenger,-and will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite;
text.DRB: And I will send an angel before thee, that I may cast out the Chanaanite, and the Amorrhite, and the Hethite, and the Pherezite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey; but I will not go up among you, or I would consume you on the way, for you are a stiff-necked people.”
text.Rotherham: into a land flowing with milk and honey,—for I will not go up in thy midst in that a stiff-necked people, thou art, lest I consume thee in the way.
text.DRB: That thou mayst enter into the land that floweth with milk and honey. For I will not go up with thee, because thou art a stiffnecked people: lest I destroy thee in the way.

text.NRSV: When the people heard these harsh words, they mourned, and no one put on ornaments.
text.Rotherham: And when the people heard these evil tidings, they afflicted themselves,—and they put not, any man, his ornaments upon him.
text.DRB: And the people hearing these very bad tidings, mourned: and no man put on his ornaments according to custom.

text.NRSV: For the Lord had said to Moses, “Say to the Israelites, ‘You are a stiff-necked people; if for a single moment I should go up among you, I would consume you. So now take off your ornaments, and I will decide what to do to you.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Say unto the sons of Israel, Ye, are a stiff-necked people, in one moment—if I should go up in thy midst, should I make an end of thee,—Now, therefore lay down thine ornaments from off thee, that I may see what I must do with thee.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Say to the children of Israel: Thou are a stiffnecked people; once I shall come up in the midst of thee, and shall destroy thee. Now presently lay aside thy ornaments, that I may know what to do with thee.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Israelites stripped themselves of their ornaments, from Mount Horeb onward.
text.Rotherham: So then the sons of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments, [and moved on] from Mount Horeb.
text.DRB: So the children of Israel laid aside their ornaments by mount Horeb.

text.NRSV: Now Moses used to take the tent and pitch it outside the camp, far off from the camp; he called it the tent of meeting. And everyone who sought the Lord would go out to the tent of meeting, which was outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: And, Moses, proceeded to take a tent, and pitch it by itself outside the camp, afar off from the camp, and he called it, The Tent of Meeting,—and so it came to pass that whosoever was seeking Yahweh went out unto the tent of meeting, which was on the outside of the camp.
text.DRB: Moses also taking the tabernacle, pitched it without the camp afar off, and called the name thereof, The tabernacle of the covenant. And all the people that had any question, went forth to the tabernacle of the covenant, without the camp.

text.NRSV: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would rise and stand, each at the entrance of a tent, and watch Moses until he had gone into the tent.
text.Rotherham: And it came about, that, when Moses went out unto the tent, all the people used to rise up, and station themselves every man at the opening of his tent,—and they watched Moses, until he entered the tent.
text.DRB: And when Moses went forth to the tabernacle, all the people rose up, and every one stood in the door of his pavilion, and they beheld the back of Moses, till he went into the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: When Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent, and the Lord would speak with Moses.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that when Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud came down, and stood at the opening of the tent,—and spake with Moses.
text.DRB: And when he was gone into the tabernacle of the covenant, the pillar of the cloud came down, and stood at the door, and he spoke with Moses.

text.NRSV: When all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tent, all the people would rise and bow down, all at the entrance of their tent.
text.Rotherham: And when all the people beheld the pillar of cloud, standing at the opening of the tent, then all the people rose up and bowed themselves down, every man at the entrance of his tent.
text.DRB: And all saw that the pillar of the cloud stood at the door of the tabernacle. And they stood, and worshipped at the doors of their tents.

text.NRSV: Thus the Lord used to speak to Moses face to face, as one speaks to a friend. Then he would return to the camp; but his young assistant, Joshua son of Nun, would not leave the tent.
text.Rotherham: Thus Yahweh used to speak unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend,—and when he returned unto the camp, his attendant Joshua son of Nun, a young man, moved not from the midst of the tent.
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to Moses face to face , as a man is wont to speak to his friend. And when he returned into the camp, his servant Josue the son of Nun, a young man, departed not from the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: Moses said to the Lord, “See, you have said to me, ‘Bring up this people’; but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found favor in my sight.’
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses, unto Yahweh, See! thou art saying unto me—Take up this people, and yet thou hast no let me know whom thou wilt send with me,—though thou thyself, didst say—I acknowledge thee by name, Yea, moreover, thou hast found favour in mine eyes.
text.DRB: And Moses said to the Lord; Thou commandest me to lead forth this people: and thou dost not let me know whom thou wilt send with me, especially whereas thou hast said: I know thee by name , and thou hast found favour in my sight.

text.NRSV: Now if I have found favor in your sight, show me your ways, so that I may know you and find favor in your sight. Consider too that this nation is your people.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, if, I pray thee, I have found favour in thine eyes, let me know, I beseech thee, thy way, that I may acknowledge thee, to the intent I may find favour in thine eyes,—see thou, therefore, that this nation is thine own people.
text.DRB: If therefore I have found favour in thy sight, shew me thy face, that I may know thee, and may find grace before thy eyes: look upon thy people this nation.

text.NRSV: He said, “My presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.”
text.Rotherham: So then he said,—Mine own presence, shall go on thus will I give thee rest.
text.DRB: And the Lord said: My face shall go before thee, and I will give thee rest.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “If your presence will not go, do not carry us up from here.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, If thy presence is not going on, do not take us up from hence.
text.DRB: And Moses said: If thou thyself dost not go before, bring us not out of this place.

text.NRSV: For how shall it be known that I have found favor in your sight, I and your people, unless you go with us? In this way, we shall be distinct, I and your people, from every people on the face of the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Whereby, indeed, shall it be known then that I have found favour in thine eyes, I, and thy people? Shall it not be, by thy going on with us? So shall we be more distinguished, I, and thy people, than any people that are on the face of the ground.
text.DRB: For how shall we be able to know, I and thy people, that we have found grace in thy sight, unless thou walk with us, that we may be glorified by all people that dwell upon the earth?

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “I will do the very thing that you have asked; for you have found favor in my sight, and I know you by name.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses: Even this thing, which thou hast spoken, will I do—For thou hast found favour in mine eyes, And I have acknowledged thee by name.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: This word also, which thou hast spoken, will I do: for thou hast found grace before me, and thee I have known by name.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “Show me your glory, I pray.”
text.Rotherham: And he said,—Show me, I pray thee, thy glory.
text.DRB: And he said: shew me thy glory.

text.NRSV: And he said, “I will make all my goodness pass before you, and will proclaim before you the name, ‘The Lord’; and I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will show mercy on whom I will show mercy.
text.Rotherham: And he said,—I, will cause all my goodness to pass before thee. And will proclaim myself by the name Yahweh, before thee, And will show favour, unto whom I may show favour, And will have compassion on whom I may have compassion.
text.DRB: He answered: I will shew thee all good, and I will proclaim in the name of the Lord before thee: and I will have mercy on whom I will, and I will be merciful to whom it shall please me.

text.NRSV: But,” he said, “you cannot see my face; for no one shall see me and live.”
text.Rotherham: But he said—Thou canst not see my face, For no son of earth can see me, and live
text.DRB: And again he said: Thou canst not see my face: for man shall not see me and live.

text.NRSV: And the Lord continued, “See, there is a place by me where you shall stand on the rock;
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, said, Lo! a place near me,—So then thou shalt station thyself upon the rock:
text.DRB: And again he said: Behold there is a place with me, and thou shalt stand upon the rock.

text.NRSV: and while my glory passes by I will put you in a cleft of the rock, and I will cover you with my hand until I have passed by;
text.Rotherham: And it shall be while my glory passeth by, I will put thee in the cleft of the rock,—and will cover thee with my hand while I pass by:
text.DRB: And when my glory shall pass, I will set thee in a hole of the rock, and protect thee with my right hand, till I pass:

text.NRSV: then I will take away my hand, and you shall see my back; but my face shall not be seen.”
text.Rotherham: Then will I take away my hand, and thou shalt see behind me, But, my face, must not be seen.
text.DRB: And I will take away my hand, and thou shalt see my back parts : but my face thou canst not see.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Cut two tablets of stone like the former ones, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the former tablets, which you broke.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses—Hew thee two tables of stone, like the first,—and I will write upon the tables the words which were upon the first tables which thou didst break in pieces;
text.DRB: And after this he said: Hew thee two tables of stone like unto the former, and I will write upon them the words which were in the tables, which thou brokest.

text.NRSV: Be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning to Mount Sinai and present yourself there to me, on the top of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: and be thou ready by the morning,—and thou shalt come up. in the morning, into Mount Sinai; then shalt thou station thyself for me there upon the top of the mount;
text.DRB: Be ready in the morning, that thou mayst forthwith go up into mount Sinai, and thou shalt stand with me upon the top of the mount.

text.NRSV: No one shall come up with you, and do not let anyone be seen throughout all the mountain; and do not let flocks or herds graze in front of that mountain.”
text.Rotherham: and, no man, may come up with thee, and let no man so much as be seen in all the mount,—and let not even the flocks or the herds, feed in front of that mountain.
text.DRB: Let no man go up with thee: and let not any man be seen throughout all the mount: neither let the oxen nor the sheep feed over against it.

text.NRSV: So Moses cut two tablets of stone like the former ones; and he rose early in the morning and went up on Mount Sinai, as the Lord had commanded him, and took in his hand the two tablets of stone.
text.Rotherham: So he hewed two tables of stone like the first and Moses rose up early in the morning, and went up into Mount Sinai, as Yahweh had commanded him,—and took in his hand two tables of stone.
text.DRB: Then he cut out two tables of stone, such as had been before: and rising very early he went up into the mount Sinai, as the Lord had commanded him, carrying with him the tables.

text.NRSV: The Lord descended in the cloud and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name, “The Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh descended in the cloud, and took his station with him there,—and proclaimed himself by the name Yahweh.
text.DRB: And when the Lord was come down in a cloud, Moses stood with him, calling upon the name of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord passed before him, and proclaimed, “The Lord, the Lord, a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness,
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh passed before him, and proclaimed,—Yahweh, Yahweh, A God of compassion and favour,—Slow to anger and abundant in lovingkindness and faithfulness:
text.DRB: And when he passed before him, he said: O the Lord, the Lord God, merciful and gracious, patient and of much compassion, and true,

text.NRSV: keeping steadfast love for the thousandth generation, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, yet by no means clearing the guilty, but visiting the iniquity of the parents upon the children and the children’s children, to the third and the fourth generation.”
text.Rotherham: Keeping lovingkindness to a thousand generations, Forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, Though he leave not, utterly unpunished, Visiting the iniquity of fathers, Upon sons, And upon sons’ sons, Unto a third and unto a fourth generation.
text.DRB: Who keepest mercy unto thousands: who takest away iniquity, and wickedness, and sin, and no man of himself is innocent before thee. Who renderest the iniquity of the fathers to the children, and to the grandchildren, unto the third and fourth generation.

text.NRSV: And Moses quickly bowed his head toward the earth, and worshiped.
text.Rotherham: Then Moses made haste,—and bent his head towards the earth and bowed himself down;
text.DRB: And Moses making haste, bowed down prostrate unto the earth, and adoring,

text.NRSV: He said, “If now I have found favor in your sight, O Lord, I pray, let the Lord go with us. Although this is a stiff-necked people, pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: and said—If, I pray thee, I have found favour in thine eyes, O My Lord, I pray thee let My Lord go on in our midst,—although a stiff-necked people, it is, so wilt thou pardon our iniquity and our sin and take us as thine own.
text.DRB: Said: If I have found grace in thy sight: O Lord, I beseech thee, that thou wilt go with us, (for it is a stiffnecked people,) and take away our iniquities and sin, and possess us.

text.NRSV: He said: I hereby make a covenant. Before all your people I will perform marvels, such as have not been performed in all the earth or in any nation; and all the people among whom you live shall see the work of the Lord; for it is an awesome thing that I will do with you.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Lo! I, am about to solemnise a covenant,-in presence of all thy people, will I do marvellous things, which have not been created in any part of the earth nor among any of the nations,—so shall all the people in whose midst thou, art see the work of Yahweh for a fearful thing, it is, which I, am doing with thee.
text.DRB: The Lord answered: I will make a covenant in the sight of all. I will do signs such as were never seen upon the earth, nor in any nation: that this people, in the midst of whom thou art, may see the terrible work of the Lord which I will do.

text.NRSV: Observe what I command you today. See, I will drive out before you the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
text.Rotherham: Take thou heed what, I, am commanding thee this day,—behold me! driving out from before thee—the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
text.DRB: Observe all things which this day I command thee: I myself will drive out before thy face the Amorrhite, and the Chanaanite, and the Hethite, and the Pherezite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: Take care not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land to which you are going, or it will become a snare among you.
text.Rotherham: Take heed to thyself, Lest thou solemnise a covenant with the inhabitant of the land, whereupon, thou, art about to enter,—Lest he become a snare in thy midst;
text.DRB: Beware thou never join in friendship with the inhabitants of that land, which may be thy ruin:

text.NRSV: You shall tear down their altars, break their pillars, and cut down their sacred poles
text.Rotherham: For their altars, shall ye smash, And their pillars, shall ye shiver,—And their sacred-stems, shall ye fell.
text.DRB: But destroy their altars, break their statues, and cut down their groves:

text.NRSV: (for you shall worship no other god, because the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God).
text.Rotherham: For thou shalt not bow thyself down to another GOD,—For as for Yahweh, Jealous, is his name, A jealous GOD, he is:
text.DRB: Adore not any strange god. The Lord his name is Jealous, he is a jealous God.

text.NRSV: You shall not make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, for when they prostitute themselves to their gods and sacrifice to their gods, someone among them will invite you, and you will eat of the sacrifice,
text.Rotherham: Lest thou solemnise a covenant with the inhabitant of the land,—And then as surely as they go unchastely after their gods and sacrifice to their gods, So surely will he invite thee, And thou wilt eat of his sacrifice;
text.DRB: Make no covenant with the men of those countries lest, when they have committed fornication with their gods, and have adored their idols, some one call thee to eat of the things sacrificed.

text.NRSV: and you will take wives from among their daughters for your sons, and their daughters who prostitute themselves to their gods will make your sons also prostitute themselves to their gods.
text.Rotherham: And thou wilt take of his daughters for thy sons,—And his daughters will go unchastely after their gods, And will cause thy sons to go unchastely after their gods.
text.DRB: Neither shalt thou take of their daughters a wife for thy son, lest after they themselves have committed fornication, they make thy sons also to commit fornication with their gods.

text.NRSV: You shall not make cast idols.
text.Rotherham: Molten gods, shalt thou not make to thee.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not make to thyself any molten gods.

text.NRSV: You shall keep the festival of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month of Abib; for in the month of Abib you came out from Egypt.
text.Rotherham: The festival of unleavened cakes, shalt thou keep, seven days, shalt thou eat unleavened cakes, which I commanded thee, at the set time, in the month Abib,—for in the month Abib, camest thou forth out of Egypt.
text.DRB: Thou shalt keep the feast of the unleavened bread. Seven days shalt thou eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee in the time of the month of the new corn: for in the month of the springtime thou camest out from Egypt.

text.NRSV: All that first opens the womb is mine, all your male livestock, the firstborn of cow and sheep.
text.Rotherham: Every one that is the first to be born, is mine,—and every one of thy cattle that is born a male, firstling of oxen or sheep.
text.DRB: All of the male kind, that openeth the womb, shall be mine. Of all beasts, both of oxen and of sheep, it shall be mine.

text.NRSV: The firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, or if you will not redeem it you shall break its neck. All the firstborn of your sons you shall redeem. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: But the firstling of an ass, thou mayst redeem with a lamb, and if thou wilt not redeem it, then shalt thou break its neck. Every firstborn of thy sons, shalt thou redeem, and he must not see my face, with empty hand.
text.DRB: The firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a sheep: but if thou wilt not give a price for it, it shall be slain. The firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem: neither shalt thou appear before me empty.

text.NRSV: Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest; even in plowing time and in harvest time you shall rest.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shalt thou labour, but on the seventh day, shalt thou rest,—in plowing time and in harvest, shalt thou rest.
text.DRB: Six days shalt thou work, the seventh day thou shalt cease to plough, and to reap.

text.NRSV: You shall observe the festival of weeks, the first fruits of wheat harvest, and the festival of ingathering at the turn of the year.
text.Rotherham: And the festival of weeks, shalt thou make to thee, the firstfruits of the wheat harvest,—and the festival of ingathering, at the closing in of the year:
text.DRB: Thou shalt keep the feast of weeks with the firstfruits of the corn of thy wheat harvest, and the feast when the time of the year returneth that all things are laid in.

text.NRSV: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord God, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: three times in the year, shall every one of thy males see the face of the Lord Yahweh the God of Israel.
text.DRB: Three times in a year all thy males shall appear in the sight of the Almighty Lord the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: For I will cast out nations before you, and enlarge your borders; no one shall covet your land when you go up to appear before the Lord your God three times in the year.
text.Rotherham: For I will root out nations from before thee, and will enlarge thy borders,—and no man shall covet thy land, when thou goes up to see the face of Yahweh thy God, three times in the year.
text.DRB: For when I shall have taken away the nations from thy face, and shall have enlarged thy borders, no man shall lie in wait against thy land when thou shalt go up, and appear in the sight of the Lord thy God thrice in a year.

text.NRSV: You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven, and the sacrifice of the festival of the passover shall not be left until the morning.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not slay, along with anything leavened, the blood of my sacrifice,—neither shall remain to the morning, the sacrifice of the festival of the passover.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice upon leaven: neither shall there remain in the morning any thing of the victim of the solemnity of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The best of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring to the house of the Lord your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother’s milk.
text.Rotherham: The beginning of the firstfruits of thy ground, shalt thou bring into the house of Yahweh, thy God, Thou shalt not boil a kid, in the milk of its dam.
text.DRB: The first of the fruits of thy ground thou shalt offer in the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not boil a kid in the milk of his dam.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Write these words; in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, Write for thyself these words, for according to the tenor of these words, have I solemnized with thee, a covenant, and with Israel.
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Moses: Write these words by which I have made a covenant both with thee and with Israel.

text.NRSV: He was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.
text.Rotherham: So then he was there, with Yahweh, forty days and forty nights, bread, did he not eat and, water, did he not drink, and he wrote upon the tables, the words of the covenant, the ten words.
text.DRB: And he was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights: he neither ate bread nor drank water, and he wrote upon the tables the ten words of the covenant.

text.NRSV: Moses came down from Mount Sinai. As he came down from the mountain with the two tablets of the covenant in his hand, Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone because he had been talking with God.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Moses came down out of Mount Sinai, with the two tables of testimony in the hand of Moses, when he came down out of the mount, that, Moses, knew not that the skin of his face shone, through his having spoken with him.
text.DRB: And when Moses came down from the mount Sinai, he held the two tables of the testimony, and he knew not that his face was horned from the conversation of the Lord.

text.NRSV: When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, the skin of his face was shining, and they were afraid to come near him.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron and all the sons of Israel saw Moses, and lo! the skin of his face shone, and they stood in awe of drawing nigh unto him.
text.DRB: And Aaron and the children of Israel seeing the face of Moses horned, were afraid to come near.

text.NRSV: But Moses called to them; and Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him, and Moses spoke with them.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses called unto them, and Aaron and all the princes in the assembly returned unto him,—and Moses spake unto them.
text.DRB: And being called by him, they returned, both Aaron and the rulers of the congregation. And after that he spoke to them.

text.NRSV: Afterward all the Israelites came near, and he gave them in commandment all that the Lord had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.
text.Rotherham: And afterwards, came nigh, all the sons of Israel, and he gave them in commandment all that Yahweh had spoken with him in Mount Sinai.
text.DRB: And all the children of Israel came to him: and he gave them in commandment all that he had heard of the Lord in mount Sinai.

text.NRSV: When Moses had finished speaking with them, he put a veil on his face;
text.Rotherham: And when Moses had made an end of speaking with them, he put over his face a veil:
text.DRB: And having done speaking, he put a veil upon his face.

text.NRSV: but whenever Moses went in before the Lord to speak with him, he would take the veil off, until he came out; and when he came out, and told the Israelites what he had been commanded,
text.Rotherham: but, as often as Moses went in before Yahweh to speak with him, he put aside the veil until he came out,—and, as often as he came out and spake unto the sons of Israel, that which he had been commanded,
text.DRB: But when he went in to the Lord, and spoke with him, he took it away until he came forth, and then he spoke to the children of Israel all things that had been commanded him.

text.NRSV: the Israelites would see the face of Moses, that the skin of his face was shining; and Moses would put the veil on his face again, until he went in to speak with him.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses face shone,—so Moses again put the veil over his face, until he went in to speak with him.
text.DRB: And they saw that the face of Moses when he came out was horned, but he covered his face again, if at any time he spoke to them.

text.NRSV: Moses assembled all the congregation of the Israelites and said to them: These are the things that the Lord has commanded you to do:
text.Rotherham: And Moses called together all the assembly of the sons of Israel and said unto them,—These are the things which Yahweh hath commanded to do them.
text.DRB: And all the multitude of the children of Israel being gathered together, he said to them: These are the things which the Lord hath commanded to be done.

text.NRSV: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day you shall have a holy sabbath of solemn rest to the Lord; whoever does any work on it shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall work be done, but on the seventh day, shall there be to you a holy sabbath-keeping, unto Yahweh, whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death.
text.DRB: Six days you shall do work: the seventh day shall be holy unto you, the sabbath, and the rest of the Lord: he that shall do any work on it, shall be put to death.

text.NRSV: You shall kindle no fire in all your dwellings on the sabbath day.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not kindle a fire, in any of your dwellings,—on the sabbath day.
text.DRB: You shall kindle no fire in any of your habitations on the sabbath day.

text.NRSV: Moses said to all the congregation of the Israelites: This is the thing that the Lord has commanded:
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, saying, This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, saying,
text.DRB: And Moses said to all the assembly of the children of Israel: This is the word the Lord hath commanded, saying:

text.NRSV: Take from among you an offering to the Lord; let whoever is of a generous heart bring the Lord’s offering: gold, silver, and bronze;
text.Rotherham: Take ye from among you, a heave-offering to Yahweh, every one whose heart is willing shall bring it in, even the heave-offering of Yahweh, gold and silver and bronze:
text.DRB: Set aside with you firstfruits to the Lord. Let every one that is willing and hath a ready heart, offer them to the Lord: gold, and silver, and brass,

text.NRSV: blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine linen; goats’ hair,
text.Rotherham: and blue and purple and crimson and fine linen and goat’s-hair;
text.DRB: Violet and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine linen, goats' hair,

text.NRSV: tanned rams’ skins, and fine leather; acacia wood,
text.Rotherham: and rams’ skins dyed red, and badgers’ skins, and acacia wood;
text.DRB: And rams' skins dyed red, and violet coloured skins, setim wood,

text.NRSV: oil for the light, spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense,
text.Rotherham: and oil for giving light, and aromatic spices for the anointing oil, and for fragrant incense;
text.DRB: And oil to maintain lights, and to make ointment, and most sweet incense.

text.NRSV: and onyx stones and gems to be set for the ephod and the breastpiece.
text.Rotherham: and onyx stones and setting stones, for the ephod, and for the breastpiece.
text.DRB: Onyx stones, and precious stones, for the adorning of the ephod and the rational.

text.NRSV: All who are skillful among you shall come and make all that the Lord has commanded: the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And, all the wise-hearted among you, shall come in, that they may make, all that which Yahweh hath commanded:—
text.DRB: Whosoever of you is wise, let him come, and make that which the Lord hath commanded:

text.NRSV: its tent and its covering, its clasps and its frames, its bars, its pillars, and its bases;
text.Rotherham: the habitation, its tent, and its covering,—its clasps its boards, and its bars its pillars, and its sockets:
text.DRB: To wit, the tabernacle and the roof thereof, and the cover, the rings, and the board work with the oars, the pillars, and the sockets:

text.NRSV: the ark with its poles, the mercy seat, and the curtain for the screen;
text.Rotherham: the ark with its staves the propitiatory, and the screening veil;
text.DRB: The ark and the staves, the propitiatory, and the veil that is drawn before it:

text.NRSV: the table with its poles and all its utensils, and the bread of the Presence;
text.Rotherham: the table with its staves and all its utensils, and the Presence-bread:
text.DRB: The table with the bars and the vessels, and the loaves of proposition:

text.NRSV: the lampstand also for the light, with its utensils and its lamps, and the oil for the light;
text.Rotherham: the lampstand for giving light, with its utensils, and its lamps, and the oil for giving light;
text.DRB: The candlestick to bear up the lights, the vessels thereof and the lamps, and the oil for the nourishing of fires:

text.NRSV: and the altar of incense, with its poles, and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense, and the screen for the entrance, the entrance of the tabernacle;
text.Rotherham: and the incense altar, with its staves, and the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense,—and the entrance screen, for the entrance of the habitation;
text.DRB: The altar of incense, and the bars, and the oil of unction and the incense of spices: the hanging at the door of the tabernacle:

text.NRSV: the altar of burnt offering, with its grating of bronze, its poles, and all its utensils, the basin with its stand;
text.Rotherham: the altar for the ascending-sacrifice, and the bronze grating which pertaineth thereto, its staves and all its utensils, the laver and its stand;
text.DRB: The altar of holocaust, and its grate of brass, with the bars and vessels thereof: the laver and its foot:

text.NRSV: the hangings of the court, its pillars and its bases, and the screen for the gate of the court;
text.Rotherham: the hangings for the court, its pillars and its sockets,—and the screen for the gate of the court:
text.DRB: The curtains of the court with the pillars and the sockets, the hanging in the doors of the entry,

text.NRSV: the pegs of the tabernacle and the pegs of the court, and their cords;
text.Rotherham: the pins of the habitation, and the pins of the court, and their cords;
text.DRB: The pins of the tabernacle and of the court with their little cords:

text.NRSV: the finely worked vestments for ministering in the holy place, the holy vestments for the priest Aaron, and the vestments of his sons, for their service as priests.
text.Rotherham: the cloths of variegated stuff for ministering in the sanctuary,—the sacred garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons for ministering as priests.
text.DRB: The vestments that are to be used in the ministry of the sanctuary, the vesture of Aaron the high priest, and of his sons, to do the office of priesthood to me.

text.NRSV: Then all the congregation of the Israelites withdrew from the presence of Moses.
text.Rotherham: So all the assembly of the sons of Israel went forth from the presence of Moses;
text.DRB: And all the multitude of the children of Israel going out from the presence of Moses,

text.NRSV: And they came, everyone whose heart was stirred, and everyone whose spirit was willing, and brought the Lord’s offering to be used for the tent of meeting, and for all its service, and for the sacred vestments.
text.Rotherham: and they brought in—every man whose heart lifted him up,—and, every one whose spirit made him willing, brought in the heave-offering of Yahweh, for the construction of the tent of meeting, and for all its service, and for the holy garments.
text.DRB: Offered firstfruits to the Lord with a most ready and devout mind, to make the work of the tabernacle of the testimony. Whatsoever was necessary to the service, and to the holy vestments,

text.NRSV: So they came, both men and women; all who were of a willing heart brought brooches, earrings, signet rings, and pendants, all sorts of gold objects, everyone bringing an offering of gold to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yea they brought in—the men with the women,—all who were willing-hearted, brought in—bracelets and nose-ornaments and rings, and buckles, all manner of jewels of gold, even every man who waved a wave-offering of gold unto Yahweh.
text.DRB: Both men and women gave bracelets and earrings, rings and tablets: every vessel of gold was set aside to be offered to the Lord.

text.NRSV: And everyone who possessed blue or purple or crimson yarn or fine linen or goats’ hair or tanned rams’ skins or fine leather, brought them.
text.Rotherham: And, every man with whom was found blue and purple and crimson and fine linen and goat’s-hair,—and rams’ skins dyed red and badgers’ skins brought them in.
text.DRB: If any man had violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, fine linen and goats' hair, rams' skins dyed red, and violet coloured skins,

text.NRSV: Everyone who could make an offering of silver or bronze brought it as the Lord’s offering; and everyone who possessed acacia wood of any use in the work, brought it.
text.Rotherham: Every one who would heave a heave-offering of silver or bronze, brought in the heave-offering of Yahweh,—and, every one with whom was found acacia wood for any construction for the service, brought it in.
text.DRB: Metal of silver and brass, they offered it to the Lord, and setim wood for divers uses.

text.NRSV: All the skillful women spun with their hands, and brought what they had spun in blue and purple and crimson yarns and fine linen;
text.Rotherham: And, all the women who were wise-hearted, with their hands, did spin,—and brought in that which they had spun—the blue, and the purple, the crimson, and the fine linen.
text.DRB: The skillful women also gave such things as they had spun, violet, purple, and scarlet, and fine linen,

text.NRSV: all the women whose hearts moved them to use their skill spun the goats’ hair.
text.Rotherham: And, all the women whose hearts lifted them up with wisdom, span the goat’s-hair.
text.DRB: And goats' hair, giving all of their own accord.

text.NRSV: And the leaders brought onyx stones and gems to be set in the ephod and the breastpiece,
text.Rotherham: And the princes, brought in the onyx stones and the setting stones,—for the ephod and for the breastpiece;
text.DRB: But the princes offered onyx stone, and precious stones, for the ephod and the rational,

text.NRSV: and spices and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the fragrant incense.
text.Rotherham: and the aromatic spices, and the oil,—for giving light, and for anointing oil, and for the fragrant incense.
text.DRB: And spices and oil for the lights, and for the preparing of ointment, and to make the incense of most sweet savour.

text.NRSV: All the Israelite men and women whose hearts moved them to bring anything for the work that the Lord had commanded by Moses to be done, brought it as a freewill offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: [In the case of], every male and woman whose heart, made them willing, to bring in for any construction which, Yahweh had commanded them to make, by the hand of Moses, [in their case] did the sons of Israel bring in a free-will offering unto Yahweh.
text.DRB: All both men and women with devout mind offered gifts, that the works might be done which the Lord had commanded by the hand of Moses. All the children of Israel dedicated voluntary offerings to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to the Israelites: See, the Lord has called by name Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto the sons of Israel—See! Yahweh hath called by name,—Bezaleel son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;
text.DRB: And Moses said to the children of Israel: Behold the Lord hath called by name Beseleel the son of Uri the son of Hur of the tribe of Juda.

text.NRSV: he has filled him with divine spirit, with skill, intelligence, and knowledge in every kind of craft,
text.Rotherham: and filled him with the spirit of God,—in, wisdom in understanding and in knowledge, and in all manner of execution;
text.DRB: And hath filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom and understanding and knowledge and all learning.

text.NRSV: to devise artistic designs, to work in gold, silver, and bronze,
text.Rotherham: to devise skilful designs,—to work in gold and in silver and in bronze;
text.DRB: To devise and to work in gold and silver and brass,

text.NRSV: in cutting stones for setting, and in carving wood, in every kind of craft.
text.Rotherham: and in the cutting of stones for setting, and in the cutting of wood,—to work in any manner of skilful workmanship,
text.DRB: And in engraving stones, and in carpenters' work. Whatsoever can be devised artificially,

text.NRSV: And he has inspired him to teach, both him and Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.
text.Rotherham: To teach also, hath he put in his heart, [the heart of] him, and of Oholiab—son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan;
text.DRB: He hath given in his heart: Ooliab also the son of Achisamech of the tribe of Dan:

text.NRSV: He has filled them with skill to do every kind of work done by an artisan or by a designer or by an embroiderer in blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and in fine linen, or by a weaver—by any sort of artisan or skilled designer.
text.Rotherham: hath filled them with wisdom of heart to work all manner of workmanship of cutter, and deviser and embroiderer, in blue and in purple, in crimson and in fine linen and of the weaver,—workers of any manner of workmanship, and devisers of skilful designs.
text.DRB: Both of them hath he instructed with wisdom, to do carpenters' work and tapestry, and embroidery in blue and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine linen, and to weave all things, and to invent all new things.

text.NRSV: Bezalel and Oholiab and every skillful one to whom the Lord has given skill and understanding to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall Bezaleel and Oholiab work, and every man that is wise-hearted, in whom Yahweh hath put wisdom and understanding, to know how to work any manner of workmanship for the holy service,—according to all that Yahweh hath commanded.
text.DRB: Beseleel, therefore, and Ooliab, and every wise man, to whom the Lord gave wisdom and understanding, to know how to work artificially, made the things that are necessary for the uses of the sanctuary, and which the Lord commanded.

text.NRSV: Moses then called Bezalel and Oholiab and every skillful person in whose mind the Lord had put skill, everyone whose heart was stirred to come to do the work;
text.Rotherham: So then Moses called upon Bezaleel, and upon Oholiab, and upon every man that was wise-hearted, in whose heart Yahweh had put wisdom,—every one whose heart lifted him up, to draw near unto the work, to do it.
text.DRB: And when Moses had called them, and every skillful man, to whom the Lord had given wisdom, and such as of their own accord had offered themselves to the making of the work,

text.NRSV: and they received from Moses all the freewill offerings that the Israelites had brought for doing the work on the sanctuary. They still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning,
text.Rotherham: And they took, from the presence of Moses, all the heave-offering which the sons of Israel had brought in for the work of the holy service to do it,—howbeit, they, brought in unto him yet more free-will offerings, morning by morning.
text.DRB: He delivered all the offerings of the children of Israel unto them. And while they were earnest about the work, the people daily in the morning offered their vows.

text.NRSV: so that all the artisans who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came, each from the task being performed,
text.Rotherham: Then came in all the wise men who were doing all the holy work,—man by man from his work which, they, were doing;
text.DRB: Whereupon the workmen being constrained to come,

text.NRSV: and said to Moses, “The people are bringing much more than enough for doing the work that the Lord has commanded us to do.”
text.Rotherham: and spake unto Moses, saying, More than enough, are the people bringing in,—[more] than is needed for the service pertaining to the work; which Yahweh hath commanded to be done.
text.DRB: Said to Moses: The people offereth more than is necessary.

text.NRSV: So Moses gave command, and word was proclaimed throughout the camp: “No man or woman is to make anything else as an offering for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing;
text.Rotherham: So Moses gave commandment and they passed a proclamation throughout the camp, saying, Let, neither man nor woman, make any more material for the holy heave-offering. So the people were restrained from bringing in.
text.DRB: Moses therefore commanded proclamation to be made by the crier's voice: Let neither man nor woman offer any more for the work of the sanctuary. And so they ceased from offering gifts,

text.NRSV: for what they had already brought was more than enough to do all the work.
text.Rotherham: For the material was enough for all the work, to make it,—and to spare.
text.DRB: Because the things that were offered did suffice, and were too much.

text.NRSV: All those with skill among the workers made the tabernacle with ten curtains; they were made of fine twisted linen, and blue, purple, and crimson yarns, with cherubim skillfully worked into them.
text.Rotherham: Then did all the wise-hearted among the workers of the material make the habitation of ten curtains, of fine-twined linen, and blue and purple and crimson, with cherubim the work of the skilful weaver, did he make them.
text.DRB: And all the men that were wise of heart, to accomplish the work of the tabernacle, made ten curtains of twisted fine linen, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, with varied work, and the art of embroidering:

text.NRSV: The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains were of the same size.
text.Rotherham: The length of each curtain, was twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth four cubits, for each curtain,—one measure, had all the curtains.
text.DRB: The length of one curtain was twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth four: all the curtains were of the same size.

text.NRSV: He joined five curtains to one another, and the other five curtains he joined to one another.
text.Rotherham: And he joined the [first] five curtains, one to another,—and the [other] five curtains, joined he one to another,
text.DRB: And he joined five curtains, one to another, and the other five he coupled one to another.

text.NRSV: He made loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain of the first set; likewise he made them on the edge of the outermost curtain of the second set;
text.Rotherham: And he made loops of blue upon the border of the first curtain, at the edge of the set,—thus, made he in the border of the other curtain at the edge of the second set:
text.DRB: He made also loops of violet in the edge of the curtain on both sides, and in the edge of the other curtain in like manner,

text.NRSV: he made fifty loops on the one curtain, and he made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that was in the second set; the loops were opposite one another.
text.Rotherham: Fifty loops, made he in the first curtain, and fifty loops, made he in the edge of the curtain that was in the second set,—the loops being, opposite, one to another.
text.DRB: That the loops might meet one against another, and might be joined each with the other.

text.NRSV: And he made fifty clasps of gold, and joined the curtains one to the other with clasps; so the tabernacle was one whole.
text.Rotherham: And he made fifty clasps of gold,—and joined the curtains one to another with the clasps, and so the habitation became, one.
text.DRB: Whereupon also he cast fifty rings of gold, that might catch the loops of the curtains, and they might be made one tabernacle.

text.NRSV: He also made curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains.
text.Rotherham: And he made curtains of goat’s-hair, for the tent over the habitation, eleven curtains, did he make them:
text.DRB: He made also eleven curtains of goats' hair, to cover the roof of the tabernacle:

text.NRSV: The length of each curtain was thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; the eleven curtains were of the same size.
text.Rotherham: the length of each curtain, was thirty cubits, and, four cubits, was the breath of each curtain,—one measure, had the eleven curtains.
text.DRB: One curtain was thirty cubits long and four cubits broad: all the curtains were of one measure.

text.NRSV: He joined five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.
text.Rotherham: And he joined five of the curtains by themselves,—and the six curtains, by themselves.
text.DRB: Five of which he joined apart, and the other six apart.

text.NRSV: He made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the other connecting curtain.
text.Rotherham: And he made fifty loops upon the border of the curtain that was outmost in the one set and fifty loops, made he upon the border of the curtain of the second set.
text.DRB: And he made fifty loops in the edge of one curtain, and fifty in the edge of another curtain, that they might be joined one to another.

text.NRSV: He made fifty clasps of bronze to join the tent together so that it might be one whole.
text.Rotherham: And he made fifty clasps of bronze,—to join together the tent, that it might become one.
text.DRB: And fifty buckles of brass wherewith the roof might be knit together, that of all the curtains there might be made one covering.

text.NRSV: And he made for the tent a covering of tanned rams’ skins and an outer covering of fine leather.
text.Rotherham: And he made a covering to the tent, of rams’ skins dyed red,—and a covering of badgers’ skins, above.
text.DRB: He made also a cover for the tabernacle of rams' skins dyed red: and another cover over that of violet skins.

text.NRSV: Then he made the upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.
text.Rotherham: And he made the boards for the habitation, of acacia wood, for standing up:
text.DRB: He made also the boards of the tabernacle of setim wood standing.

text.NRSV: Ten cubits was the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the width of each frame.
text.Rotherham: ten cubits, the length of each board, and a cubit and a half the breadth of each board:
text.DRB: The length of one board was ten cubits: and the breadth was one cubit and a half.

text.NRSV: Each frame had two pegs for fitting together; he did this for all the frames of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: two tenons to each board, united one to another,—thus, made he for all the boards of the habitation.
text.DRB: There were two mortises throughout every board, that one might be joined to the other. And in this manner he made for all the boards of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The frames for the tabernacle he made in this way: twenty frames for the south side;
text.Rotherham: And he made the boards for the habitation, twenty boards, for the south side southwards;
text.DRB: Of which twenty were at the south side southward,

text.NRSV: and he made forty bases of silver under the twenty frames, two bases under the first frame for its two pegs, and two bases under the next frame for its two pegs.
text.Rotherham: and, forty sockets of silver, made he, under the twenty boards, two sockets, under the first board, for its two tenons, and, two sockets, under the next board for its two tenons.
text.DRB: With forty sockets of silver, two sockets were put under one board on the two sides of the corners, where the mortises of the sides end in the corners.

text.NRSV: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty frames
text.Rotherham: And for the second side of the habitation to the north quarter, made he twenty boards;
text.DRB: At that side also of the tabernacle, that looketh toward the north, he made twenty boards.

text.NRSV: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under the first frame and two bases under the next frame.
text.Rotherham: and their forty sockets of silver, two sockets, under the first board, and, two sockets, under the next board.
text.DRB: With forty sockets of silver, two sockets for every board.

text.NRSV: For the rear of the tabernacle westward he made six frames.
text.Rotherham: And, for the hinder part of the habitation westward, made he six boards;
text.DRB: But against the west, to wit, at that side of the tabernacle, which looketh to the sea, he made six boards,

text.NRSV: He made two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear.
text.Rotherham: and two boards, made he for the corners of the habitation,—in the hinderpart.
text.DRB: And two others at each corner of the tabernacle behind:

text.NRSV: They were separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring; he made two of them in this way, for the two corners.
text.Rotherham: Thus were they to be double beneath, and at the same time, should they be entire, at the top thereof, into each ring, thus, did he for them both, for the two corners.
text.DRB: Which were also joined from beneath unto the top, and went together into one joint. Thus he did on both sides at the corners:

text.NRSV: There were eight frames with their bases of silver: sixteen bases, under every frame two bases.
text.Rotherham: Thus were there to be eight boards, and, their sockets of silver, were sixteen sockets, two sockets apiece under each board.
text.DRB: So there were in all eight boards and they had sixteen sockets of silver, to wit, two sockets under every board.

text.NRSV: He made bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And he made bars of acacia wood,—five, for the boards of the first side of the habitation,
text.DRB: He made also bars of setim wood, five to hold together the boards of one side of the tabernacle,

text.NRSV: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
text.Rotherham: and five bars, for the boards of the other side of the habitation, and, five bars, for the boards of the habitation at the hinderpart westward.
text.DRB: And five others to join together the boards of the other side: and besides these, five other bars at the west side of the tabernacle towards the sea.

text.NRSV: He made the middle bar to pass through from end to end halfway up the frames.
text.Rotherham: And he made the middle bar,—to run along in the midst of the boards, from end to end.
text.DRB: He made also another bar, that might come by the midst of the boards from corner to corner.

text.NRSV: And he overlaid the frames with gold, and made rings of gold to hold the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
text.Rotherham: And the boards, overlaid he with gold and their rings, made he of gold, as receptacles for the bars, and he overlaid the bars with gold.
text.DRB: And the board works themselves he overlaid with gold, casting for them sockets of silver. And their rings he made of gold, through which the bars might be drawn: and he covered the bars themselves with plates of gold.

text.NRSV: He made the curtain of blue, purple, and crimson yarns and fine twisted linen, with cherubim skillfully worked into it.
text.Rotherham: And he made the veil, of blue and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen,—of the work of a skilful weaver, made he it, with cherubim.
text.DRB: He made also a veil of violet, and purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, varied and distinguished with embroidery:

text.NRSV: For it he made four pillars of acacia, and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold, and he cast for them four bases of silver.
text.Rotherham: And he made for it four pillars of acacia, and overlaid them with gold their hooks of gold, and cast for them, four sockets of silver.
text.DRB: And four pillars of setim wood, which with their heads be overlaid with gold, casting for them sockets of silver.

text.NRSV: He also made a screen for the entrance to the tent, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns and fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework;
text.Rotherham: And he made a screen for the opening of the tent, of blue and purple and crimson and fine twined linen, the work of an embroiderer;
text.DRB: He made also a hanging in the entry of the tabernacle of violet, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, with the work of an embroiderer.

text.NRSV: and its five pillars with their hooks. He overlaid their capitals and their bands with gold, but their five bases were of bronze.
text.Rotherham: and the five pillars thereof and their hooks, and he overlaid their capitals and their cross-rods, with gold, but, their five sockets, were of bronze.
text.DRB: And five pillars with their heads, which he covered with gold, and their sockets he cast of brass.

text.NRSV: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood; it was two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.Rotherham: And Bezaleel made the ark, of acacia wood, two cubits and a half, the length thereof and, a cubit and a half, the breadth thereof, and, a cubit and a half, the height thereof;
text.DRB: And Beseleel made also the ark of setim wood: it was two cubits and a half in length, and a cubit and a half in breadth, and the height was of one cubit and a half: and he overlaid it with the purest gold within and without.

text.NRSV: He overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made a molding of gold around it.
text.Rotherham: and he overlaid it with pure gold within and without,-and made for it a rim of gold, round about;
text.DRB: And he made to it a crown of gold round about,

text.NRSV: He cast for it four rings of gold for its four feet, two rings on its one side and two rings on its other side.
text.Rotherham: and he cast for it four rings of gold, upon the four feet thereof,—even, two rings, on the one side thereof and, two rings, on the other side thereof;
text.DRB: Casting four rings of gold at the four corners thereof: two rings in one side, and two in the other.
