text.NRSV: He made poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold,
text.Rotherham: and he made staves of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold;
text.DRB: And he made bars of setim wood, which he overlaid with gold,

text.NRSV: and put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, to carry the ark.
text.Rotherham: and he brought the staves into the rings, upon the sides of the ark, for lifting the ark.
text.DRB: And he put them into the rings that were at the sides of the ark to carry it.

text.NRSV: He made a mercy seat of pure gold; two cubits and a half was its length, and a cubit and a half its width.
text.Rotherham: And he made a propitiatory of pure gold,—two cubits and a half, the length thereof, and, a cubit and a half, the breadth thereof:
text.DRB: He made also the propitiatory, that is, the oracle, of the purest gold, two cubits and a half in length, and a cubit and a half in breadth.

text.NRSV: He made two cherubim of hammered gold; at the two ends of the mercy seat he made them,
text.Rotherham: and he made two cherubim, of gold, of beaten work, did he make them, out of the two ends of the propitiatory:
text.DRB: Two cherubims also of beaten gold, which he set on the two sides of the propitiatory:

text.NRSV: one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other end; of one piece with the mercy seat he made the cherubim at its two ends.
text.Rotherham: one cherub, out of this end, and, one cherub, out of that end, out of the propitiatory itself, made he the cherubim, out of the two ends thereof:
text.DRB: One cherub in the top of one side, and the other cherub in the top of the other side: two cherubims at the two ends of the propitiatory,

text.NRSV: The cherubim spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings. They faced one another; the faces of the cherubim were turned toward the mercy seat.
text.Rotherham: and the cherubim were spreading out their wings on high making a shelter with their wings over the propitiatory, with, their faces, one towards the other, towards the propitiatory, were the faces of the cherubim,
text.DRB: Spreading their wings, and covering the propitiatory, and looking one towards the other, and towards it.

text.NRSV: He also made the table of acacia wood, two cubits long, one cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.Rotherham: And he made the table of acacia wood, two cubits, the length thereof and, a cubit, the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof,
text.DRB: He made also the table of setim wood, in length two cubits, and in breadth one cubit, and in height it was a cubit and a half.

text.NRSV: He overlaid it with pure gold, and made a molding of gold around it.
text.Rotherham: And he overlaid it with pure gold,—and he made thereto a rim of gold round about:
text.DRB: And he overlaid it with the finest gold, and he made to it a golden ledge round about.

text.NRSV: He made around it a rim a handbreadth wide, and made a molding of gold around the rim.
text.Rotherham: and he mane thereto a border of a handbreadth, round about, and he made a rim of gold to the border thereof round about:
text.DRB: And to the ledge itself he made a polished crown of gold, of four fingers' breadth, and upon the same another golden crown.

text.NRSV: He cast for it four rings of gold, and fastened the rings to the four corners at its four legs.
text.Rotherham: and he cast for it, four rings of gold, and placed the rings upon the four corners, which pertained to the four feet thereof:
text.DRB: And he cast four rings of gold, which he put in the four corners at each foot of the table,

text.NRSV: The rings that held the poles used for carrying the table were close to the rim.
text.Rotherham: near to the border, were the rings, as receptacles for the staves, for lifting the table;
text.DRB: Over against the crown: and he put the bars into them, that the table might be carried.

text.NRSV: He made the poles of acacia wood to carry the table, and overlaid them with gold.
text.Rotherham: and he made the staves of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold,—for lifting the table;
text.DRB: And the bars also themselves he made of setim wood, and overlaid them with gold,

text.NRSV: And he made the utensils that were to be on the table, its plates and dishes for incense, and its bowls and flagons with which to pour drink offerings, of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: and he made the utensils which were to be upon the table, its dishes and its pans and its bowls, and its basins for pouring out therewith, of pure gold.
text.DRB: And the vessels for the divers uses of the table, dishes, bowls, and cups, and censers of pure gold, wherein the libations are to be offered.

text.NRSV: He also made the lampstand of pure gold. The base and the shaft of the lampstand were made of hammered work; its cups, its calyxes, and its petals were of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: And he made the lampstand, of pure gold, of beaten work, made he the lampstand, its base and its shafts, its cups, its apples and its blossoms were, of the same;
text.DRB: He made also the candlestick of beaten work of the finest gold. From the shaft whereof its branches, its cups, and bowls, and lilies came out:

text.NRSV: There were six branches going out of its sides, three branches of the lampstand out of one side of it and three branches of the lampstand out of the other side of it;
text.Rotherham: with, six branches coming out of its sides,—three branches of the lampstand out of its one side and three branches of the lampstand out of its other side:
text.DRB: Six on the two sides: three branches on one side, and three on the other.

text.NRSV: three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on one branch, and three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on the other branch—so for the six branches going out of the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: three cups like almond-flowers in the one branch—apple and blossom, and, three cups like almond-flowers, in the next branch, apple and blossom, so for the six branches coming out of the lampstand;
text.DRB: Three cups in manner of a nut on each branch, and bowls withal and lilies; and three cups of the fashion of a nut in another branch, and bowls withal and lilies. The work of the six branches that went out from the shaft of the candlestick was equal.

text.NRSV: On the lampstand itself were four cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with its calyxes and petals.
text.Rotherham: and in the lampstand itself, four cups, like almond-flowers, its apples and its blossoms:
text.DRB: And in the shaft itself were four cups after the manner of a nut, and bowls withal at every one, and lilies:

text.NRSV: There was a calyx of one piece with it under the first pair of branches, a calyx of one piece with it under the next pair of branches, and a calyx of one piece with it under the last pair of branches.
text.Rotherham: with, an apple, under the two branches of the same, and, an apple, under the next two branches of the same, and, an apple, under the next two branches of the same, for the six branches coming out of the same:
text.DRB: And bowls under two branches in three places, which together make six branches going out from one shaft.

text.NRSV: Their calyxes and their branches were of one piece with it, the whole of it one hammered piece of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: their apples and their brunches were of the same, all of it one beaten work, of pure gold.
text.DRB: So both the bowls, and the branches were of the same, all beaten work of the purest gold.

text.NRSV: He made its seven lamps and its snuffers and its trays of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: And he made the lamps thereof seven,—with its snuffers and its snuff-trays, of pure gold.
text.DRB: He made also the seven lamps with their snuffers, and the vessels where the snuffings were to be put out, of the purest gold.

text.NRSV: He made it and all its utensils of a talent of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: Of a talent of pure gold, made he it,—and all the utensils thereof.
text.DRB: The candlestick with all the vessels thereof weighed a talent of gold.

text.NRSV: He made the altar of incense of acacia wood. It was square; it was a cubit long and a cubit wide, and two cubits high; its horns were of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: And he made the incense altar, of acacia wood,—a cubit, the length thereof, and, a cubit, the breadth thereof, foursquare, and two cubits the height thereof, of the same, were the horns thereof.
text.DRB: He made also the altar of incense of setim wood, being a cubit on every side foursquare, and in height two cubits: from the corners of which went out horns.

text.NRSV: He overlaid it with pure gold, its top, and its sides all around, and its horns; and he made for it a molding of gold all around,
text.Rotherham: And he overlaid it with pure gold—the top thereof and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof,-and he made thereto a rim of gold, round about.
text.DRB: And he overlaid it with the purest gold, with its grate and the sides, and the horns.

text.NRSV: and made two golden rings for it under its molding, on two opposite sides of it, to hold the poles with which to carry it.
text.Rotherham: And two rings of gold, made he thereto—beneath the rim thereof, upon the two corners thereof, upon the two sides thereof,—as receptacles for the staves, to lift it therewith.
text.DRB: And he made to it a crown of gold round about, and two golden rings under the crown at each side, that the bars might be put into them, and the altar be carried.

text.NRSV: And he made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold.
text.Rotherham: And he made the staves of acacia wood,—and overlaid them with gold.
text.DRB: And the bars themselves he made also of setim wood, and overlaid them with plates of gold.

text.NRSV: He made the holy anointing oil also, and the pure fragrant incense, blended as by the perfumer.
text.Rotherham: And he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure fragrant incense,—the work of a perfumer.
text.DRB: He compounded also the oil for the ointment of sanctification, and incense of the purest spices, according to the work of a perfumer.

text.NRSV: He made the altar of burnt offering also of acacia wood; it was five cubits long, and five cubits wide; it was square, and three cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And he made the altar for the ascending-sacrifice of acacia wood,—five cubits, the length thereof and, five cubits, the breadth thereof foursquare, and, three cubits, the height thereof.
text.DRB: He made also the altar of holocaust of setim wood, five cubits square, and three in height:

text.NRSV: He made horns for it on its four corners; its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
text.Rotherham: And he made the horns thereof, upon the four corners thereof, of the same, were the horns thereof,—and he overlaid it with bronze.
text.DRB: The horns whereof went out from the corners, and he overlaid it with plates of brass.

text.NRSV: He made all the utensils of the altar, the pots, the shovels, the basins, the forks, and the firepans: all its utensils he made of bronze.
text.Rotherham: And he made all the utensils of the altar—the pans and the shovels, and the sprinkling bowls, the flesh-hooks and the fire-pans,—all the utensils thereof, made he of bronze.
text.DRB: And for the uses thereof, he prepared divers vessels of brass, cauldrons, tongs, fleshhooks, pothooks, and firepans.

text.NRSV: He made for the altar a grating, a network of bronze, under its ledge, extending halfway down.
text.Rotherham: And he made to the altar a grating, of network of bronze,—under the margin thereof beneath as far as the midst thereof.
text.DRB: And he made the grate thereof of brass, in manner of a net, and under it in the midst of the altar a hearth,

text.NRSV: He cast four rings on the four corners of the bronze grating to hold the poles;
text.Rotherham: And he cast four rings in the four corners to the grating of bronze,—as receptacles for the staves,
text.DRB: Casting four rings at the four ends of the net at the top, to put in bars to carry it.

text.NRSV: he made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with bronze.
text.Rotherham: And he made the staves of acacia wood,—and overlaid them with bronze.
text.DRB: And he made the bars of setim wood, and overlaid them with plates of brass:

text.NRSV: And he put the poles through the rings on the sides of the altar, to carry it with them; he made it hollow with boards.
text.Rotherham: And he brought the staves into the rings, upon the sides of the altar, to lift it therewith,—hollow with boards, did he make it.
text.DRB: And he drew them through the rings that stood out in the sides of the altar. And the altar itself was not solid, but hollow, of boards, and empty within.

text.NRSV: He made the basin of bronze with its stand of bronze, from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: And he made the laver of bronze, and the stand thereof of bronze,—with the mirrors of the female hosts who did service at the opening of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: He made also the laver of brass, with the foot thereof, of the mirrors of the women that watch at the door of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: He made the court; for the south side the hangings of the court were of fine twisted linen, one hundred cubits long;
text.Rotherham: And he made the court,—on the south side southward, the hangings of the court of fine twined linen, a hundred cubits;
text.DRB: He made also the court, in the south side whereof were hangings of fine twisted linen, of a hundred cubits,

text.NRSV: its twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
text.Rotherham: their pillars, twenty, and their sockets twenty, of bronze,—the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods, of silver:
text.DRB: Twenty pillars of brass with their sockets, the heads of the pillars, and the whole graving of the work, of silver.

text.NRSV: For the north side there were hangings one hundred cubits long; its twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
text.Rotherham: and, on the north side, a hundred cubits, their pillars, twenty, and, their sockets twenty, of bronze,—the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods, of silver.
text.DRB: In like manner at the north side the hangings, the pillars, and the sockets and heads of the pillars were of the same measure, and work and metal.

text.NRSV: For the west side there were hangings fifty cubits long, with ten pillars and ten bases; the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
text.Rotherham: And on the west side, hangings, fifty cubits, their pillars, ten, and, their sockets, ten,—and, the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods, of silver.
text.DRB: But on that side that looketh to the west, there were hangings of fifty cubits, ten pillars of brass with their sockets, and the heads of the pillars, and all the graving of the work, of silver.

text.NRSV: And for the front to the east, fifty cubits.
text.Rotherham: And on the east side towards the dawn, fifty cubits:
text.DRB: Moreover towards the east he prepared hangings of fifty cubits:

text.NRSV: The hangings for one side of the gate were fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: hangings, fifteen cubits, for the side-piece,—their pillars, three, and, their sockets three;
text.DRB: Fifteen cubits of which were on one side with three pillars, and their sockets:

text.NRSV: And so for the other side; on this hand and that hand by the gate of the court were hangings of fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: and, for the second sidepiece on this side and on that of the gate of the court, hangings, fifteen cubits,—their pillars three, and their sockets, three.
text.DRB: And on the other side (for between the two he made the entry of the tabernacle) there were hangings equally of fifteen cubits, and three pillars, and as many sockets.

text.NRSV: All the hangings around the court were of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: All the hangings of the court round about were of fine twined linen.
text.DRB: All the hangings of the court were woven with twisted linen.

text.NRSV: The bases for the pillars were of bronze, the hooks of the pillars and their bands of silver; the overlaying of their capitals was also of silver, and all the pillars of the court were banded with silver.
text.Rotherham: And, the sockets for the pillars, were of bronze, the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver,—and the overlaying of their capitals, was silver,—and, they themselves, were filleted with silver, even all the pillars of the court,
text.DRB: The sockets of the pillars were of brass, and their heads with all their gravings of silver: and he overlaid the pillars of the court also with silver.

text.NRSV: The screen for the entrance to the court was made of blue and purple and crimson yarns and fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework; it was twenty cubits long and, like the hangings of the court, five cubits high,
text.Rotherham: And, the screen of the gate of the court was the work of an embroiderer, blue and purple and crimson, and fine-twined linen,—and, twenty cubits, in length, and the height, according to the breadth, of five cubits, to match the curtains of the court;
text.DRB: And he made in the entry thereof an embroidered hanging of violet, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, that was twenty cubits long, and five cubits high according to the measure of all the hangings of the court.

text.NRSV: with four pillars and four bases of bronze, their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their bands of silver.
text.Rotherham: and, their pillars, were four, and, their sockets, four, of bronze,—their hooks, of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their connecting-rods, of silver.
text.DRB: And the pillars in the entry were four with sockets of brass, and their heads and gravings of silver.

text.NRSV: All the pegs for the tabernacle and for the court all around were of bronze.
text.Rotherham: And, all the pins for the habitation, and for the court round about, of bronze.
text.DRB: The pins also of the tabernacle and of the court round about he made of brass.

text.NRSV: These are the records of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the covenant, which were drawn up at the commandment of Moses, the work of the Levites being under the direction of Ithamar son of the priest Aaron.
text.Rotherham: These, are the details of the habitation—the habitation of testimony, which were detailed at the bidding of Moses,—the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.
text.DRB: These are the instruments of the tabernacle of the testimony, which were counted according to the commandment of Moses, in the ceremonies of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest:

text.NRSV: Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the Lord commanded Moses;
text.Rotherham: So then Bezaleel son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that Yahweh had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: Which Beseleel the son of Uri the son of Hur of the tribe of Juda had made as the Lord commanded by Moses,

text.NRSV: and with him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, engraver, designer, and embroiderer in blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and in fine linen.
text.Rotherham: And, with him, was Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an artificer and skilful weaver,—and an embroiderer—in blue, and in purple and in crimson and in fine linen.
text.DRB: Having for his companion Ooliab the son of Achisamech of the tribe of Dan: who also was an excellent artificer in wood, and worker in tapestry and embroidery in violet, purple, scarlet, and fine linen.

text.NRSV: All the gold that was used for the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary, the gold from the offering, was twenty-nine talents and seven hundred thirty shekels, measured by the sanctuary shekel.
text.Rotherham: As for all the gold that was used for, the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary, it came to pass that the gold of the wave-offering was nine-and-twenty talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary.
text.DRB: All the gold that was spent in the work of the sanctuary, and that was offered in gifts was nine and twenty talents, and seven hundred and thirty sicles according to the standard of the sanctuary.

text.NRSV: The silver from those of the congregation who were counted was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels, measured by the sanctuary shekel;
text.Rotherham: And, the silver of them who were numbered of the assembly, was one hundred talents,—and one thousand, seven hundred and seventy-five shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary;
text.DRB: And it was offered by them that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upwards, of six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty men able to bear arms.

text.NRSV: a beka a head (that is, half a shekel, measured by the sanctuary shekel), for everyone who was counted in the census, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty men.
text.Rotherham: a bekah per head, that is a half shekel, by the shekel of the sanctuary, for all that passed over to them who had been numbered, from twenty years old and upwards, for six hundred and three thousand, and five hundred and fifty.
text.DRB: There were moreover a hundred talents of silver, whereof were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and of the entry where the veil hangeth.

text.NRSV: The one hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary, and the bases of the curtain; one hundred bases for the one hundred talents, a talent for a base.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the hundred talents of silver were used for casting the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the veil,—one hundred sockets with the hundred talents a talent for a socket.
text.DRB: A hundred sockets were made of a hundred talents, one talent being reckoned for every socket.

text.NRSV: Of the one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their capitals and made bands for them.
text.Rotherham: And with the thousand, seven hundred and seventy-five, made the hooks for the pillars,—and overlaid their capitals and united them with connecting-rods.
text.DRB: And of the thousand seven hundred and seventy-five he made the heads of the pillars, which also he overlaid with silver.

text.NRSV: The bronze that was contributed was seventy talents, and two thousand four hundred shekels;
text.Rotherham: And the bronze of the wave-offering, was seventy talents,—and two thousand and four hundred shekels.
text.DRB: And there were offered of brass also seventy-two thousand talents, and four hundred sicles besides.

text.NRSV: with it he made the bases for the entrance of the tent of meeting, the bronze altar and the bronze grating for it and all the utensils of the altar,
text.Rotherham: And he made therewith, the sockets for the opening of the tent of meeting, and the altar of bronze and the grating of bronze which pertained thereto,—and all the utensils of the altar,
text.DRB: Of which were cast the sockets in the entry of the tabernacle of the testimony, and the altar of brass with the grate thereof, and all the vessels that belong to the use thereof.

text.NRSV: the bases all around the court, and the bases of the gate of the court, all the pegs of the tabernacle, and all the pegs around the court.
text.Rotherham: and the sockets for the court round about, and the sockets for the gate of the court,—and all the pins for the habitation and all the pins for the court round about.
text.DRB: And the sockets of the court as well round about as in the entry thereof, and the pins of the tabernacle and of the court round about.

text.NRSV: Of the blue, purple, and crimson yarns they made finely worked vestments, for ministering in the holy place; they made the sacred vestments for Aaron; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And of the blue and the purple, and the crimson, made they cloths of variegated stuff for ministering in the sanctuary,—and they made the holy garments which were for Aaron,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And he made, of violet and purple, scarlet and fine linen, the vestments for Aaron to wear when he ministered in the holy places, as the Lord commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He made the ephod of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: So he made the ephod,—of gold, blue, and purple, and crimson, and fine twined linen,
text.DRB: So he made an ephod of gold, violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen.

text.NRSV: Gold leaf was hammered out and cut into threads to work into the blue, purple, and crimson yarns and into the fine twisted linen, in skilled design.
text.Rotherham: And they did beat out the plates of gold and he proceeded to cut them into threads, to work into the midst of the blue and into the midst of the purple and into the midst of the crimson, and into the midst of the fine linen,—with the work of a skilful weaver.
text.DRB: With embroidered work: and he cut thin plates of gold, and drew them small into threads, that they might be twisted with the woof of the aforesaid colours,

text.NRSV: They made for the ephod shoulder-pieces, joined to it at its two edges.
text.Rotherham: Shoulder-pieces, made they thereto, for joining,—at its two edges, was it joined.
text.DRB: And two borders coupled one to the other in the top on either side,

text.NRSV: The decorated band on it was of the same materials and workmanship, of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And as for the curious fastening girdle which was thereon, of the same, it was according to the working thereof, gold blue, and purple, and crimson and fine twined linen,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And a girdle of the same colours, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The onyx stones were prepared, enclosed in settings of gold filigree and engraved like the engravings of a signet, according to the names of the sons of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And they wrought the onyx stones, enclosed in ouches of gold,—graven with the engravings of a seal-ring, after the names of the sons of Israel.
text.DRB: He prepared also two onyx stones, fast set and closed in gold, and graven by the art of a lapidary, with the names of the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: He set them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, to be stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And he put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod, as stones of memorial for the sons of Israel,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And he set them in the sides of the ephod for a memorial of the children of Israel, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He made the breastpiece, in skilled work, like the work of the ephod, of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: And he made the breastpiece the work of a skilful weaver like the work of the ephod,—of gold blue and purple and crimson, and fine twined linen.
text.DRB: He made also a rational with embroidered work, according to the work of the ephod, of gold, violet, purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen,

text.NRSV: It was square; the breastpiece was made double, a span in length and a span in width when doubled.
text.Rotherham: Foursquare, it was doubled, made they the breastpiece,—a span, was the length thereof, and, a span, the breadth thereof, doubled.
text.DRB: Foursquare, double, of the measure of a span.

text.NRSV: They set in it four rows of stones. A row of carnelian, chrysolite, and emerald was the first row;
text.Rotherham: And they set therein four rows of stones,—one row a sardius, a topaz and an emerald, the first row;
text.DRB: And he set four rows of precious stones in it. In the first row was a sardius, a topaz, an emerald.

text.NRSV: and the second row, a turquoise, a sapphire, and a moonstone;
text.Rotherham: and the second row,—a carbuncle a sapphire, and a diamond;
text.DRB: In the second, a carbuncle, a sapphire, and a jasper.

text.NRSV: and the third row, a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.Rotherham: and the third row,—an opal an agate and an amethyst;
text.DRB: In the third, a ligurius, an agate, and an amethyst.

text.NRSV: and the fourth row, a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper; they were enclosed in settings of gold filigree.
text.Rotherham: and the fourth row, a Tarshish stone, a sardonyx, and a jasper,—enclosed in ouches of gold, when they were set
text.DRB: In the fourth, a chrysolite, an onyx, and a beryl, set and enclosed in gold by their rows.

text.NRSV: There were twelve stones with names corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel; they were like signets, each engraved with its name, for the twelve tribes.
text.Rotherham: And as for the stones, after the names of the sons of Israel, they were twelve, after their names,—with the engravings of a seal-ring, each one, after his name, for the twelve tribes,
text.DRB: And the twelve stones were engraved with the names of the twelve tribes of Israel, each one with its several name.

text.NRSV: They made on the breastpiece chains of pure gold, twisted like cords;
text.Rotherham: And they made, upon the breastpiece, chains like cords of wreathen work,—of pure gold.
text.DRB: They made also in the rational little chains linked one to another of the purest gold,

text.NRSV: and they made two settings of gold filigree and two gold rings, and put the two rings on the two edges of the breastpiece;
text.Rotherham: And they made two ouches of gold, and two rings of gold,—and they placed the two rings upon the two ends of the breastpiece;
text.DRB: And two hooks, and as many rings of gold. And they set the rings on either side of the rational,

text.NRSV: and they put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece.
text.Rotherham: and they placed the two wreathen chains of gold upon the two rings,-upon the ends of the breastpiece;
text.DRB: On which rings the two golden chains should hang, which they put into the hooks that stood out in the corners of the ephod.

text.NRSV: Two ends of the two cords they had attached to the two settings of filigree; in this way they attached it in front to the shoulder-pieces of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: and the other two ends of the two wreathen chains, placed they upon the two ouches,—and placed them upon the shoulder-pieces of the ephod in the forefront thereof.
text.DRB: These both before and behind so answered one another, that the ephod and the rational were bound together,

text.NRSV: Then they made two rings of gold, and put them at the two ends of the breastpiece, on its inside edge next to the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And they made two rings of gold, and put on the two ends of the breastpiece, upon the border thereof, which is towards the ephod, inwards.
text.DRB: Being fastened to the girdle and strongly coupled with rings, which a violet fillet joined, lest they should flag loose, and be moved one from the other, as the Lord commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: And they made two rings of gold, and attached them in front to the lower part of the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod, at its joining above the decorated band of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And they made two other rings of gold, and placed them upon the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod below on the front of the face thereof, to match the joining thereof.—above the curious girdle of the ephod,
text.DRB: They made also the tunick of the ephod all of violet,

text.NRSV: They bound the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord, so that it should lie on the decorated band of the ephod, and that the breastpiece should not come loose from the ephod; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And they bound the breastpiece by the rings thereof into the rings of the ephod, with a cord of blue that it might remain upon the curious girdle of the ephod, and the breastpiece not be removed from upon the ephod,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And a hole for the head in the upper part at the middle, and a woven border round about the hole:

text.NRSV: He also made the robe of the ephod woven all of blue yarn;
text.Rotherham: And he made the robe of the ephod, the work of a weaver, wholly of blue;
text.DRB: And beneath at the feet pomegranates of violet, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen:

text.NRSV: and the opening of the robe in the middle of it was like the opening in a coat of mail, with a binding around the opening, so that it might not be torn.
text.Rotherham: with the opening of the robe in the midst thereof, like the opening of a coat of mail,—a border to the opening thereof round about it might not be rent open.
text.DRB: And little bells of the purest gold, which they put between the pomegranates at the bottom of the tunick round about:

text.NRSV: On the lower hem of the robe they made pomegranates of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: And they made upon the skirts of the robe, pomegranates—of blue and purple and crimson,—twined.
text.DRB: To wit, a bell of gold, and a pomegranate, wherewith the high priest went adorned, when he discharged his ministry, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: They also made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates on the lower hem of the robe all around, between the pomegranates;
text.Rotherham: And they made bells of pure gold, and placed the bells in the midst of the pomegranates upon the skirts of the robe, roundabout, in the midst of the pomegranates:
text.DRB: They made also fine linen tunicks with woven work for Aaron and his sons:

text.NRSV: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate all around on the lower hem of the robe for ministering; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, upon the skirts of the robe, round about, to minister in—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And mitres with their little crowns of fine linen:

text.NRSV: They made the coats of fine linen of woven work for Aaron and his sons,
text.Rotherham: And they made the tunics of fine linen the work of a weaver, for Aaron and for his sons:
text.DRB: And linen breeches of fine linen:

text.NRSV: and the turban of fine linen, and the headdresses of fine linen, and the linen undergarments of fine twisted linen,
text.Rotherham: and the turban of fine linen, and the ornamental caps of fine linen,—and the linen drawers, of fine twined linen:
text.DRB: And a girdle of fine twisted linen, violet, purple, and scarlet twice dyed, of embroidery work, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: and the sash of fine twisted linen, and of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, embroidered with needlework; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and the girdle of fine twined linen, and blue and purple and crimson the work of an embroiderer,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.DRB: They made also the plate of sacred veneration of the purest gold, and they wrote on it with the engraving of a lapidary, The Holy of the Lord:

text.NRSV: They made the rosette of the holy diadem of pure gold, and wrote on it an inscription, like the engraving of a signet, “Holy to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And they made the burnished plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and wrote thereupon a writing, with the engravings of a seal-ring Holy unto Yahweh.
text.DRB: And they fastened it to the mitre with a violet fillet, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: They tied to it a blue cord, to fasten it on the turban above; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And they placed there, upon a cord of blue, to fasten it upon the turban up above, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.DRB: So all the work of the tabernacle and of the roof of the testimony was finished: and the children of Israel did all things which the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: In this way all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was finished; the Israelites had done everything just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: Thus was finished all the work of the habitation of the tent of meeting,—thus did the sons of Israel, according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did they.
text.DRB: And they offered the tabernacle and the roof and the whole furniture, the rings, the boards, the bars, the pillars, and their sockets,

text.NRSV: Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses, the tent and all its utensils, its hooks, its frames, its bars, its pillars, and its bases;
text.Rotherham: And they brought in the habitation unto Moses, the tent, and all its furniture,—its clasps, its boards, its bars, and its pillars and its sockets;
text.DRB: The cover of rams' skins dyed red, and the other cover of violet skins,

text.NRSV: the covering of tanned rams’ skins and the covering of fine leather, and the curtain for the screen;
text.Rotherham: and the covering of rams’ skins dyed red, and the covering of badgers’ skins, and the screening veil;
text.DRB: The veil, the ark, the bars, the propitiatory,

text.NRSV: the ark of the covenant with its poles and the mercy seat;
text.Rotherham: the ark of the testimony, with its staves,—and the propitiatory;
text.DRB: The table, with the vessels thereof, and the loaves of proposition:

text.NRSV: the table with all its utensils, and the bread of the Presence;
text.Rotherham: the table all its utensils, and the Presence-bread;
text.DRB: The candlestick, the lamps, and the furniture of them with the oil:

text.NRSV: the pure lampstand with its lamps set on it and all its utensils, and the oil for the light;
text.Rotherham: the pure lampstand the lamps thereof the lamps to be put in order, and all the utensils thereof,—and the oil for giving light;
text.DRB: The altar of gold, and the ointment, and the incense of spices:

text.NRSV: the golden altar, the anointing oil and the fragrant incense, and the screen for the entrance of the tent;
text.Rotherham: and the altar of gold, and the oil of anointing, and the fragrant incense,—and the screen for the opening of the tent;
text.DRB: And the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle:

text.NRSV: the bronze altar, and its grating of bronze, its poles, and all its utensils; the basin with its stand;
text.Rotherham: the altar of bronze and the grating of bronze which pertaineth thereto, the staves thereof, and all the utensils thereof,—the laver, and its stand,
text.DRB: The altar of brass, the grate, the bars, and all the vessels thereof: the laver with the foot thereof: the hangings of the court, and the pillars with their sockets:

text.NRSV: the hangings of the court, its pillars, and its bases, and the screen for the gate of the court, its cords, its pegs—all the utensils for the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of meeting;
text.Rotherham: the hangings of the court its pillars and its sockets, and the screen for the gate of the court, its cords and its pins,—and all the utensils for the service of the habitation for the tent of meeting;
text.DRB: The hanging in the entry of the court, and the little cords, and the pins thereof. Nothing was wanting of the vessels, that were commanded to be made for the ministry of the tabernacle, and for the roof of the covenant.

text.NRSV: the finely worked vestments for ministering in the holy place, the sacred vestments for the priest Aaron, and the vestments of his sons to serve as priests.
text.Rotherham: them cloths of variegated stuff, for ministering in the sanctuary,—the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons for ministering as priests.
text.DRB: The vestments also, which the priests, to wit, Aaron and his sons, used in the sanctuary,

text.NRSV: The Israelites had done all of the work just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did the sons of Israel make all the work.
text.DRB: The children of Israel offered as the Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: When Moses saw that they had done all the work just as the Lord had commanded, he blessed them.
text.Rotherham: And Moses looked upon all the structure and lo! they had made it, as Yahweh had commanded, so, had they made it,—and Moses blessed them.
text.DRB: And when Moses saw all things finished, he blessed them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—

text.NRSV: On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: On the first day of the month, in the first month, shalt thou rear the habitation, of the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: You shall put in it the ark of the covenant, and you shall screen the ark with the curtain.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt put therein, the ark of the testimony,—and shalt screen the ark with the veil;

text.NRSV: You shall bring in the table, and arrange its setting; and you shall bring in the lampstand, and set up its lamps.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt bring in the table, and set in order what is to be arranged thereupon,—and thou shalt bring in the lampstand, and mount the lamps thereof;

text.NRSV: You shall put the golden altar for incense before the ark of the covenant, and set up the screen for the entrance of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place the altar of gold for incense, before the ark of the testimony,—and put up the screen for the opening of the habitation;

text.NRSV: You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place the altar for the ascending-sacrifice,—before the opening of the habitation of the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: and place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place the laver between the tent of meeting, and the altar, and shalt put therein water,

text.NRSV: You shall set up the court all around, and hang up the screen for the gate of the court.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put up the court round about, and hang up the screen of the gate of the court.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it, and consecrate it and all its furniture, so that it shall become holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take the oil of anointing, and anoint the habitation and all that is therein,—and shalt hallow it. and all the furniture thereof so shall it be holy.

text.NRSV: You shall also anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, and consecrate the altar, so that the altar shall be most holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt anoint the altar of ascending sacrifice, and all its utensils,—and hallow the altar, so shall the altar be most holy:

text.NRSV: You shall anoint the basin and its stand, and consecrate it.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt anoint the laver and its stand,—and shalt hallow it.

text.NRSV: Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and shall wash them with water,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt bring near Aaron and his sons, unto the opening of the tent of meeting,—and shalt bathe them in the water;

text.NRSV: and put on Aaron the sacred vestments, and you shall anoint him and consecrate him, so that he may serve me as priest.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt clothe Aaron with the holy garments,—and shalt anoint him and hallow him so shall he minister as priest unto me.

text.NRSV: You shall bring his sons also and put tunics on them,
text.Rotherham: His sons also, shalt thou bring near,—and shalt clothe them with tunics;

text.NRSV: and anoint them, as you anointed their father, that they may serve me as priests: and their anointing shall admit them to a perpetual priesthood throughout all generations to come.
text.Rotherham: and shalt anoint them as thou didst anoint their father, and they shall minister as priests unto me,—so shall their anointing remain to them for an age-abiding priesthood, to their generations.

text.NRSV: Moses did everything just as the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Moses did,—according to all that Yahweh had commanded him, so did he.

text.NRSV: In the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, the tabernacle was set up.
text.Rotherham: Thus came it to pass, in the first month, in the second year, on the first of the month, that the habitation was reared.

text.NRSV: Moses set up the tabernacle; he laid its bases, and set up its frames, and put in its poles, and raised up its pillars;
text.Rotherham: Yea Moses reared the habitation, and placed its sockets, and fixed its boards, and fastened its bars,—and reared its pillars.

text.NRSV: and he spread the tent over the tabernacle, and put the covering of the tent over it; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: Then spread he abroad the tent upon the habitation, and put the covering of the tent upon it, above,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He took the covenant and put it into the ark, and put the poles on the ark, and set the mercy seat above the ark;
text.Rotherham: And he took and placed the testimony within the ark, and put the staves upon the ark,—and placed the propitiatory upon the ark, above;

text.NRSV: and he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the curtain for screening, and screened the ark of the covenant; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and brought in the ark into the habitation, and put up the screening veil, and screened over the ark of the testimony,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He put the table in the tent of meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside the curtain,
text.Rotherham: And he placed the table in the tent of meeting,—on the side of the habitation northward,—outside the veil;

text.NRSV: and set the bread in order on it before the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and set in order thereupon the arrangement of bread before Yahweh,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He put the lampstand in the tent of meeting, opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And he put the lampstand, in the tent of meeting, over against the table,—on the side of the habitation southward;

text.NRSV: and set up the lamps before the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and mounted the lamps before Yahweh,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the curtain,
text.Rotherham: And he put the altar of gold in the tent of meeting,—before the veil;

text.NRSV: and offered fragrant incense on it; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and burned thereupon the fragrant incense,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He also put in place the screen for the entrance of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: And he put up the screen of the entrance pertaining to the habitation;

text.NRSV: He set the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the grain offering as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and, the altar of ascending-sacrifice, put he at the entrance of the habitation of the tent of meeting,—and he caused to ascend thereupon the ascending-sacrifice, and the meal-offering,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it for washing,
text.Rotherham: And he put the laver, between the tent of meeting, and the altar,—and put therein water for bathing;

text.NRSV: with which Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
text.Rotherham: and Moses and Aaron and his sons thenceforth bathed thereat,—their hands and their feet;

text.NRSV: When they went into the tent of meeting, and when they approached the altar, they washed; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: when they were entering into the tent of meeting, and when they were drawing near unto the altar, then used they to bathe,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: He set up the court around the tabernacle and the altar, and put up the screen at the gate of the court. So Moses finished the work.
text.Rotherham: And he reared the court round about to the habitation and to the altar, and put up the screen of the gate of the court,—and Moses finished the work.

text.NRSV: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: Then did the cloud cover the tent of meeting,—and, the glory of Yahweh, filled the habitation;

text.NRSV: Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled upon it, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: and Moses was not able to enter into the tent of meeting, because the cloud had made its habitation thereupon,—and the glory of Yahweh, filled the habitation.

text.NRSV: Whenever the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle, the Israelites would set out on each stage of their journey;
text.Rotherham: And whensoever the cloud ascended from off the habitation, then did the sons of Israel set forward, in all their journeyings;

text.NRSV: but if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not set out until the day that it was taken up.
text.Rotherham: but if the cloud did not ascend, then did they not set forward—until the day when it did ascend.

text.NRSV: For the cloud of the Lord was on the tabernacle by day, and fire was in the cloud by night, before the eyes of all the house of Israel at each stage of their journey.
text.Rotherham: For, the cloud of Yahweh, was upon the habitation by day, and, a fire, came to be by night therein,—in the sight of all the house of Israel in all their journeyings.

24.1: And he said to Moses: Come up to the Lord, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abiu, and seventy of the ancients of Israel, and you shall adore afar off.
24.2: And Moses alone shall come up to the Lord, but they shall not come nigh; neither shall the people come up with him.
24.3: So Moses came and told the people all the words of the Lord, and all the judgments: and all the people answered with one voice: We will do all the words of the Lord, which he hath spoken.
24.4: And Moses wrote all the words of the Lord: and rising in the morning he built an altar at the foot of the mount, and twelve titles according to the twelve tribes of Israel.
24.5: And he sent young men of the children of Israel, and they offered holocausts , and sacrificed pacific victims of calves to the Lord.
24.6: Then Moses took half of the blood, and put it into bowls: and the rest he poured upon the altar.

40.1: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
40.2: The first month, the first day of the month, thou shalt set up the tabernacle of the testimony,
40.3: And shalt put the ark in it, and shalt let down the veil before it:
40.4: And thou shalt bring in the table, and set upon it the things that are commanded according to the rite. The candlestick shall stand with its lamps,
40.5: And the altar of gold whereon the incense is burnt, before the ark of the testimony. Thou shalt put the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle,
40.6: And before it the altar of holocaust:
40.7: The laver between the altar and the tabernacle, and thou shalt fill it with water.
40.8: And thou shalt encompass the court with hangings, and the entry thereof.
40.9: And thou shalt take the oil of unction and anoint the tabernacle with its vessels, that they may be sanctified:
40.10: The altar of holocaust and all its vessels:
40.11: The laver with its foot: thou shalt consecrate all with the oil of unction, that they may be most holy.
40.12: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tabernacle of the testimony, and having washed them with water,
40.13: Thou shalt put on them the holy vestments, that they may minister to me, and that the unction of them may prosper to an everlasting priesthood.
40.14: And Moses did all that the Lord had commanded.
40.15: So in the first month of the second year, the first day of the month, the tabernacle was set up.
40.16: And Moses reared it up, and placed the boards and the sockets and the bars, and set up the pillars,
40.17: And spread the roof over the tabernacle, putting over it a cover, as the Lord had commanded.
40.18: And he put the testimony in the ark, thrusting bars underneath, and the oracle above.
40.19: And when he had brought the ark into the tabernacle, he drew the veil before it to fulfill the commandment of the Lord.
40.20: And he set the table in the tabernacle of the testimony at the north side without the veil,
40.21: Setting there in order the loaves of proposition, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.22: He set the candlestick also in the tabernacle of the testimony over against the table on the south side,
40.23: Placing the lamps in order, according to the precept of the Lord.
40.24: He set also the altar of gold under the roof of the testimony over against the veil,
40.25: And burnt upon it the incense of spices, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.26: And he put also the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle of the testimony,
40.27: And the altar of holocaust of the entry of the testimony, offering the holocaust, and the sacrifices upon it, as the Lord had commanded.
40.28: And he set the laver between the tabernacle of the testimony and the altar, filling it with water.
40.29: And Moses and Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and feet,
40.30: When they went into the tabernacle of the covenant, and went to the altar, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.31: He set up also the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, drawing the hanging in the entry thereof. After all things were perfected,
40.32: The cloud covered the tabernacle of the testimony, and the glory of the Lord filled it.
40.33: Neither could Moses go into the tabernacle of the covenant, the cloud covering all things and the majesty of the Lord shining, for the cloud had covered all.
40.34: If at any time the cloud removed from the tabernacle, the children of Israel went forward by their troops:
40.35: If it hung over, they remained in the same place.
40.36: For the cloud of the Lord hung over the tabernacle by day, and a fire by night, in the sight of all the children of Israel throughout all their mansions.
